## PAGE 04912: Creating an empty Instance Table

- page_id: `243966996`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966996/Creating+an+empty+Instance+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:16.218+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an empty Instance Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.
2. Do one of the following: The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - On the main toolbars, click the Create Diagram button. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > Instance Table .

- If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.
- If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an empty Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>On the main toolbars, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Other </strong><strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance Table</strong>.</li></ul>The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4864ee64-0471-41e8-b42e-dcde36bf0813"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.</li><li>If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table.<br /> <ac:image ac:title="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default" ac:alt="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561545 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04915: Creating and displaying contextual relationships

- page_id: `249561545`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561545/Creating+and+displaying+contextual+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:33.081+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Contextual relationships
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you enable the creation of contextual relationships in the **Project Options** dialog, you can start creating them immediately.

multiple places listYou can create contextual relationships from multiple places in your modeling tool:

- Structure tab ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''])
- Internal Block Diagram andBlock Definition Diagram
- 
- 

##### Creating contextual relationships from all the places listed above

To create contextual relationships from the **Create Relations** dialog

1. Right-click an element, click Create Relation > Outgoing / Incoming , and then select the contextual relationship type to create. The Create New <relation name> (contextual) To / From dialog opens.
2. Do one or more of the following :
  - In the Model Browser, select the element to create a relationship to / from.
  - On the diagram pane , select the element to create a relationship to / from.
  - In the **Create New <relation name> (contextual) To** /**From** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the element to create a relationship to / from.
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships using the Create Relations dialog.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a relationship' space='']>]]>

##### contextual relationships in diagramsCreating and displaying contextual relationships in diagrams

To create contextual relationships from the **diagram palette**or **smart manipulator toolbar**

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram palette, select the relationship type to create.
  - On the diagram pane, select the relationship end and click [ATTACHMENT filename='satisfy_relationship_icon.png'] in the smart manipulator toolbar.
2. Select the appropriate elements on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships from the diagram palette or smart manipulator toolbar.

To contextualize relationships from the **smart manipulator toolbar**

1. In the diagram pane, click on the existing relationship. The smart manipulator toolbar appears.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='contextualize.png'] to contextualize the selected relationship.

You can only contextualize *Allocate*,*Refine*,*Satisfy*,*Trace*,and*Verify*relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Contextualizing relationships using the smart manipulator toolbar.

You can decontextualize the existing contextual relationship by clicking[IMAGE alt='' src=''](Decontextualize) in the smart manipulator toolbar.

For a more compact Internal Block Diagram view, contextual relationships can be represented in the **Element Properties** compartment of the element shape. For this, you need to create the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space=''] first. Note that this expression must be identical to the dependency matrix criteria for displaying contextual relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying Contextual Relationship in the element compartment.

##### contextual relationships in dependency matricesCreating contextual relationships in dependency matrices

Contextual relationships can only be created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='']**Structure** tree-view dependency matrices.

Contextual Relationships in Dependency MatricesTo create contextual relationships in **dependency matrices**

1. In the Criteria area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''] , next to Dependency Criteria , click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_elements_from_the_model.png'] .
2. Click the Simple Navigation operation and scroll down to the Contextual Relations category.
3. Under Relation Criterion , select the contextual relationship type to create.
4. When you are done, click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying Relation Criterion to build a Dependency Matrix.

##### contextual relationships in tables

Contextual relationships can only be created in[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying row elements hierarchically' space='']**Structure**tree-view tables.

Before you can create contextual relationships in your structure tree-view table, you need to add a custom column or create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space=''] to represent them.

To add a custom column/derived property to represent contextual relationships

1. In the diagram toolbar, click**Columns** >**New Custom Column**/**New Derived Property**. Select **New Custom Column** to add a custom column to the specific table only.Select **New Derived Property** to add a column to be able to use in all future tables.
2. In the Expression dialog, click the Simple Navigation operation and scroll down to the Contextual Relations category.
3. Under Relation Criterion , select the contextual relationship ty pe (e.g., contextual Satisfy) to create. [ATTACHMENT filename='collecting_contextual_satisfy.png']
4. When you are finished, click OK .

Once you have added a custom column, you can now create contextual relationships directly in your table.

To create contextual relationships in **structure tree-view tables**

1. In the structure tree-view table, select the appropriat e row and th en click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dots_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='structure_tree_view_table_contextual_relationships.png']
2. In the open Select Elements dialog, select the element(s) to create the contextual relationship(s). [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_requirement_in_select_elements_dialog.png']
3. When you are finished, click OK .

The contextual relationship is created in the model and represented in the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565267 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04916: Creating and displaying derived properties

- page_id: `249565267`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565267/Creating+and+displaying+derived+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:36.651+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['create-derived-properties', 'edit-derived-properties', 'delete-derived-properties', 'display-derived-properties', 'display-information-indirectly-related-to-element-through-derived-properties', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to create derived properties to display the information indirectly related to an element. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

You can , , or derived properties in the:

- Element Specification window
- Table

You can manage derived properties using the **Expression** dialog.

create_newTo create a new derived property

1. Open the Expression dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, select the Traceability property group and click the Create button.
  - In the table toolbar, click Show Columns and select New Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property.
3. Click **OK**.

If you want to create a derived property with a result other than an element, you must manually change the derived property type.

editTo edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element Specification window, select the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, right-click the derived property column heading and select Edit Derived Property .
2. In the Expression dialog make changes and click OK .

removeTo remove a derived property

1. Do one of the following:

- In the selected element Specification window, select the Traceability property group, choose the derived property you want to remove, and click the Delete button.
- In the active table, right-click the derived property column heading and select Remove Derived Property .

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- 
- (as a new column)

display_on_noteTo display derived properties on a Note shape

1. Create a Note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Note and click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator. The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
  - Right-click the Note shape and select Edit Compartment . The Compartment Edit dialog opens. Choose the Element Properties tab.
3. In the**Hidden** list, move the properties you want to display on a Note shape to the **Selected** list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the Note.

display_in_tableTo display derived properties in a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Show Columns button and select Select Columns .
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select the properties you want to display.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

**Case Study**

Let’s say, we need to show *Warning Order* and *Medical Condition* exchange elements produced and consumed by the performer *Search Node*. Exchange elements are information indirectly related to the *Search Node* element.

We will analyze the OV-2 diagram from the DoDAF sample project.

First, we will show the exchange elements produced by the*Search Node*.

Let’s create the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the Operational Performer *Search Node*. Open the *Search Node* Specification window, and in the **Traceability** property group, click the **Create** button. In the **Expression** dialog, select the **Metachain Navigation**property group. Then specify the derived property name and the meta chain expression as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following figure shows that a new subgroup named **Other** is created in the Operational Performer Specification window. Our created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property is placed under this subgroup. The **Produces Exchange Element** derived property has Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements as its values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, we need to display values of the **Produces Exchange Element** derived property for the Operational Performer *Search Node* in the OV-2 diagram.

A derived property is applicable to an element type, not the specific element. In this particular example, our created derived properties are applicable to all Operational Performers, not only to *Search Node*.

We can use a Note element wherein we can display information indirectly related to the Search Node. Let’s create a Note and connect it to the Operational Performer*Search Node*. Then click **Edit Element Properties**. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab. From the properties list, select to display the Produces Exchange Element property. After clicking the **OK** button, Warning Order and Medical Condition exchange elements are displayed in the Note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a generic table wherein we can also display information indirectly related to the *Search Node* as well as to other Operational Performers. For that purpose, we select Operational Performer as an**Element Type** and add existing Operational Performers to the table. Then we select which information to display as new columns. Previously created **Produces Exchange Element** derived property can be added as a new column through the **Show Columns** menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Now, let’s create a new derived property directly in the generic table. This property will show which exchange elements are consumed by the performer element. We can do that using the same **Expression** dialog and doing the same actions as described previously. The following figure is an example of the **Consumes Exchange Element** derived property expression.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the expression is specified, the newly created derived property is displayed as a new column.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to create derived properties to display the information indirectly related to an element. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.</p><p>You can <ac:link ac:anchor="create_new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="edit" />, or <ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[delete]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> derived properties in the:</p><ul><li>Element Specification window</li><li>Table</li></ul><p>You can manage derived properties using the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" column heading and select<strong> Edit Derived Property</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog make changes and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br derived property</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li>In the selected element Specification window, select the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group, choose the derived property you want to remove, and click the <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li><li>In the active table, right-click the derived property column heading and select <strong>Remove Derived Property</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_on_note"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Note]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="display_in_table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (as a new column)</li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05f2a73d-d527-4af5-89d3-c4cccd5e9218"><ac:parameter ac:name="">display_on_note</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display derived properties on a Note shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Note for the selected element or select an existing one.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Note and click the <strong>Edit Element Properties </strong>smart manipulator. Choose the <strong>Element Properties </strong>tab. </li></ul></li><li><p>In the<strong> Hidden</strong> list, move the properties you want to display on a Note shape to the <strong>Selected</strong> list by doing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Select one or more properties and click the <strong>&gt;</strong> or<strong> &gt;&gt;</strong> button.</li><li>Double-click to select properties one by one.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Selected derived properties are displayed in the Note.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="480fa142-1110-4c6d-9d2d-a7db832ca8ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="">display_in_table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display derived properties in a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Show Columns</strong> button and select <strong>Select Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog, select the properties you want to display.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong>Case Study</strong></p><p>Let’s say, we need to show <em>Warning Order</em> and <em>Medical Condition</em> exchange elements produced and consumed by the performer <em>Search Node</em>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561174 space=MT version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 04917: Creating and managing Block properties

- page_id: `249561174`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561174/Creating+and+managing+Block+properties
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:00.302+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096811557

1096811559

1096811556

**On this page**

43

1096811558

When the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] is represented in several [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''], all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] roles are represented in the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 specific compartments' space=''].

You can create and manage the Block properties in several ways:

33

##### Using the smart manipulator

44

###### Creating a property for a Block

You can create and manage properties for a Block directly on the diagram pane by using the symbol [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''].

To create a property for a Block

1. Select the Block shape.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. From the opened menu select the property you want to create for a Block. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Type its name. [ATTACHMENT filename='typing_name_for_part_property.png']
5. Specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_type_for_part_property.png'] The property is created and displayed on the Block shape in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 specific compartments' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='part_property_in_Block_compartments.png']

###### Removing a Block property from the Block shape

To remove a Block property from the Block shape

1. Select the property on Block shape.
2. Press Delete . The property is removed from the Block compartments but not from the model.

###### Deleting a Block property from the model

To delete a Block property from the model

1. Right-click the property on the Block shape.
2. From the shortcut menu select the Select in Containment Tree command. The property is selected in the Containment tree.
3. Do one of the following: If a property is shared with other people, the **Question** dialog opens. You can then confirm or cancel the **Delete** command. The Block property is removed from the model.
  - Press Delete .
  - Right-click and from the shortcut menu and select the Delete command.

##### Using the Specification window

All owned and inherited Block properties are collected on the left of the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] > **Properties**. Block properties are grouped in the Block compartments. Manage them by creating, redefining or deleting directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Property name. |
| Type | Property type. |
| Default Value | Property default value. |
| Owner | Block name that contains the current property. |

****

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the Specification window of the selected property. |
| Create | Opens the list of available properties to create. Click to create the Connector property, Part property, Reference property, Value property, Constraint property, Flow property. |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967169 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04918: Creating and managing Legend Items

- page_id: `243967169`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967169/Creating+and+managing+Legend+Items
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:49.985+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695897

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695899

1293695896

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695898

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''], you must create and specify its Legend Items. Legend Itemsdefine different styles used in a diagram or table in a Legend.

##### Creating Legend Items

You can create Legend Items directly from a Legend shape or in the Specification window of a Legend. If you are working with a Legend in a symbol diagram, you can use both methods. However, if you are working with a Legend in a table, you can create Legend Items only in the Specification window of the Legend.

To create a Legend Item from a Legend shape

1. In a diagram, select the shape of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item.
2. Click the smart manipulator on the top right corner of the Legend shape, highlighted in the figure below, and select **Create Legend Item**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space=''].
4. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend shape and applied to diagram symbols according to the properties you specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']**Applied To** property group.

To create a Legend Item in the Specification window of a Legend

1. Open the Specification window of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item by doing one of the following:
  - In a symbol diagram, double-click the shape of the desired Legend.
  - In a table, hover the mouse pointer over the desired Legend, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and select Specification .
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the open Specification window of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space=''].
5. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend and applied to diagram symbols or table rows according to the properties you specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']**Applied To** property group.

Alternatively, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space=''].

##### Managing Legend Items

The **Legend Items** group in the Specification window of a Legend allows you to create new Legend Items and modify or reorder the existing ones.

To access the Legend Item management area

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.

The Legend Item management area is displayed on the right side of the Specification window. The following table explains how to use the buttons found there to create and modify Legend Items.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Up | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position up in the list. |
| Down | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position down in the list. |
| Create | Click the button to create a Legend Item. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item opens automatically, allowing you to specify its properties. |
| Clone | Select a Legend Item and click the button to create its copy. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item clone opens automatically, allowing you to modify its properties. By default, the clone inherits all property values of the Legend Item from which it was cloned. |
| Delete | Select a Legend Item and click the button to remove it. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Legend Item management area in the Specification window of a Legend.

#### NOTE: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

Alternative method to manage Legend Items

You can also manage Legend Items in the **Legend Items** property specification cell:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the specification cell of the Legend Items property.
3. Complete one of the following actions:
  - To create a Legend Item, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] .
  - To remove a Legend Item, select the Legend Item you want to remove and click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] .
  - To reorder Legend Items, click [ATTACHMENT filename='order.png'] and in the open dialog use the Up and Down buttons to change the position of the selected Legend Item.

580309733

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567006 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 04919: Creating and Using a BPMN Event

- page_id: `249567006`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567006/Creating+and+Using+a+BPMN+Event
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:30:58.663+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section will describe how to create and specify Events in BPMN diagrams.

To create a BPMNEvent

- On the Process diagram palette, click a desired event button and then select a type of BPMNEvent.

To create a Boundary Event

- On the Process diagram pallete, click the Boundary Event (Message) button and then select a type of Boundary Event.
- Click an Activity or Choreography Activity in the diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_boundary_event.png']

To change a BPMNEvent type

You can change an Event type for Start, Intermediate, Boundary, or End Events.

- Right-click a created BPMNEvent and select a new BPMNEvent type.

To change a Start Event to non-interrupting, do one of the following

Not all Start Event types can be non-interrupting. The command *Is Interrupting*is only available for the following Start Event types:

- Message Start Event
- Timer Start Event
- Escalation Start Event
- Conditional Start Event
- Signal Start Event
- Multiple Start Event
- Parallel Multiple Start Event

- Right-click the Start Event and on the shortcut menu click Is Interrupting > False .
- Open the Start Event Specification window. It the Is Interrupting property box, select False and click Close .

To change a Boundary Event to non-interrupting, do one of the following

Not all Boundary Event types can be non-interrupting. The command *Cancel Activity* is only available for the following Boundary Event types:

- Message Boundary Event
- Timer Boundary Event
- Escalation Boundary Event
- Conditional Boundary Event
- Signal Boundary Event
- Multiple Boundary Event
- Parallel Multiple Boundary Event

- Right-click the Boundary Event and on the shortcut menu click Cancel Activity > False .
- Open the Boundary Event Specification window. In the Cancel Activity property box, select False , and then click Close .

**Related elements**

- Start Events
- Intermediate Catch Event
- Intermediate Throwing Event
- Boundary Events
- End Events
- Activities
- Choreography Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

- Creating and Using SubProcesses
- Using Activities

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567009 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04920: Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

- page_id: `249567009`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567009/Creating+and+Using+a+Sequence+Flow
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:33:26.513+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Sequence Flow connects Activities, Choreography Activities, Events, and Gateways. A Conditional Sequence Flow has a condition expression and is drawn with a mini-diamond marker at the beginning of the Sequence Flow A default Sequence Flow is indicated with a backslash at the beginning of the Sequence Flow.

To create a Sequence Flow, do one of the following

- Click an Activity on the diagram. On the Smart Manipulators toolbar click the Sequence Flow button.
- Click the Sequence Flow button on the diagram pallet and connect appropriate shapes on the diagram pane.

To add a condition to Sequence Flow, do one of the following

- Select the created Sequence Flow, open its Specification window and enter the Condition Expression property value.
- Select the created Sequence Flow and type the condition between the brackets. [ATTACHMENT filename='sequence_flow_condition_expression.png']

A Conditional Sequence Flow outgoing from a Gateway is displayed without a mini diamond marker.

To set a default Sequence Flow

A default Sequence Flow can be specified for Activities (Tasks, Subprocesses, and Call Activities) or exclusive, inclusive, and complex Gateways.

1. Select the Sequence flow on the diagram pane.
2. On the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the Make Default button. [ATTACHMENT filename='default_sequence_flow.png']

A default Sequence flow does not have a condition expression.

To change direction of Sequence Flow

- Right-click the Sequence Flow and from the shortcut menu, select Refactor > Reverse Direction .

**Related element**

- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagrams
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Creating and Using Tasks
- Creating and Using SubProcesses
- Using Activities
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']
- Creating and Using Data Items
- Using Common BPMN Elements
- Navigation Between BPMN Diagrams

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A Conditional Sequence Flow has a condition expression and is drawn with a mini-diamond marker at the beginning of the Sequence Flow A default Sequence Flow is indicated with a backslash at the beginning of the Sequence Flow.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Sequence Flow, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="7c23e9ab-99d6-4a9e-a269-82ab274e0fd4">Click an Activity on the diagram. On the Smart Manipulators toolbar click the Sequence Flow button.</li><li data-uuid="33f819f1-7625-4138-9674-c659300cd754">Click the <strong>Sequence Flow</strong> button on the diagram pallet and connect appropriate shapes on the diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a condition to Sequence Flow, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="e97103cc-c82b-4a12-aea9-4cb4a3af5d8d">Select the created Sequence Flow, open its Specification window and enter the <strong>Condition Expression</strong> property value.</li><li data-uuid="be2812d7-579f-42f2-bfe7-e673e21acd75">Select the created Sequence Flow and type the condition between the brackets.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sequence_flow_condition_expression.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="52172a33-a14d-463d-a9c5-90450b0feecf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Conditional Sequence Flow outgoing from a Gateway is displayed without a mini diamond marker.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To set a default Sequence Flow</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56f66f9-163b-49bc-b90b-82439aa76248"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A default Sequence Flow can be specified for Activities (Tasks, Subprocesses, and Call Activities) or exclusive, inclusive, and complex Gateways.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="7e2b11bc-f71e-48d0-a4c5-9805d2467699">Select the Sequence flow on the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="408462c7-2f43-4848-a371-b966dca585e0">On the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the Make Default button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_sequence_flow.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dab14980-bc5e-4d79-bf2f-215ae73ca5e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A default Sequence flow does not have a condition expression.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To change direction of Sequence Flow</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="1ea9644a-6f8d-4c01-83f3-4290e0090a8d">Right-click the Sequence Flow and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Reverse Direction</strong>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="c6f99cd0-fc42-4800-8982-ae13c88b2b1f"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Sequence+Flow">Sequence Flow</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="47f15dbf-cc17-494c-bb30-9fb2823fc683"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagrams</a></li><li data-uuid="6776469e-4312-423e-95c0-ae3fbb06ddd4"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567033 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 04921: Creating and Using Choreography Task

- page_id: `249567033`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567033/Creating+and+Using+Choreography+Task
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:15:42.472+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Choreography Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Choreography Task can have references to Message Flows, existing among referenced Resources.

To create a Choreography Task

- On the diagram pallet, click Choreography Task .

To select Message Flows referenced by a Choreography Task

1. Open the Choreography Task Specification window.
2. Select Message Flow property value cell and click the ... button. The Select Message Flows dialog opens.
3. Select the Message Flows and click OK .

If the Message Flows, which are referenced by a Choreography Task, have the Messages specified, they will be displayed and attached to the Choreography Task in the diagram.

To specify Messages for a Choreography Task

1. Create a BPMN Collaboration diagram with Pools representing Resources and Message Flows with Messages.
2. On a BPMN Choreography diagram and create Choreography Task.
3. Open the Choreography Task Specification window. Specify the Participants for the Participant Refs property and a Participant for the Initiating Participant Ref property.
4. Select the Message Flow property and click the ... button. The Select Message Flows dialog opens.
5. Select the Message Flows you have created in step 3 as the Message Flow property value and click Close .

- Initiating Messages (connected to an initiating Participant compartment) will be displayed in yellow.
- Non-initiating messages (connected to a non-initiating Participant compartment) will be displayed in gray.

To hide the Messages for a Choreography Task, do one of the following

- Right-click a Choreography Task and select Show Messages .
- Right-click a Choreography Task and click Symbol(s) Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens. Select Show Messages .

**Related elements**

- Choreography Task
- Resource
- Participant
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']
- Collaboration

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Using Choreography Activity
- Creating SubChoreography
- Creating Call Choreography Activity
- Creating and Using Pool and Lanes
- Creating and Using Message Flow
- Using Common BPMN Elements

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567025 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04922: Creating and Using Conversation Nodes

- page_id: `249567025`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567025/Creating+and+Using+Conversation+Nodes
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:21:32.438+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Conversation groups messages exchanged among collaboration participants.

To create a Communication between two Pools

1. Create a Conversation and Pool.
2. Create a Conversation Link from the Conversation to a Pool.
3. Draw another Conversation Link from the Conversation to another Pool.

A Pool requires a specified Representing Resource.

To select Message Flows grouped by a Conversation

1. Do one of the following: The **Select Message Flows** dialog opens. It shows all Message Flows that are grouped by a Conversation.
  - 
    - Open the Conversation shape Specification window. Select the Message Flow Refs property value cell and then click the ... button.
    - Select a Communication shape and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the Message Flows button.
2. Select one or more Message Flows and click **OK**. When the Show Message Flows Related to Participants check box is selected, only the message flows that exist in a project between the Representing Participants of the pools connected by a Conversation will show. 
Click to clear the check box to see all message flows in a project.

You can also assign a Message Flow to a Conversation by dragging it from the Containment tree to a Conversation shape on the BPMN Collaboration diagram.

To create a SubConversation

- On the diagram pallet, click SubConversation .

A SubConversation can contain inner Conversation Nodes (Conversation and SubConversation).

To create a Conversation Node for a SubConversation, do one of the following

- Select SubConversation and on the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the SubConversation button.
- Open the Specification window, select Conversation Nodes in the property group list on the left. Click the Create button and select a Node type. Type the name and click Close .

A CallConversation can reference Collaboration or Global Conversation.

To create a CallConversation

- On the diagram pallet, click SubConversation .

**Related elements**

- Conversation
- SubConversation
- Call Conversation
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']
- Message Flow
- Pool and Lane

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Creating and Using Pool and Lanes
- Creating and Using Message Flow
- Creating and Using Participant

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567013 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04923: Creating and Using Data Items

- page_id: `249567013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567013/Creating+and+Using+Data+Items
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:34:57.303+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the following data items in a BPMN Process diagram:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Object' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Store' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Input and Data Output' space='']

You can mark a Data Object as a collection.

With a Data Association, you can connect a data item to other elements of the BPMN Process diagram.

To create a data item

Business Analyst perspective provide most popular SubProcess types. Full list ofSubProcesses types you can have only in Business Modeler and Full Featured perspectives.

- On the Process diagram pallet, click Data Objec t and select needed Data item.

To create a new Data Object, which is typed by a classifier

- Select the classifier in the Containment tree and drag it to a BPMN Process or BPMN Collaboration diagram.

To specify a type of Data Item, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it on the Data Item.
- Right-click the Data Item, from the shortcut menu, select Type and choose the needed type.
- Open the Specification window and select a Type.

To mark a Data Object as collection, do one of the following

- Right-click the Data Object and from the shortcut menu choose Is Collection > true .
- Open the Data Object Specification window, Is Collection property set to true.

To create a Data Association between two data items

1. On the Process diagram pallet, click the Data Association button.
2. On the diagram pane
  - Click an element, which will be the source of the Data Association.
  - Click an element, which will be the target of the Data Association.

- The source or target of a Data Association can only be a Data Object, Data Store, Data Input, or Data Output.
- The other end of the Data Association should always be an activity or event.

To display a Data Object directly connected to a Sequence Flow

A Data Object connected to a Sequence Flow is a visual shortcut of two Data Associations.In the model, the Data Associations still exist, and the Data Object connecting to the Sequence Flow will be displayed.

1. Draw a Data Association from an Activity to a Data Object.
2. Draw another Data Association from the Data Object to another Activity.
3. Draw a Sequence Flow from the first to the second Activity.
4. Right-click the Data Object and select Show Connected to Sequence Flow from the shortcut menu.

**Related element**

- Common BPMN Elements

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create the following data items in a BPMN Process diagram:</p><ul><li data-uuid="568def96-449c-4a44-a064-63201f17bbff"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Data Object" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="51f84ec9-b50f-4a4a-a082-f787ec6c49c6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Data Store" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="c27448fa-5ddb-463d-aa16-420b4e9deb58"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Data Input and Data Output" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>You can mark a Data Object as a collection.</p><p>With a Data Association, you can connect a data item to other elements of the BPMN Process diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a data item</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ae59501-80ba-47c3-81c1-6195979319dc"><ac:rich-text-body>Business Analyst perspective provide most popular SubProcess types. Full list ofSubProcesses types you can have only in Business Modeler and Full Featured perspectives.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="cbd722bc-0b2c-4483-91da-99a8ee8df2f6">On the <strong>Process</strong> diagram pallet, click <strong>Data Objec</strong>t and select needed Data item.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a new Data Object, which is typed by a classifier</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="c8080c50-cd8c-4c25-a2c5-2ddd9b47f336">Select the classifier in the Containment tree and drag it to a BPMN Process or BPMN Collaboration diagram.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To specify a type of Data Item, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="3ff130fd-66cd-49c2-a1cc-59249609bb89">In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it on the Data Item.</li><li data-uuid="41c63401-8949-436b-b290-89bbc385d7ef">Right-click the Data Item, from the shortcut menu, select Type and choose the needed type.</li><li data-uuid="e2921565-a2d0-4a0e-9b32-f9c7e72ff4e3">Open the Specification window and select a Type.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To mark a Data Object as collection, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="85e57bbd-e61d-493a-83b1-4280ded52ac2">Right-click the Data Object and from the shortcut menu choose <strong>Is Collection</strong> &gt; <strong>true</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="baf0b56d-1923-4749-bfe6-6d14520c9608">Open the Data Object Specification window, <strong>Is Collection</strong> property set to true.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a Data Association between two data items</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e6b5b4fc-bfcb-4359-9a57-0636d7993cd3">On the Process diagram pallet, click the <strong>Data Association</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="ea4258ef-dcca-4119-b109-bc45ac63d711">On the diagram pane<ul><li>Click an element, which will be the source of the Data Association.</li><li>Click an element, which will be the target of the Data Association.</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eecd0ebf-5961-40cb-90e2-968c01a61ff2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="b924200a-f2d9-47ec-ba6f-da022f94f942">The source or target of a Data Association can only be a Data Object, Data Store, Data Input, or Data Output.</li><li data-uuid="aafc8a01-2722-441a-9cef-4f6f88fbba54">The other end of the Data Association should always be an activity or event.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display a Data Object directly connected to a Sequence Flow</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e38d3546-acb6-4895-9c34-8f7ebdcd1f27"><ac:rich-text-body>A Data Object connected to a Sequence Flow is a visual shortcut of two Data Associations.In the model, the Data Associations still exist, and the Data Object connecting to the Sequence Flow will be displayed.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="23ad376a-6268-420c-9a50-256e621bb7ce">Draw a Data Association from an Activity to a Data Object.</li><li data-uuid="e15ddab8-7daa-4314-b563-6f7b445fa3a7">Draw another Data Association from the Data Object to another Activity.</li><li data-uuid="476f8b56-5919-4b8a-8f00-1dc3ffe978b3">Draw a Sequence Flow from the first to the second Activity.</li><li data-uuid="5ffee8db-bff0-44f6-ad6e-d5043d170d4e">Right-click the Data Object and select <strong>Show Connected to Sequence Flow</strong> from the shortcut menu.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="92691510-92fe-419a-a638-467a062616f6"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Common+BPMN+Elements">Common BPMN Elements</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="7dc1c653-a9a8-4df7-8ebb-39e23d5d37c9"><a <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567023 space=MT version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 04924: Creating and Using Message Flow

- page_id: `249567023`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567023/Creating+and+Using+Message+Flow
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:19:48.258+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To draw a Message Flow between two elements or Pools, do one of the following

- On the Collaboration diagram pallet, click Message Flow button and connect appropriate shapes.
- Select the Pool header and on the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the Message Flow button. A Message Flow must connect two different Pools or inner elements of different Pools.

To create a new Message for a Message Flow, which is typed by a classifier, by using drag and drop operation

- Select the classifier (Class, Input Set, Output Set, or Error) in the Containment tree and drag it to a Message Flow on a Collaboration diagram.

To create a Message for a Message Flow from the Smart Manipulator toolbar

You can create a Message in a BPMN Process, Collaboration, Choreography, or SubProcess element.

1. On the diagram pane, select a Message Flow path and on the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click the Referenced Messages button. The Select Message dialog opens.
2. Select a Collaboration where you want to create a new message and click the Creation Mode button.
3. Select a Collaboration element and click the Create button.
4. Click OK .

To change a Message display mode

- Right-click a Message Flow and from the shortcut menu select one of the following:
  - Show Message and select a message display mode.
  - Symbol(s) Properties and select the needed Show Message property value.

To change direction of Message Flow

- Right-click the Message Flow and from the shortcut menu, select Refactor > Reverse Direction .

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']
- Message Flow
- Pool and Lane

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Creating and Using Pool and Lanes
- Creating and Using Conversation Nodes
- Creating and Using Participant

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567027 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04925: Creating and Using Participant

- page_id: `249567027`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567027/Creating+and+Using+Participant
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:22:25.710+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Participant

- Right-click the BPMN Collaboration or Choreography element in the Containment tree and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element > Participant .

To create a multi-instance Participant

1. Right-click a created Participant in the Containment tree and select Specification from the shortcut menu. The Participant Specification window opens.
2. In the Participant Specification window, under the Multiplicity category in the General property group, type the Minimum property value (“2” or greater).

The Minimum Multiplicity value of a MultiInstance participant propertymust be “2” or greater.

**Related elements**

- Participant
- Pool and Lane
- Choreography Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Using BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- Using BPMN Choreography Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567021 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04926: Creating and Using Pool and Lanes

- page_id: `249567021`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567021/Creating+and+Using+Pool+and+Lanes
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:18:42.134+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Pool in a BPMN collaboration diagram represents a participant of a collaboration. A Pool can have its inner process flows defined. You can add the elements from a BPMN Process diagram to a pool. You can also hide the pool details by displaying a “black box” pool. A Pool can also have inner lanes.

To create a Pool from Diagram pallet

1. On the Collaboration Diagram pallet, click Horizontal Pool and select a needed type of Pool.
2. Select Resources and click OK .

To create a Pool with a representing Resources from the Containment tree

- Drag a Resource, Organization, Post, or Person from the Containment tree to a BPMN Collaboration diagram pane.

To create a Pool with a suppressed content

- On the Collaboration Diagram pallet, under Conversations click Participant (Pool) .

To add Lane to a Pool, do one of the following

- Right-click a Pool, point to Insert Inner Lanes or Insert Lanes and select a lane type.
- Right-click a Pool header and click to select Insert Lanes .

To open a Pool Specification window

- Right-click the Pool header and select Specification .

To specify a model element represented by a Pool or Lane, do one of the following

A Pool can represent a BPMN Resource element or UML classifier element.

- Click a Pool or Lane header on the diagram. On the Smart Manipulators toolbar click the Represents button.
- Open the Pool or Lane Specification window, click **Represents** and select the represented elements from drop down list. Any type of element you can select only from Specification window.
- Select a represented element in the Containment tree and drag it to the Pool or Lane header on the diagram.

To hide a Pool content on a diagram (to display a “black box” pool)

- Right-click a Pool header and from the shortcut menu select one of the following:
  - Suppress Pool Content
  - Symbol(s) Properties and in the open dialog set the Suppress Pool Content property value to true.

A Pool with suppressed content that references a MultiInstance Participant will be displayed with a MultiInstance marker.

To display a MultiInstance marker on a Pool

1. Open the Specification window.
2. Click **Represents** and select a represented multi-instance Participant. A MultiInstance marker can be displayed only on a Poll with suppressed contents.

To review the Pool or Lane traceability information

- Open the Specification window, click Traceability .

**Related elements**

- Resource
- Participant
- Pool and Lane
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Creating and Using Message Flow
- Creating and Using Conversation Nodes
- Creating and Using Participant
- Using Common BPMN Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Pool in a BPMN collaboration diagram represents a participant of a collaboration. A Pool can have its inner process flows defined. You can add the elements from a BPMN Process diagram to a pool. You can also hide the pool details by displaying a “black box” pool. A Pool can also have inner lanes.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Pool from Diagram pallet</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a9bc005a-b93a-45a3-b247-33f7156b6e62">On the Collaboration Diagram pallet, click <strong>Horizontal Pool</strong> and select a needed type of Pool.</li><li data-uuid="91c1c951-acd0-451c-81e0-aa9280cadc1d">Select Resources and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a Pool with a representing Resources from the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="9871fe5b-cbed-4ead-bce7-be076148aac2">Drag a Resource, Organization, Post, or Person from the Containment tree to a BPMN Collaboration diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a Pool with a suppressed content</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="04a93003-d86b-44e9-95c4-1bede500e4c7">On the Collaboration Diagram pallet, under <strong>Conversations</strong> click <strong>Participant (Pool)</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add Lane to a Pool, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="79d8a45d-809e-4793-88f9-02ddaa58f13b">Right-click a Pool, point to <strong>Insert Inner Lanes</strong> or <strong>Insert Lanes</strong> and select a lane type.</li><li data-uuid="6c7012f9-e37e-41ec-a1df-1df82f25691c">Right-click a Pool header and click to select <strong>Insert Lanes</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To open a Pool Specification window</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="5b1649ad-b587-4442-9515-ba496e629573">Right-click the Pool header and select <strong>Specification</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To specify a model element represented by a Pool or Lane, do one of the following</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f77c8e05-ba6d-48e8-befc-72902e18da92"><ac:rich-text-body>A Pool can represent a BPMN Resource element or UML classifier element.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="1fe612b8-eae4-42f9-aeec-7b323c1425ac">Click a Pool or Lane header on the diagram. On the Smart Manipulators toolbar click the <strong>Represents</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="ab18aa0d-52d9-4654-a71d-2fc1b0e17036"><p>Open the Pool or Lane Specification window, click <strong>Represents</strong> and select the represented elements from drop down list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec55315d-89d1-46a5-9fa6-b901b453ea55"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Any type of element you can select only from Specification window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="2ad3a9c7-fd18-4a9f-8b46-aa1b709678d3"><p>Select a represented element in the Containment tree and drag it to the Pool or Lane header on the diagram.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To hide a Pool content on a diagram (to display a “black box” pool)</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="48c28b13-f33f-4ce9-a7a0-6ef44b190554">Right-click a Pool header and from the shortcut menu select one of the following:<ul><li><strong>Suppress Pool Content</strong></li><li><strong>Symbol(s) Properties</strong> and in the open dialog set the <strong>Suppress Pool Content</strong> property value to true.</li></ul></li></ul><p>A Pool with suppressed content that references a MultiInstance Participant will be displayed with a MultiInstance marker.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display a MultiInstance marker on a Pool</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0d0b98b8-938d-4d29-af4d-eefb246dbc27">Open the Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="7d751203-1e35-4f2b-bc05-8ac31a0c9bee"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Represents</strong> and select a represented multi-instance Participant.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eae4f50c-0c1a-45e4-ad82-f95f45ef0cfd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A MultiInstance marker can be displayed only on a Poll with suppressed contents.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To review the Pool or Lane traceability information</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="b38a204b-9e6c-4314-a440-497d2b7d913e">Open the Specification window, click <strong>Traceability</strong>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567002 space=MT version=4 -->
````
## PAGE 04928: Creating and Using SubProcesses

- page_id: `249567002`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567002/Creating+and+Using+SubProcesses
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:28:40.887+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A BPMN diagram can display subprocesses with different symbol properties. They are as follows:

- An expanded SubProcess can contain inner shapes.
- A collapsed SubProcess cannot display inner shapes. A plus sign (+) marker will be displayed for a collapsed subprocess.

If the *Is Triggered By Event* property of a SubProcess is set to true, it will call an Event SubProcess and will be drawn with a dashed border.

There are two specific types of SubProcesses:

- Transaction SubProcess. It is represented with double borders.
- AdHoc SubProcess. It is represented with an AdHoc marker on its shape.

To create a SubProcess

Business Analyst perspective provide most popular SubProcess types. Full list of SubProcesses types you can have only in Business Modeler and Full Featured perspectives.

1. Open a BPMN Process diagram.
2. On the diagram pallet, click the arrow next to the SubProcess and select the needed type of SubProcess.

To expand a SubProcess, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubProcess shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol(s) Properties. Then, in the Symbol Properties dialog, click to clear the Suppress Content .
- Right-click a SubProcess shape and in the shortcut menu click to clear Suppress Content.

To collapse SubProcess, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubProcess shape and then on the shortcut menu click to select Suppress Content.
- Right-click a SubProcess shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol(s) Properties. Then in the Symbol Properties dialog click to select the Suppress Content.

To mark a created SubProcess as an event SubProcess, do one of the following

- Right-click the SubProcess and on the shortcut menu click to select Triggered By Event .
- Open the SubProcess Specification window. Select the Triggered By Event check box.

To convert a SubProcess to a Transaction SubProcess or AdHoc SubProcess

- Right-click the SubProcess and from the shortcut menu select Refactor > Convert To > More Specific > AdHoc SubProcess or Transaction SubProcess .

To convert a Transaction SubProcess or AdHoc SubProcess to a SubProcess

- Right-click the Ad Hoc SubProcess or Transaction SubProcess and from shortcut menu select Refactor > Convert To > More General > SubProcess .

To display a Start Event icon on a Event SubProcess

1. Create an expanded Event SubProcess.
2. Create a Start Event to the Event SubProcess.
3. Right-click the SubProcess and click to select the Suppress Content check box.

**Related element**

- SubProcesses

**Related Diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Creating and Using Tasks
- Using Activities
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']
- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow
- Creating and Using Data Items
- Navigation Between BPMN Diagrams

It is represented with double borders.</li><li data-uuid="4724fdf4-f649-401c-a06c-0069257cfe65">AdHoc SubProcess. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567000 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04929: Creating and Using Tasks

- page_id: `249567000`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567000/Creating+and+Using+Tasks
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:26:41.113+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are multiple types of Tasks that you can create in a BPMN Process diagram.

To create a Task

1. Open a Process diagram.
2. On the diagram pallet, click the Task button and select a type of Task you need to create.

To change a task type

- Right-click a Task and select one of the following task types:
  - Business Rule Task
  - Manual Task
  - Receive Task
  - Script Task
  - Send Task
  - Service Task
  - User Task

**Related element**

- Tasks

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

- Using Activities
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']
- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow
- Creating and Using Data Items
- Navigation Between BPMN Diagrams

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561670 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04930: Creating Binding Connector

- page_id: `249561670`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561670/Creating+Binding+Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:35.057+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='MED'] in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Parametric diagram toolbar

To create a Binding Connector from the Parametric diagram toolbar

1. Click the Binding Connector button.
2. Select the source of the connector ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] ).
3. Select the target of the connector.

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

To create a Binding Connector from the smart manipulator toolbar

1. Select the connector's source (either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] ).
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] , select the Binding Connector button.
3. Select the target of the connector.

A a result the Binding Connector is created with keyword «equal». You can change keyword«equal»to sign "=" in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialogby selecting the**All**properties mode and setting the**Show Equal Sign Notation**property value to true.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Binding Connector" /></ac:link> in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8939534-962d-4f76-aac2-abde89329f1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Using the Parametric diagram toolbar</h4><p>To create a Binding Connector from the Parametric diagram toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Binding Connector</strong> button.</li><li>Select the source of the connector (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>).</li><li>Select the target of the connector.</li></ol><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar</h4><p>To create a Binding Connector from the smart manipulator toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the connector's source (either <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>).</li><li>From the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the <strong>Binding Connector</strong> button.</li><li>Select the target of the connector.</li></ol><p>A a result the Binding Connector is created with keyword <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">«equal</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">»</span>. <span>You can change keyword </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">«equal</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">» </span><span>to sign &quot;=&quot; in the</span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link><span> by selecting the </span><strong>All</strong><span> properties mode and setting the</span><strong> Show Equal Sign Notation</strong><span> property value to true.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_equal_sign_notation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566440 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04931: Creating BPMN Projects

- page_id: `249566440`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566440/Creating+BPMN+Projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:20:30.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new project from template, you can choose one of the following templates:

- A BPMN2 Project, which provides the workspace for business process modeling. This project is empty.
- A Business Model project, which provides workspace with predefined project structure and guidelines how to create business model.

The Cameo SOA+ plugin is discontinued. However, you can load projects created in earlier versions with this plugin in your modeling tool. SoaML diagrams will be converted to pure UML diagrams and maintenance of the SoaML profile will continue. The SoaML Profile is available through the **Resource / Plugin Manager**.

To create a new workspace for an empty project

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the main menu, click File > New Project .
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='new_project.png'] button on the File toolbar.
  - Press Ctrl + N.
2. In the New Project dialog, select the project template under the Business Process Modeling domain. [ATTACHMENT filename='bpm_select.png']
3. Specify the file name in the Name box.
4. Click the ... button to define the location for storing your newly created project in your computer.
5. Click OK .

If you work not in the Business Analyst perspective, a message asking whether you want to change the perspective will open. Click Yes to switch to the Business Analyst perspective supporting business modeling diagrams.

**Related resource**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This project is empty.</li><li data-uuid="1af280ae-408e-4583-bba1-03e4f355d390">A Business Model project, which provides workspace with predefined project structure and guidelines how to create business model.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e5988e-5c6d-461c-bbd4-02a24ba8603d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Cameo SOA+ plugin is discontinued. Click Yes to switch to the Business Analyst perspective supporting business modeling diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related resource</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="ea9e0d02-2d28-4430-a561-9093103c9674"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563409 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04932: Creating C1 diagram

- page_id: `249563409`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563409/Creating+C1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:54.463+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C1 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C1 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can link Capabilities with the Logical and Physical Resource elements using Exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibition fill in the Exhibits relationship Environment Conditions property.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563592 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04933: Creating C1-S1 matrix

- page_id: `249563592`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563592/Creating+C1-S1+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:20.363+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-c1-s1-matrix', 'build-c1-s1-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix****depicts which Services contribute to the achievement of a Capability directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.****

To create C1-S1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix<strong> </strong>depicts which Services contribute to the achievement of a Capability directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.<strong> </strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To create C1-S1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563416 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04934: Creating C2 diagram

- page_id: `249563416`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563416/Creating+C2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:54.638+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C2 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['create-c2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C2 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. Specify the types of parts. The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.

Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To complete fully the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''], the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add the Structural and Temporal Parts for the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Create Element" ac:alt="Create Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_from_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image> smart manipulator.</li><li><p>Specify the types of parts.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7c75b24-976a-43ed-bce0-dacb135d54e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Additionally the Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To complete fully the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. The Exhibits relationship specifies the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises.</p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 04935: Creating C3 diagram

- page_id: `249563422`
- space_key: `MT`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C3 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C1 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependency relationships.
