<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a C3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C1 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C1 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>(recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependency relationships.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563427 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04936: Creating C4 matrix

- page_id: `249563427`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563427/Creating+C4+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:17.434+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C4 Standard Processes
- labels: ['create-c4', 'build-c4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Standard Processes (C4)****describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a C4 matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope (Standard Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Columns Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Standard Processes (C4)<strong> </strong><span>describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p>To create a C4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong> (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563433 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04937: Creating C5 diagram

- page_id: `249563433`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563433/Creating+C5+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:55.131+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C5 Effects
- labels: ['create-c5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a C5 diagram

1. Create a Desirer (e.g., Capability).
2. Create an Actual State (e.g., Actual Effect) and set a type (e.g., Effect).
3. Connect a Desirer with the Actual State using the Desires relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of C5 Effects diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a C5 diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Desirer (e.g., Capability).</li><li>Create an Actual State (e.g., Actual Effect) and set a type (e.g., Effect).</li><li>Connect a Desirer with the Actual State using the Desires relationship.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="c5_effects_diagram_sample.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of C5 Effects diagram.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563442 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04938: Creating C8 diagram

- page_id: `249563442`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563442/Creating+C8+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:17.697+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C8 Planning Assumptions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1709625638

1709625659

1709625637

**On this page**

1709625649

##### Creating a diagram

To create a C8 diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the C8 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > C8 Planning Assumptions . [ATTACHMENT filename='c8_creating.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for C8 Planning Assumptions and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='c8_new_capability.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the C8 package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating and applying Strategic Constraints

You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See . Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.

To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed Capability and open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Strategic > Strategic Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.

To apply Strategic Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability.
- In the needed Capability Specification window, go to the Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

1487199865

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='']

Search for the Capability </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or another appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="227" ac:width="304"><ri:attachment ri:filename="c8_new_capability_containment.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="271" ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="c8_drag_capability.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating and applying Strategic Constraints</h3><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating an element" />. Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a needed Capability and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Constraints property group, click <strong>Create </strong>&gt; <strong>Strategic </strong>&gt; <strong>Strategic Constraint</strong>.</li><li>Specify the name.</li><li><p>In the Specification cell, define the rule. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0e4674c-3d35-46c9-8132-9ab14424f243"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The specification<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> (rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To apply Strategic Constraints, d</span>o one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability.</li><li>In the needed Capability <strong>Specification </strong>window, go to the <strong>Constraints </strong>property group. Click the <strong>Apply </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> window, choose a constraint.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17ae0cde-5543-4dba-b5f8-ce8ef96358e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487199865</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C8 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563448 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04939: Creating C8 table

- page_id: `249563448`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563448/Creating+C8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:55.527+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > C8 Planning Assumptions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487265801

1487265822

1487265812

The**Planning Assumptions (C8)**table identifies and describes assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.

To add a new Strategic Constraint to the C8 table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the**Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint**dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Strategic Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the C8 Planning Assumptions table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

1487265800

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating C8 diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and in the<span> </span><strong>Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint</strong><span> </span>dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Strategic Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table. </li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the C8 Planning Assumptions table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73422e5-e808-4731-a6be-c207afc98a52"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487265800</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating C8 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567037 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04940: Creating Call Choreography Activity

- page_id: `249567037`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567037/Creating+Call+Choreography+Activity
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:17:36.171+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Choreography Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Business Analyst perspective provide most popular Call Choreographytypes. Full list of Call Choreography types you can have only in Business Modeler and Full Featured perspectives.

A Call Choreography Activity holds a reference to a Choreography.

To create a Call Choreography Activity

1. On the diagram pallet, click Call Choreography Activity. The Select Behavior dialog opens.
2. Select a Choreography Activity .
3. Click OK .

To change the element called by a Call Choreography Activity

- Right-click a Call Choreography Activity and click Called Choreography Ref . Select a Choreography from the elements list.
- Open the Call Choreography Activity Specification window. Click Called Choreography Ref and select a Choreography as property value.

**Related element**

- Call Choreography

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Using Choreography Activity
- Creating and Using Choreography Task
- Creating SubChoreography
- Creating Call Choreography Activity
- Using Common BPMN Elements

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565125 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04941: Creating comments for UAF elements

- page_id: `249565125`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565125/Creating+comments+for+UAF+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:48.964+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Specification

Documentation/Comment

Additionally

.

Create Element

###### Creating a Definition for any UAF element

To create a Definition

1. Select an element and open its Specification window.
2. Go to the Documentation/Comment property group.
3. Click the Add button and choose Definition.
4. Add required specifics and click Close .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating Vision Statement for Enterprise Vision

To create a Vision Statement

1. Select an Enterprise Vision element and open its Specification window.
2. Go to the Documentation/Comment property group.
3. Click the Add button and choose Vision Statement.
4. Add required specifics and click Close .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561605 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04942: Creating Constraint Parameters automatically

- page_id: `249561605`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561605/Creating+Constraint+Parameters+automatically
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:07.804+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create Constraint Parameters automatically when:

- 
- Using the Parse and Create Parameters button .

bindingTo automatically create new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] when creating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='MED']

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select an appropriate property shape and, from the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='binding_connector.png'] .
  - From the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='binding_connector_from_toolbar.png'] , and select an appropriate property shape.
2. Connect it to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] shape: - if all parameters from the constraint equation are already created in the model, a new parameter is created with the same name as the property name. [ATTACHMENT filename='automatically_ created_parameter.png'] - if one or more parameters from the constraint equation are not created in the model, choose them from the Select Parameter menu list. [ATTACHMENT filename='automatically_ created_parameter_from_menu_list.png']

equationTo create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] automatically using the **Parse and Create Parameters** button

1. Create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] shape or select an existing one.
2. Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.
3. Do either: Constraint Parameters are created. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_parameters_automatically_from_smart_manipulator_toolbar1.png']
  - Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='parse_and_create_parameters.png'] .
  - Right-click the shape and select Tools > Parse and Create Parameters .

Note that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] type is always a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'], thus all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='MED'] are created under that particular Constraint Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561570 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04943: Creating contextual Satisfy

- page_id: `249561570`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561570/Creating+contextual+Satisfy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:07.019+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Contextual relationships
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a contextual Satisfy relationship by simply dragging-and-dropping the selected property onto the requirement directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space=''].

To create a contextual Satisfy relationship

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space=''] , locate the property that satisfies the selected requirement.
2. Place it onto the requirement and select Create Satisfy (contextual) . The relationship is created . [ATTACHMENT filename='dragging_and_dropping_to_create_contextual_satisfy.png'] Creating a contextual Satisfy relationship from the Structure tab.

Dragging-and-dropping a property from another [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] tab results in the automatic creation of a non-contextual Satisfy relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a contextual Satisfy relationship by simply dragging-and-dropping the selected property onto the requirement directly <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ce4c533e-5531-426a-9b65-844bdb0dd7d0">from</ac:inline-comment-marker> the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[S]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tructure tab]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p><br /></p><p>To create a contextual Satisfy relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure tab" /></ac:link>, locate the property that satisfies the selected requirement.</li><li>Place it onto the requirement and select <strong>Create Satisfy (contextual)</strong>. The relationship is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e0bae97d-fa3c-4636-beda-842d8d876281">created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_and_dropping_to_create_contextual_satisfy.png" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a contextual Satisfy relationship from the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="978bc76b-d0cf-47ee-bf4b-45a4e9a61101">Structure</ac:inline-comment-marker> tab.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e488cf16-4d91-49f8-94bf-815179b5455b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging-and-dropping a property from another <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> tab results in the automatic creation of a non-contextual Satisfy relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563456 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04944: Creating Cr diagram

- page_id: `249563456`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563456/Creating+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:11.303+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: ['create-cr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Cr diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To create a Cr diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap** check box.

To create an Cr diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Cr Package and select Create Diagram > Cr Capability Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e741dd50-b862-463a-b157-79fa209e36fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Cr Capability Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Cr diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Cr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Cr Capability Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Cr Capability Roadmap chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12453914-2d6d-4cf8-906c-7319424ff5ab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967411 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04945: Creating custom aspects

- page_id: `243967411`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967411/Creating+custom+aspects
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:10:34.639+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581366052

581366054

581366053

You can define your own diagram aspects that you want to use in the project. You can create custom aspects in the same way you create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']. The procedures below describe how to create a custom diagram aspect, e.g., Air, in two different ways:

- .
- 

##### createpd

To create a new aspect on the Profile Diagram pane

1. Open or create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space=''] .
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , click the Stereotype button, place it on the diagram pane, and type its name, e.g., Air.
3. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option.
4. Expand packages *UML Standard Profile* > *MagicDraw Profile*, and drag the *aspect* Stereotype on the same diagram pane.
5. Create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MED'] relationship from the newly created Stereotype to the *aspect* Stereotype. 
The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The creation of the new diagram aspect on the Profile Diagram pane.

##### createct

To create a new aspect in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select either a Profile or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED']
2. From its shortcut menu , click the Create Element button.
3. In the Create Element dialog, select Stereotype, and type its name, e.g., Air.
4. Open its Specification window, select the All properties mode, and find the Base Classifier property.
5. Select the value cell of the Base Classifier property, click the Edit button.
6. In the Select Stereotype dialog, clear the Apply Filter check box.
7. In the Tree tab, expand packages *UML Standard Profile* > *MagicDraw Profile*, and double click to select the *aspect* Stereotype.
8. Click **OK** and **Close**. The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The creation of the new diagram aspect in the Containment tree.

After the custom stereotype is created in the model, the Composite Structure diagram with the custom aspect applied can be found in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']**Create Diagram** dialog under the **Aspect Diagrams** group when creating a new aspect diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an aspect diagram' space='']>]]>

581366051

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966020 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04946: Creating custom project templates

- page_id: `243966020`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966020/Creating+custom+project+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:50.972+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Creating and using project templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create your custom project template that appears in the **New Project** dialog.

Each project template is saved in a separate folder in *<modeling tool installation directory>\templates*. The template folder should consist of the following files:

- A project file containing predefined initial project data.
- An .xml file describing project template data. See the following figure wherein all customizable template data are depicted.
- An image of a dialog title bar icon that is displayed in the title bar of the New Project dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 80 x 80 pixels. Recommended image file formats are .gif or .png .
- An image of a template icon that is displayed in a particular project template category of the New Project dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 24 x 24 pixels. Recommended image file formats are .gif or .png .

Folder name, project file name and *.xml* file name must be identical.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

While creating a project template, you can also create a category wherein your template can be located. If you need to have several project templates under the same category, in the .xml file, define the same category name for these templates. 
 
To create a custom project template

1. If the modeling tool is running, close it.
2. In < modeling tool installation directory>\templates create a new folder for your template files.
3. Paste a project file and icon image files to the created folder. The project file name should be the same as the folder name.
4. In the same folder, create an .xml file and name it the same name as a folder name. The .xml file content should be as follows: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> 
<custom_template> 
<icon>Project_Template_Icon.gif</icon> 
<iconLabel>Projecttemplatename</iconLabel> 
<perspective>MagicDrawperspectivename</perspective> 
<tooltip>Projecttemplatetooltiptext</tooltip> 
<banner_title>Titlebarname</banner_title> 
<banner_description>Projecttemplatedescription</banner_description> 
<category>Templatecategoryname</category> 
<banner_icon>Title_Bar_Icon.png</banner_icon> 
<cloneModules/> 
</custom_template>
5. Save the file and start the modeling tool. Your created project template is displayed in the **New Project** dialog in the category defined by you. Select your template and create a new project. The easiest way to create a custom template is to copy and paste the **Guide to UML****Diagrams****Project** folder with the different name and edit its inner files. This folder comes with the default modeling tool installation and is located in *<modeling tool installation directory>\templates*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create your custom project template that appears in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog.</p><p>Each project template is saved in a separate folder in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\templates</em>. The template folder should consist of the following files:</p><ul><li>A project file containing predefined initial project data.</li><li>An <em>.xml</em> file describing project template data. See the following figure wherein all customizable template data are depicted.</li><li>An image of a dialog title bar icon that is displayed in the title bar of the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 80 x 80 pixels. Recommended image file formats are <em>.gif</em> or <em>.png</em>.</li><li>An image of a template icon that is displayed in a particular project template category of the <strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong> dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 24 x 24 pixels. Recommended image file formats are <em>.gif</em> or <em>.png</em>.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="948270d5-a602-4f5f-b37a-d7ad20535f01"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Folder name, project file name and <em>.xml</em> file name must be identical.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="Customizable data for project templates" ac:alt="Customizable data for project templates"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizable_data_project_template.png" /></ac:image></p><p>While creating a project template, you can also create a category wherein your template can be located. If you need to have several project templates under the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="870a3c91-07c4-417a-9af2-fc2d699a2559">same</ac:inline-comment-marker> category, in the .xml file, define the same category name for these templates. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create a custom project template</p><hr /><ol><li>If the modeling tool is running, close it.</li><li>In &lt;<em>modeling tool installation directory&gt;\templates</em> create a new folder for your template files.</li><li>Paste a project file and icon image files to the created folder. The project file name should be the same as the folder name.</li><li>In the same folder, create an .xml file and name it the same name as a folder name. The .xml file content should be as follows:<br /><pre>&lt;?xml version=&quot;1.0&quot; encoding=&quot;UTF-8&quot;?&gt;<br />&lt;custom_template&gt;<br />&lt;icon&gt;Project_Template_Icon.gif&lt;/icon&gt;<br />&lt;iconLabel&gt;Projecttemplatename&lt;/iconLabel&gt;<br />&lt;perspective&gt;MagicDrawperspectivename&lt;/perspective&gt;<br class="atl-forced-newline" />&lt;tooltip&gt;Projecttemplatetooltiptext&lt;/tooltip&gt;<br />&lt;banner_title&gt;Titlebarname&lt;/banner_title&gt;<br />&lt;banner_description&gt;Projecttemplatedescription&lt;/banner_description&gt;<br />&lt;category&gt;Templatecategoryname&lt;/category&gt;<br />&lt;banner_icon&gt;Title_Bar_Icon.png&lt;/banner_icon&gt;<br />&lt;cloneModules/&gt;<br />&lt;/custom_template&gt;</pre><p><br /></p></li><li><p>Save the file and start the modeling tool. Your created project template is displayed in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog in the category defined by you. Select your template and create a new project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="894dc761-0f00-44bb-810c-5baf55900a9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The easiest way to create a custom template is to copy and paste the <strong>Guide to UML</strong><strong> Diagrams</strong><strong> Project</strong> folder with the different name and edit its inner files. This folder comes with the default modeling tool installation and is located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\templates</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971398 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04947: Creating custom tool

- page_id: `243971398`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971398/Creating+custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:13.250+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are a few steps involved in developing a custom tool. These steps can be summarized as follows:

1. Developing a Tool Class
2. Creating an Extension Package

#### Developing a Tool Class

Developing a Tool class requires setting a class path to *magicreport.jar*. You can find *magicreport.jar* from the library folder under the Report Wizard plugin directory. The following sample shows the source code for *Hello.java*.

```text

```

The sample shows two methods following the getter concept defined by the JavaBean specification.

Creating an Extension Package

The extension package is delivered in a *JAR* file. *JAR* (Java ARchive) is a file format based on the popular *ZIP* file format and is used for aggregating many files into one. To create a *JAR* file, you can store *.class with the Java package folder structure in a *ZIP* file format or creating from a *JAR* tool.

To combine files into a *JAR* file (in this case), here is the sample code fragment:

```text

```

In this example, all the class files in the current directory are placed in the file named "*MyTool.jar*". A manifest file entry named *META-INF/MANIFEST.MF* is automatically generated by the *JAR* tool. The manifest file is the place where any meta-information about the archive is stored as named: Value pairs. Please refer to the *JAR* file specification for more details.

```text

```

The example above shows that all the class files in the directory mytool are placed in the file named "*MyTool.jar*".

A complete *JAR* tool tutorial can be found at:

- http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/deployment/jar/
- http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/tools/windows/jar.html

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are a few steps involved in developing a custom tool. These steps can be summarized as follows:</p><ol><li>Developing a Tool Class</li><li>Creating an Extension Package</li></ol><h2>Developing a Tool Class</h2><p>Developing a Tool class requires setting a class path to <em>magicreport.jar</em>. You can find <em>magicreport.jar</em> from the library folder under the Report Wizard plugin directory. The following sample shows the source code for <em>Hello.java</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d70e644-5f8b-4de6-a310-af5773944920"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[package mytool;
import com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool;
public class HelloTool extends Tool
{
   public String getHello() {
      return "Hello World";
   }
   public String getHello(String name) {
      return "Hello " + name;
   }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The sample shows two methods following the getter concept defined by the JavaBean specification.</p><p>Creating an Extension Package</p><p>The extension package is delivered in a <em>JAR</em> file. <em>JAR</em> (Java ARchive) is a file format based on the popular <em>ZIP</em> file format and is used for aggregating many files into one. To create a <em>JAR</em> file, you can store *.class with the Java package folder structure in a <em>ZIP</em> file format or creating from a <em>JAR</em> tool.</p><p>To combine files into a <em>JAR</em> file (in this case), here is the sample code fragment:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86e17c54-b85f-42de-a480-1c3105057a4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[jar cf MyTool.jar *.class]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In this example, all the class files in the current directory are placed in the file named &quot;<em>MyTool.jar</em>&quot;. A manifest file entry named <em>META-INF/MANIFEST.MF</em> is automatically generated by the <em>JAR</em> tool. The manifest file is the place where any meta-information about the archive is stored as named: Value pairs. Please refer to the <em>JAR</em> file specification for more details.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c222cf1-44d1-4526-9a37-3acbb179510c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[jar cf MyTool.jar mytool]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The example above shows that all the class files in the directory mytool are placed in the file named &quot;<em>MyTool.jar</em>&quot;.</p><p>A complete <em>JAR</em> tool tutorial can be found at:</p><ul><li><a href="http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/deployment/jar/">http://java.sun.com/docs/books/tutorial/deployment/jar/</a></li><li><a style="line-height: 1.4285715;" href="http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/tools/windows/jar.html">http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/tools/windows/jar.html</a></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562484 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04948: Creating CV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562484`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562484/Creating+CV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.358+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-1 Vision
- labels: ['create-cv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-1 should be the first diagram for DoDAF architectures.

To create a CV-1 diagram

1. Create a Capability andActualEnterprise Phase.
2. Connect the Capability and the Actual Enterprise Phase using the Exhibits relationship.

An additional Actual Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Enterprise Goals and the Enterprise Vision by using the goal and vision properties of the Actual Enterprise Phase.

In order to complete the CV-1, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] should be modelled to create the Capabilities that exhibits ActualEnterprise Phase.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-1 should be the first diagram for DoDAF architectures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create a Capability and<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Enterprise Phase</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connect the Capability and the </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Enterprise Phase using the Exhibits relationship.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">An additional Actual Enterprise Phase can be created and linked to Enterprise Goals and the Enterprise Vi</span>sion by using the goal and vision properties of the Actual Enterprise Phase.</p><p>In order to complete the CV-1, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be modelled to create the Capabilities that exhibits <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span>Enterprise Phase.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562489 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04949: Creating CV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562489`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562489/Creating+CV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-cv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-2 should be modeled just before the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-1 Vision' space=''] is completed.

To create a CV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational Performers and Resources usingExhibits relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the *environmentConditions* property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-2 should be modeled just before the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-1 Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general-specific relationships) or Aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational Performers and Resources using<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Exhibits</span> relationship. Relationship can be associated with environmental Conditions by filling in the <em>environmentConditions</em> property and expressing the civil, military or physical conditions required for the realization of target Capability. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562494 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04950: Creating CV-3 diagram

- page_id: `249562494`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562494/Creating+CV-3+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:10:30.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['create-cv-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating CV-3 diagram, the CV-1 diagram with all necessary elements should be already created in the model. For this, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space='']. Otherwise, you can create those elements directly in the blank CV-3 chart. The following elements required for a smooth CV-3 diagram creation:

- Capability.
- Capability Provision.
- Incremental Milestone.
- End of Service Milestone.

The following procedures describe how to:

- Create CV-3 diagram by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''] .
- Create an CV-3 diagram when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''] is disabled.
- Fill in a blank CV-3 diagram.

##### Creating CV-3 diagram by using the CV-3 Creation Wizard

When you choose to create a new CV-3 diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens automatically.

To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create CV-3** check box.

##### Creating an CV-3 diagram when CV-3 Creation Wizard is disabled

To create an CV-3 diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Right-click the CV-3 Package and select Create Diagram > CV-3 Capability Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank CV-3 chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Filling in a blank CV-3 diagram

To fill in a blank CV-3 diagram

1. Add a Capability in the following ways: - To create new Capability, in the diagram toolbar, click the Add New button and select Capability. - To add already existing Capability from the model, in the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button and select Capability. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-12-2 16:58:42.png']
2. Type the name of added Capability.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For this, click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-1 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Otherwise, you can create those elements directly in the blank CV-3 chart. The following elements required for a smooth CV-3 diagram creation:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0c9172b6-0c8f-4ce9-b23f-3b52697d3c71">Capability.</li><li data-uuid="251cd399-eb86-4c7b-bc42-2bc157853259">Capability Provision.</li><li data-uuid="e6134e3e-639f-45b6-9974-d846c10aff5e"><p>Incremental Milestone.</p></li><li data-uuid="6a30099c-b78c-4b86-973a-d9250f98d5e7"><p>End of Service Milestone.</p></li></ul><p>The following procedures describe how to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="73d5b933-ada6-4d9b-b56a-01e2de400cbd">Create CV-3 diagram by using the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-3 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li data-uuid="bad93b43-9d8f-4fd6-892f-ce14eae93e00">Create an CV-3 diagram when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-3 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled.</li><li data-uuid="3f5a410f-6bbd-4c17-82f9-5c1a7af69ce9">Fill in a blank CV-3 diagram.</li></ul><h3>Creating CV-3 diagram by using the <span class="confluence-link">CV-3 Creation Wizard</span></h3><p>When you choose to create a new CV-3 diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-3 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens automatically.</p><p>To create a CV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3229fdf5-49ac-4d4b-9dac-e3cb3e8d0ae9">Right-click the CV-3 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="51ed8f86-492f-4110-9614-2e503e77026f">In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF </strong>options group.</li><li data-uuid="f492ad41-b126-4e3e-ad4e-e5c83d198594">In the General properties, set the <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Filling in a blank CV-3 diagram</h3><p>To fill in a blank CV-3 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="26126cc7-b6b0-4ea9-9e99-790c3c90b897">Add a Capability in the following ways:<br />- To create new Capability, in the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Capability.<br />- To add already existing Capability from the model, in the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select Capability.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-12-2 16:58:42.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d85c0d35-d400-40a2-b3c1-f08562f0933a">Type the name of added Capability.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="081d0f25-554f-4bd4-980d-1ab6ee88982a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="fea6c53d-b9d1-4470-8ace-551ca8d0b066"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562502 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04951: Creating CV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249562502`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562502/Creating+CV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:33.917+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['create-cv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CV-4 is created just after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] is completed.

To create a CV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-2 Capability Taxonomy' space=''] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CV-4 is created just after the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562506 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04952: Creating CV-5 table

- page_id: `249562506`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562506/Creating+CV-5+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:46.797+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-5', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

228955995

228956009

924237466

**On this page**

228955998

##### Creating CV-5 table

The context of the CV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the CV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the CV-5 table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a CV-5 table

To create an empty CV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a CV-5 table

To remove the deployment of theActual Resources from a CV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the CV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

228955990

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
