````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="253d647f-78f8-46a2-8506-edf4d9cae327"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955995</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0463f24b-1ec3-4776-9e36-435f67db21a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228956009</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f493355f-de7f-4970-99c2-8b6bd73db50b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924237466</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bf22f3c-9dbf-4c51-879f-068de9457660" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9af5bbcd-15a2-4587-a065-9aa2c4c4ded3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955998</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating CV-5 table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the CV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating CV-1 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_relation_to_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The content of the CV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the CV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5124318-7a44-4b50-acc7-4bc4bfa6ad14"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.</p><p>There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p>Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a CV-5 table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a CV-5</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a CV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt;<strong>CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>An<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capabilities from a CV-5 table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To hide Capabilities in a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Actual Resources from a CV-5 table</h3><p>To remove <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the deployment of the </span>Actual Resources from a CV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an Actual Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the CV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb6e248f-608d-45d2-8699-ff9b314e18f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228955990</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562522 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04953: Creating CV-6 matrix

- page_id: `249562522`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562522/Creating+CV-6+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.159+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-6 Capability to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-6-matrix', 'relate-operational-activity-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-6-matrix', 'build-cv-6-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Operational Activities Mapping (CV-6) describes the mapping between the capabilities and the operational activities that those capabilities support directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a CV-6 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using theMaps ToCapability relationship.

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Operational Activities Mapping (CV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the capabilities and the operational activities that those capabilities support directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Maps To </span>Capability relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map an Operational Activity to a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562528 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04954: Creating CV-7 matrix

- page_id: `249562528`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562528/Creating+CV-7+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.241+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Capability viewpoint > CV-7 Capability to Services Mapping
- labels: ['create-cv-7-matrix', 'relate-service-access-to-capability', 'delete-relation-from-cv-7-matrix', 'build-cv-7-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping (CV-7) depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enabledirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].The rows of this matrix areServicesand the columns are Capabilities.

To create a CV-7 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope ( Services ).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibitsrelationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping (CV-7) <span>depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enable </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>The rows of this matrix are<span> Services </span>and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a CV-7 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Services</span>).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Services exhibit</span> Capabilities using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563788 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04955: Creating D2 diagram

- page_id: `249563788`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563788/Creating+D2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:04.755+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Deployed Resources viewpoint > D2 Deployed Resources
- labels: ['create-d2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space=''] view.

To create an D2 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.
- the Persons and Actual Persons in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space=''] diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2012531-7254-4590-a8e5-84f712c78a1a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an D2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3260655-28c4-4062-a3ab-b66935884793"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others by using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Persons and Actual Persons in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966478 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04956: Creating Dependency Matrix

- page_id: `243966478`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966478/Creating+Dependency+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:32.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The matrix element in the model is similar to the diagram element. After creating a new matrix, it appears in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] as a model element.

To create a Dependency Matrix

1. In the Containment tree, select an element that can be the owner of the generic table.
2. Do one of the following: The newly dependency matrix opens on the right side of the application window.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Select Dependency Matrix and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbars, click the Create Diagram button. Select Dependency Matrix and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl + N . Select Dependency Matrix and press Enter .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select **Create Diagram** > **Analysis Diagrams** > **Dependency Matrix**. If you cannot see the **Dependency Matrix**, make sure the Expert mode is enabled.
3. Type the name of the matrix.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting criteria' space=''] for the matrix.
5. Click the Refresh [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The matrix is created.

You can also create dependency matrices using a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new dependency matrix type' space=''].****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The matrix element in the model is similar to the diagram element. After creating a new matrix, it appears in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> as a model element.</p><p> </p><p>To create a Dependency Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element that can be the owner of the generic table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Select <strong>Dependency Matrix</strong> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbars, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Select <strong>Dependency Matrix</strong> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl</strong>+<strong>N</strong>. Select <strong>Dependency Matrix</strong> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Analysis Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Dependency Matrix</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0888c118-b6c0-42b0-aff3-6fc4c53e7208"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see the <strong>Dependency Matrix</strong>, make sure the Expert mode is enabled.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The newly dependency matrix opens on the right side of the application window.</p></li><li>Type the name of the matrix.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select the criteria]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the matrix.</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The matrix is created.</li></ol><p> </p><p>You can also create dependency matrices using a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new dependency matrix type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom dependency matrix type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<strong><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></strong></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565129 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04957: Creating Desires

- page_id: `249565129`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565129/Creating+Desires
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:32.981+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['desired-effect', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Desires relationship relates the Desirer (Capability, Resource Performer, Operational Agent, or Capability Role) to an actual state (e.g., Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Resource).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Desires relationship relates the Desirer (Capability, Resource Performer, Operational Agent, or Capability Role) to an actual state (e.g., Actual Organization, Actual Person, Actual Resource).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="desired_effect.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967078 space=MT version=9 -->
## PAGE 04958: Creating diagrams

- page_id: `243967078`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967078/Creating+diagrams
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-10-15T10:45:43.942+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create any diagram in one of the following ways:

- 
- 
- 
- 

dlgTo create a diagram using the diagram creation****dialog

1. In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - On the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_diagram.png'] .
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the Create Diagram button.
3. When the diagram creation dialog opens, in the **Create Diagram** box, enter the diagram type you are searching for. Use the Camel Case, when searching for diagrams:Type the first letters (for example, "CD" to find "Class Diagram")Type lower-case letters (for example, "cd" instead of "CD")Skip the spacers (for example, "cv3" works as well as "CV-3" or "cv-3")Perform a partial search (for example, "ibd" finds SysML v1 Internal Block diagram) [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Select the diagram. Press Esc or click outside the dialog to close the diagram creation dialog without creating the diagram.

browserTo create a diagram from the Model Browser

1. Right-click the Package, Profile, Model, or other element where you want to create the diagram.
2. From the shortcut menu, point to **Create Diagram**, then click the diagram type you want to create. You can also create diagrams for multiple elements at the same time. Press and hold the**Shift**key while selecting several elements in your model.

specTo create a diagram from the element’s Specification window

1. Open the Inner Elements property group in the Package’s, Profile’s, Model’s, or other element’s Specification window.
2. Click the Create button. From the button's shortcut menu select Create Diagram , then choose the new diagram type. The Specification window of the new diagram opens.
3. In the open window, type the diagram name and click Close .

wizardTo create a diagram using the diagram creation wizard

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the main menu, click Diagrams > Diagram Wizards , then select the appropriate wizard depending on the type of diagram you want to create.
  - On the main menu, click Analyze > Model Visualizer . The Model Visualizer dialog with the list of available diagram creation wizards is opened. Select one and click the Start button.
2. Follow the steps of the selected diagram type creation wizard.

The newly created diagram is automatically named after the owner. If you create several diagrams of the same type under the same owner, their names are numbered.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create any diagram in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2b075842-0ccf-41a0-a367-c3f0d8d11b0b"><ac:link ac:anchor="dlg"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By using the diagram creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1c7b6762-ba89-4195-a15b-e6fbbe12ab7b"><ac:link ac:anchor="browser"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[From the Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="c7d1e355-9176-4798-9251-6904351c53ee"><ac:link ac:anchor="spec"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[From the element's Specification window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="44f785a7-5135-4d99-ab8e-70b8ee567f90"><ac:link ac:anchor="wizard"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[By using the diagram creation wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5b4bc1e-490c-43ba-8ad8-592401551d41"><ac:parameter ac:name="">dlg</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a diagram using the diagram creation<strong> </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1bbe51ab-c26a-4122-b70b-96e7a1d81e80">In the Model Browser, select the owner for the new diagram.</li><li data-uuid="bace7dc2-376c-42e1-9721-ebcbc9a95c3f">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagram.png" /></ac:image><span> .</span></li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Create Diagram</strong><span>.</span></li><li>Open the Content diagram and on its palette, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a9281742-1c91-468f-bb16-246f54da06bd"><p>When the diagram creation dialog opens, in the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> box, enter the diagram type you are searching for.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74718848-54af-4dbc-90b6-b9ccc9b058cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the Camel Case, when searching for diagrams:</p><ul><li>Type the first letters (for example, &quot;CD&quot; to find &quot;Class Diagram&quot;)</li><li>Type lower-case letters (for example, &quot;cd&quot; instead of &quot;CD&quot;)</li><li>Skip the spacers (for example, &quot;cv3&quot; works as well as &quot;CV-3&quot; or &quot;cv-3&quot;)</li><li>Perform a partial search (for example, &quot;ibd&quot; finds SysML v1 Internal Block diagram)</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:title="Create Diagram dialog" ac:alt="Create Diagram dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_diagram_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li data-uuid="e7381ca5-5867-4845-846b-d1ac59b6a094"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Select the diagram.</span><span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b45f97d6-f527-46bf-8167-81eff1993710"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Press Esc or click outside the dialog to close the diagram creation dialog without creating the diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0b254ed-f0b2-4f87-b1bf-aef0b567a1b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">browser</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a diagram from the Model Browser</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d77296ed-3a98-4dc7-824a-1559222a6cb5"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the Package, Profile, Model, or other element where you want to create the diagram.</p></li><li data-uuid="3a9377c1-2c3f-4bad-be61-957426908f40"><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, then click the diagram type you want to create.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0805ca03-cd0b-4ad8-add9-c6dc6a839f3e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can also create diagrams for multiple elements at the same time. Press and hold the </span><strong>Shift</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> key while selecting several elements in your model.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78576c04-16c2-4b4b-9988-4dbd475e3c22"><ac:parameter ac:name="">spec</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a diagram from the element’s Specification window</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="25d2b6cf-b8ac-440a-8327-e5fbbad2f1f5">Open the <strong>Inner Elements</strong> property group in the Package’s, Profile’s, Model’s, or other element’s Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="769854c0-3630-4270-a275-499a9fb8b7ed">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. From the button's shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, then choose the new diagram type. The Specification window of the new diagram opens.</li><li data-uuid="d2a003f6-36df-431d-8627-a5b21b02afae">In the open window, type the diagram name and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e78f307b-d10e-4366-a4c0-dde7418361fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="">wizard</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a diagram using the diagram creation wizard</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8a5788c6-d99d-46a0-b5e9-b70dd0c0bfab">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong>, then select the appropriate wizard depending on the type of diagram you want to create.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. The <strong>Model Visualizer</strong> dialog with the list of available diagram creation wizards is opened. Select one and click the <strong>Start</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="f758b00f-056e-4c92-9abf-b5d6406e7971">Follow the steps of the selected diagram type creation wizard.</li></ol><p>The newly created diagram is automatically named after the owner. If you create several diagrams of the same type under the same owner, their names are numbered.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_diagram_name.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="616a1e66-d32a-40fc-a71d-7e8e1901839e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968117 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04962: Creating elements from other resources

- page_id: `243968117`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968117/Creating+elements+from+other+resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:50.716+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

It is possible to copy a listed text from other resources (Word, Excel, HTML, etc.) and create as many elements as you need by pasting it. Each item of this list becomes a new element in the model. The basic numbering and bullets are not reproduced in the model.

You can do this either:

43

Avoid names duplication, because elements with the same name cannot be created for the same owner.

##### Creating elements in the Containment tree

To create the elements from the clipboard in the Containment tree

1. Copy a list.
2. Select the possible owner in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] .
3. From the shortcut menu select Paste or press Ctrl+V.
4. From the Select Type dialog choose the element type. The named elements are created in the model. As example, see the figure below where the Actors are created in the Containment tree by pasting the list.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Steps of creating elements from clipboard in Containment tree

##### Creating elements on the diagram pane

There are two ways to create the elements from the clipboard on the diagram pane:

- Using **Select Type** dialog.
- 

select_typeTo create the elements from the clipboard on the diagram pane using **Select Type** dialog

1. Copy a list.
2. Click a free space on the diagram pane.
3. Press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog select Elements . [ATTACHMENT filename='paste_special_dialog.png']
4. From the Select Type dialog choose the element type. The named elements are displayed on the diagram pane and are created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

paletteTo create the elements from the clipboard on the diagram pane using diagram palette

1. Copy a list.
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] select the element type.
3. Press Ctrl+V. The named elements are displayed in the diagram pane and are created in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967305 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04963: Creating elements in compartments on shapes

- page_id: `243967305`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967305/Creating+elements+in+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:27.893+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To quickly create elements in compartments on shapes, do one of the following

- On the diagram, select a shape and click the Create Element smart manipulator. Then in the menu, select the element that you want to create.
- On the diagram, select a shape. Then next to the particular compartment, click the Create <element name> smart manipulator.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To quickly create elements in compartments on shapes, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, select a shape and click the <em>Create Element</em> smart manipulator. Then in the menu, select the element that you want to create.</li><li>On the diagram, select a shape. Then next to the particular compartment, click the <em>Create &lt;element name&gt;</em> smart manipulator.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565132 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04964: Creating Entity Relationship Diagram

- page_id: `249565132`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565132/Creating+Entity+Relationship+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:33.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Entity Relationship diagram only shows entities as boxes (without structure) and relationships between them. It is useful for high-level, abstract domain modeling - provide a structure for business data, or define business terminology.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Entity Relationship diagram only shows entities as boxes (without structure) and relationships between them. It is useful for high-level, abstract domain modeling - provide a structure for business data, or define business terminology.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969088 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04965: Creating executable opaque behaviors

- page_id: `243969088`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969088/Creating+executable+opaque+behaviors
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:54.060+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations > Calling operations from the model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

337226649

337226661

337226647

**On this page**

337226651

Language of an executable opaque behavior can be OCL 2.0, binary, BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript, Jython, or StructuredExpression. Also, it must have the proper number of parameters with proper types.

##### How many parameters can an opaque behavior have?

The number of parameters an opaque behavior can have depends on the selected language of the opaque behavior body:

- An OCL 2.0 expression must have a single parameter.
- A binary expression must declare the exact number of parameters has the Java class, to which the expression body of the opaque behavior references.
- Other script expressions, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need.
- Structured expression can have as many parameters as you need.

##### How to create a parameter for the opaque behavior?

To create a parameter for the opaque behavior

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the opaque behavior, Parameters property group, click the Create button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the new parameters opens.
2. Enter a name of the new parameter.
3. Specify the multiplicity of the parameter: either select a value from the drop-down list or type a new one. In this case, only the upper bound of the multiplicity is important. For example, the multiplicity [0..1] has the same meaning as the multiplicity [1].
4. Specify the type of the parameter: either select a value from the drop-down list or create a new one by typing directly in the cell. Available types:Built-in UML primitives (Integer, Real, String, and so forth)UML element typesJava classes (java.io.File, java.util.Properties, and so forth)
5. If the multiplicity upper bound is 1, you may skip this step. Otherwise, specify the following:
  - Whether the arguments are unique.
  - Whether the arguments are ordered. If you do not see the Is Unique and Is Ordered properties, select All from the Properties drop-down list in the upper right of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
6. Close the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .

##### How to access the arguments and other values from script body of the opaque behavior?

The following instructions applies to BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript, and Jython scripts only.

To access an argument from a script body, you should refer to the corresponding parameter name.

The script body can access the following values:

- Arguments passed to an opaque behavior as parameters.
- Globally defined values:
  - project (current project)
  - application

337251506

**Related Pages**

- Model Elements
- Specification Window

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Also, it must have the proper number of parameters with proper types.</p><h3>How many parameters can an opaque behavior have?</h3><p>The number of parameters an opaque behavior can have depends on the selected language of the opaque behavior body:</p><ul><li>An OCL 2.0 expression must have a single parameter.</li><li>A binary expression must declare the exact number of parameters has the Java class, to which the expression body of the opaque behavior references.</li><li>Other script expressions, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need.</li><li>Structured expression can have as many parameters as you need.</li></ul><h3>How to create a parameter for the opaque behavior?</h3><p><br />To create a parameter for the opaque behavior</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the opaque behavior, <strong>Parameters</strong> property group, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the new parameters opens.</li><li>Enter a name of the new parameter.</li><li><p>Specify the multiplicity of the parameter: either select a value from the drop-down list or type a new one.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4c3540b-5434-4911-918a-dc5cae5182de"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this case, only the upper bound of the multiplicity is important. For example, the multiplicity [0..1] has the same meaning as the multiplicity [1].</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify the type of the parameter: either select a value from the drop-down list or create a new one by typing directly in the cell.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35106340-f3f6-48e9-af39-6ac10bb7111a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available types:</p><ul><li>Built-in UML primitives (Integer, Real, String, and so forth)</li><li>UML element types</li><li><span>Java classes (</span>java.io<span>.File, java.util.Properties, and so forth)</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If the multiplicity upper bound is 1, you may skip this step. Otherwise, specify the following:<ul><li>Whether the arguments are unique.</li><li><p>Whether the arguments are ordered.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b21a157-8b11-4315-8a8e-fe5ccfc7c7c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the Is Unique and Is Ordered properties, select All from the Properties drop-down list in the upper right of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>Close the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3>How to access the arguments and other values from script body of the opaque behavior?</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b834381-8056-477b-983a-50cec418284c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following instructions applies to BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript, and Jython scripts only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To access an argument from a script body, you should refer to the corresponding parameter name.</p><p>The script body can access the following values:</p><ul><li>Arguments passed to an opaque behavior as parameters.</li><li>Globally defined values:<ul><li>project (current project)</li><li>application</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60496968-0779-4026-acdd-024469e8e633"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">337251506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/UML+elements">Model Elements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification Window</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966939 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04966: Creating Generic Table

- page_id: `243966939`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966939/Creating+Generic+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:42.206+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Generic table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a generic table by any of the methods described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space=''].

When creating a Generic Table you should specify the following:

1. Denote the name of the table and the owner package (MANDATORY).
2. Define element types that will be used in the table (MANDATORY).
3. Select elements corresponding to the element types (OPTIONAL).
4. Specify table columns (OPTIONAL). Columns represent element properties, so the table creation could be finished without specifying columns.

You can modify the table after it is created.

This section describes several of the most popular ways of creating a generic table:

43

##### Creating a generic table without using a wizard

Once created, the table can be filled with data using the generic table environment capabilities.

To create a Generic Table without using a wizard

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , select an element to be the owner of the Generic Table.
2. Do one of the following: The newly created Generic Table opens on the right side of the application window.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “gen” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbars, click the Create Diagram button. Type “gen” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N . Type “gen” and press Enter .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > Generic Table .
3. Type the name of the table.
4. Select element types. select_type [ATTACHMENT filename='generic_table_select_element_type.png'] Select desired element types and/or stereotypes in the **Select Element Types** dialog. The **Element Type** box in the **Criteria** area of the Generic Table will display the selected items. This action is optional, so you can continue the creation without selecting any type or stereotype. You will be able to modify selected element types when the Generic Table is created. If you drag an element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] to the table, the type of this element will be automatically set as the **Element Type** box value.
5. Fill the table with elements:
  - Specify the scope, from which the elements should be added to the table. All the elements created in that particular scope will be automatically added to the table. If the contents of the table have not been updated, try the following:Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the **View** toolbar, in case a smart package is specified as the scope criteria.Make sure the specified scope contains elements that are specified in the**Element Type** box. Element types must correspond to the selected element types!Make sure at least one element type is specified for the table.
  - Create new elements directly from the table. How to craete new elements >>
  - Copy and paste elements from another resource. How to paste elements >>
  - Add existing elements to the table individually. [How to add existing elements >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Filling+the+table+with+elements#Fillingthetablewithelements-Addingexistingelementsfromthemodel)

##### creating_with_wizardCreating a Generic Table using the diagram creation wizard

Once created the table is already filled with data.

To create a Generic Table using the diagram creation wizard

1. Open the Generic Table Wizard dialog in one of the following ways:
  - On the Diagrams menu, click Diagram Wizards > Generic Table Wizard .
  - On the Analyze menu, click Model Visualizer .
2. Type the name of the table and select the owner for it. [ATTACHMENT filename='generic_table_wizard_create_diagram.png']
3. Select element types (see ). [ATTACHMENT filename='generic_table_wizard_select_types.png']
4. Select elements. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
Select elements from the model tree and add them into the **Selected** list. Though you can select any element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''], only the elements of selected types will be added into the Generic Table. 
If the Generic Table creation wizard is opened from one or more elements’ shortcut menu, the element or the set of elements will be added to the **Selected** list automatically.For more information about the element selection and dialog buttons, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space=''].
5. Specify the set of columns to show on the table. [ATTACHMENT filename='generic_table_wizard_selecting_columns.png'] The list of all available properties corresponding to selected element types is presented in the dialog. If selected element types have tags, they are also displayed in the list. All selected properties will be displayed as columns in the Generic Table. The **Name** property is selected automatically; all other properties should be selected by the user. Select properties you need to see as columns to finish the table.

##### Creating a Generic Table for a set of selected elements using the diagram creation wizard

Note that element types, elements, and columns of the Generic Table will be automatically selected according to theset of selected elements.

To create a new Generic Table for a set of selected elements

1. Select a set of elements you want to add to the Generic Table.
2. From the shortcut menu select Tools > Generic Table Wizard .
3. Follow the steps described in but note that element types, elements, and columns will be automatically selected according to the set of selected elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a generic table by any of the methods described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating any diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>When creating a Generic Table you should specify the following:</p><ol><li>Denote the name of the table and the owner package (MANDATORY).</li><li>Define element types that will be used in the table (MANDATORY).</li><li>Select elements corresponding to the element types (OPTIONAL).</li><li>Specify table columns (OPTIONAL). Columns represent element properties, so the table creation could be finished without specifying columns.</li></ol><p>You can <span class="confluence-link">modify the table</span> after it is created.</p><p><br /></p><p>This section describes several of the most popular ways of creating a generic table:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbb60f3b-8f4e-42cc-a549-fe62022083fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Creating a generic table without using a wizard</h3><p>Once created, the table can be filled with data using the generic table environment capabilities.</p><p>To create a Generic Table without using a wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select an element to be the owner of the Generic Table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “gen” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+N</strong>. Type “gen” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Other Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Generic Table</strong>.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The newly created Generic Table opens on the right side of the application window.</p></li><li>Type the name of the table.</li><li>Select element types.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e1e2f04-eb33-4dc6-af85-b3e4dabb4b35"><ac:parameter ac:name="">select_type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generic_table_select_element_type.png" /></ac:image> <br /><p>Select desired element types and/or stereotypes in the <strong>Select Element Types</strong> dialog. This action is optional, so you can continue the creation without selecting any type or stereotype. You will be able to modify selected element types when the Generic Table is created.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60efd6c5-acb3-4559-93be-cbfeeb4fe9ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you drag an element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the table, the type of this element will be automatically set as the <strong>Element Type</strong> box value.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Fill the table with elements:<br /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the scope, from which the elements should be added to the table. Element types must correspond to the selected element types!</li><li>Make sure at least one element type is specified for the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Create new elements directly from the table. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Filling+the+table+with+elements#Fillingthetablewithelements-Creatingnewelements">How to craete new elements &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>Copy and paste elements from another resource. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Filling+the+table+with+elements#Fillingthetablewithelements-Copyingandpastingelementsfromanotherresources">How to paste elements &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><p>Add existing elements to the table individually. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Filling+the+table+with+elements#Fillingthetablewithelements-Addingexistingelementsfromthemodel">How to add existing elements &gt;&gt;</a></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="690adcd6-91da-4c9f-b1dc-44841cbda6d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">creating_with_wizard</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a Generic Table using the diagram creation wizard</h3><p>Once created the table is already filled with data.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Generic Table using the diagram creation wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Generic Table Wizard</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, click<strong> Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Generic Table Wizard</strong>.</li><li>On the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, click <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Type the name of the table and select the owner for it.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Generic table creation wizard. Selecting elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generic_table_wizard_select_elements.png" /></ac:image> <br /><br />Select elements from the model tree and add them into the <strong>Selected</strong> list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5160217-5245-4a05-a4b3-86a3337ab483"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Though you can select any element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, only the elements of selected types will be added into the Generic Table.<br />If the Generic Table creation wizard is opened from one or more elements’ shortcut menu, the element or the set of elements will be added to the <strong>Selected</strong> list automatically.</p><p>For more information about the element selection and dialog buttons, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Elements multiple selection" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Specify the set of columns to show on the table.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Generic table creation wizard. Selecting columns" ac:alt="Generic table creation wizard. Selecting columns"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generic_table_wizard_selecting_columns.png" /></ac:image> <br /><p>The list of all available properties corresponding to selected element types is presented in the dialog. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565171 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 04967: Creating Impact Matrix

- page_id: `249565171`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565171/Creating+Impact+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:33.969+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Impact Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Impact Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Strategic Element, Operational Element, Resource Element, and Service Element).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Strategic Element).
3. Click the Refresh button.

The Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements mapps to Strategic Elements using the ImpactedBy relationship.

ImpactedBy relationship is available between the following element pairs:

- Actual Outcome and Enterprise Goal
- Capability and Opportunity
- Actual Strategic Phase and Challenge
- Operational Architecture/Resource Architecture/Service Architecture and Challenge
- Operational Activity and Challenge

To map the elements

- Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

To change the matrix axes

- In the matric toolbar, click Change Axes .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Impact Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Strategic Element, Operational Element, Resource Element, and Service Element).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Strategic Element).</li><li>Click the Refresh button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements mapps to Strategic Elements using the ImpactedBy relationship.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="113ee3cb-384b-4589-abc4-43272d65dfbd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>ImpactedBy relationship is available between the following element pairs:</p><ul><li>Actual Outcome and Enterprise Goal</li><li>Capability and Opportunity</li><li>Actual Strategic Phase and Challenge</li><li>Operational Architecture/Resource Architecture/Service Architecture and Challenge</li><li>Operational Activity and Challenge</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To map the elements</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the matrix axes</p><hr /><ul><li>In the matric toolbar, click<span> </span><strong>Change Axes</strong>.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565176 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04968: Creating Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `249565176`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565176/Creating+Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-27T09:30:10.892+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Implementation Matrix
- labels: ['create-implementation-matrix', 'build-implementation-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Implementation Matrix describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elementsdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].

To create an Implementation Matrix

1. Specify the Rows Scope . The available row scopes:
  - Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.
  - Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.
  - Service Elements: Service Interface.
  - Summary & Overview Elements: Architecture.
2. Specify the Columns Scope . The available column scoper:
  - Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.
  - Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.
  - Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.
  - Resource Elements: Resource Connector.
3. Click the Refresh button.

The Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements map to Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elements using the Implements relation.

You can swap the rows and columns by changing the axes in the matrix.

To change the matrix axes

- In the matric toolbar, click Change Axes .

To map the elements

1. Double-click an empty intersection between the desired elements.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

The Implements relationship is available between the following element pairs:

- Architecture and Actual Strategic Phase.
- Function and Actual Strategic Phase.
- Function and Operational Activity or Service Function.
- Operational Activity and Actual Strategic Phase.
- Operational Information and Strategic Information.
- Resource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource Connector.
- Resource Exchange and Operational Exchange
- Resource Information and Operational Information.
- Resource Interface and Operational Interface or Service Interface.
- Resource Performer and Operational Agent.
- Resource Role and Operational Role.
- Resource Service and Service.
- Service Function and Operational Activity.
- Service Interface and Operational Interface.

You can choose which element pairs to show or hide in the Implementation matrix.

To show/hide element pairs in the matrix

1. In the matrix toolbar, click Show Element Pairs .
2. Click to select or clear the selection of the element pairs.

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Implementation Matrix <span>describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary &amp; Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elements </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span></p><p>To create an Implementation Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3ca68d3b-bbde-4b44-a87a-ec4be9f39c3b">Specify the <strong>Rows Scope</strong>. The available row scopes:<ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service Elements: Service Interface.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Summary &amp; Overview Elements: Architecture. </span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="9a29b13b-2c91-4a54-8fea-afcd6083f3c5">Specify the <strong>Columns Scope</strong>. &amp; Overview Elements</span> map to <span>Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elements</span> using the Implements relation.</p><p>You can swap the rows and columns by changing the axes in the matrix. </p><p><br /></p><p>To change the matrix axes</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="899697c9-1165-443b-92aa-79ea361ad3af">In the matric toolbar, click <strong>Change elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="76ef88a7-d49c-4ab1-adb2-22ecd6284069">Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>The Implements relationship is available between the following element pairs:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3444ce73-961f-4f2d-8208-87d88c9d85df">Architecture and Actual Strategic Phase.</li><li data-uuid="948f17f3-2dd1-4b3b-9492-f817cf22b205"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Function and Actual Strategic Phase.</span></li><li data-uuid="e074149e-959f-461e-be11-cec7ef18eb20"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Function and Operational Activity or Service Function.</span></li><li data-uuid="6c13fc0f-8ff5-4dea-9917-3244357d92ef">Operational Activity and Actual Strategic Phase.</li><li data-uuid="257ac58d-117d-4b6e-be18-eeb2c656cac7">Operational Information and Strategic Information.</li><li data-uuid="2a259494-6f20-4efe-b600-4c0cdbcc5a5a">Resource Connector and Operational Connector or Resource Connector.</li><li data-uuid="f6094292-30ba-4632-b516-9ee908c4bc78">Resource Exchange and Operational Exchange</li><li data-uuid="13e9ecff-4388-4faf-8938-4f1958c256d4">Resource Information and Operational Information.</li><li data-uuid="86b35f96-52b9-4b51-bc93-63e6a72cbbf6">Resource Interface and Operational Interface or Service Interface.</li><li data-uuid="1d8196fb-1468-4933-ad3c-308597b15408">Resource Performer and Operational Agent.</li><li data-uuid="1431bf22-6bc1-4cce-9c6f-c1e469120255">Resource Role and Operational Role.</li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968220 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 04969: Creating inner element as property value

- page_id: `243968220`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968220/Creating+inner+element+as+property+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.466+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Cases of advanced editing
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some properties whose values can be their inner elements. Specifying such property value requires to create a new element. The element, which has the property with the inner element assigned as its value, becomes the owner of this inner element. 
Examples of properties whose values are their inner elements: *Owned Comment*, *Owned Attribute*.

To create an inner element as a property value

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Add button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. Select an element you want to create. The Specification window of the created element opens.
4. Specify properties of the new inner element.
5. When you are done, do one of the following:

- Close the Specification window.
- Click **Back**to return to the Specification window of the owning element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To remove an inner element from a property value list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Select the value you want to remove.
3. Click the Remove button.

The element is removed from both the property value list and the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
