<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561412 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04970: Creating Interface Control Document tables

- page_id: `249561412`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561412/Creating+Interface+Control+Document+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:29.616+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interface Control Document (ICD) table describes interfaces between systems or/and subsystems. That helps to ensure a compatibility between system components.The ICD tables can describe the following:

- The inputs and outputs of a single system.
- The interface between two systems or subsystems.
- The complete interface protocol from the lowest physical elements (e.g., the mating plugs, the electrical signal voltage levels) to the highest logical levels (e.g., the level 7 application layer of the OSI model), or some subset thereof.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Blackbox ICD table of the Distiller Block.

The views for system components interface are generated automatically from the system model. This provides up to date, efficiently managed, and fully featured model-based ICDs. The true model-based document-free approach is supported, along with all the capabilities of table export to document (images, *html*, *.xls*, *.csv*files).You can create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space=''] and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space=''] directly from the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

##### Creating Blackbox ICD Table

To create a Blackbox ICD Table

1. In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] which ports/ interfaces you want to represent in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N . Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - Right-click the Block and select Create Diagram > Blackbox ICD Table .
  - Open the Specification window of the Block, select the Ports/Interfaces property group and click the Create ICD Table button. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_icd_table_button_in_block_specification_window.png'] The Blackbox ICD Table is created. All external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block are represented in the table automatically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space='']>]]>
3. Type a table name and press Enter.

##### Creating Whitebox ICD Table

To create a Whitebox ICD Table

1. In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] which assembly you want to represent in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N . Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - Right-click the Block and select **Create Diagram** > **Whitebox ICD Table**. 
The Whitebox ICD Table is created. All Parts, their Ports/interfaces and flows are represented in the table automatically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type a table name and press Enter.

##### Customizing the representation of the ICD table

You can customize the representation of the ICD table by using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An Interface Control Document (ICD) table describes interfaces between systems or/and <span class="mw-redirect">subsystems</span>. That helps to ensure a compatibility between system components. </span>The ICD tables can describe the following:</p><ul style="margin-left: 1.5em;"><li data-uuid="f939d08f-4bbd-4ef2-806e-004e30316cea"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The inputs and outputs of a single system.</span></li><li data-uuid="24778ff6-6b72-4df6-a147-f39fe5e6b829"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The interface between two systems or subsystems.</span></li><li data-uuid="ad97c700-17e9-4861-be97-a0829eb21c82"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The complete interface protocol from the lowest physical elements (e.g., the mating plugs, the electrical signal voltage levels) to the highest logical levels (e.g., the level 7 application layer of the OSI model), or some subset thereof.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="distiller_blackbox_icd_table.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Blackbox ICD table of the Distiller Block.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The views for system components interface are generated automatically from the system model. This provides up to date, efficiently managed, and fully featured model-based ICDs. The true model-based document-free approach is supported, along with all the capabilities of table export to document (images, <em>html</em>, <em>.xls</em>, <em>.csv </em>files). </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Blackbox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Balckbox ICD table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and/or</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Whitebox ICD table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">directly from the</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link>. </span></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Blackbox ICD Table</h3><p>To create a Blackbox ICD Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="50b8745f-c9b0-401f-a4ed-4ebd238f33b8">In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> which ports/ interfaces you want to represent in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Blackbox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Blackbox ICD Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="320da7df-e51e-46d5-a803-5ced6071240b">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “bla” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Type “bla” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+N</strong>. Type “bla” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the Block and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Blackbox ICD Table</strong>.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the Block, select the <strong>Ports/Interfaces</strong> property group and click the <strong>Create ICD Table</strong> button.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Create ICD Table button in the Specification window" ac:alt="Create ICD Table button in the Specification window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_icd_table_button_in_block_specification_window.png" /></ac:image><br />The Blackbox ICD Table is created. All external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block are represented in the table automatically. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Blackbox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Blackbox ICD Table >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="f4ae90b6-c8eb-47d7-b6e7-5239ad4f9698"><p>Type a table name and press Enter.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Whitebox ICD Table</h3><p>To create a Whitebox ICD Table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c6f1d5c9-fe3b-4cb0-8653-59e7b9da94cf">In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> which assembly you want to represent in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="90fb1069-bd87-4555-8bd8-282c9ab1a5ff">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “wh” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Type “wh” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>Press <strong>Ctrl+N</strong>. Type “wh” and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the Block and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Whitebox ICD Table</strong>.<br />The Whitebox ICD Table is created. All Parts, their Ports/interfaces and flows are represented in the table automatically. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Whitebox ICD Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Whitebox ICD Table >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="whitebox_icd_table.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="28307510-c74d-4118-af41-27d6b67f7769"><p>Type a table name and press Enter.</p></li></ol><h3>Customizing the representation of the ICD table</h3><p>You can customize the representation of the ICD table by using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561095 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 04971: Creating Item Flow

- page_id: `249561095`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561095/Creating+Item+Flow
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-26T12:39:33.064+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Item Flows

The following procedure showcases how to create Item Flows whether you are working with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space=''] creation mode.

To create an Item Flow

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Connector or an Association (in Direct mode) or an Association, Connector, Message, Control Flow, or Object Flow (in Between Part Types mode) on the diagram pane, and on its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_item_flow.png'] .
  - Drag the classifier you want to use as the Conveyed Item onto the intended Connector.
2. In the Create/Edit Item Flow dialog, Create new or Select Existing Item Flow step, specify the following options:
  - **Item Flow**. From the drop-down menu, select an existing Item Flow or create a new one by selecting <NEW>. If you select an existing Item Flow, you don't need to specify the rest of the options in this dialog.
  - **Direction**. From the drop-down menu, select the direction of the new Item Flow. If you are creating the Item Flow on the directed relationship, the **Direction** is set automatically and is not available for editing.If you are creating the Item Flow between an Activity Parameter Node and an Action, the**Direction**field displays suggested sender/receiver elements based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated.
  - **Conveyed Items**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Conveyed Classifier** dialog, select a classifier to be used as the Conveyed Classifier. Click **OK**. If you have invoked the **Create/Edit Item Flow** dialog by dragging a classifier onto the Connector and do not want to change it, you may skip this option.
  - (Optional) **Item Property**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Item Property** dialog, select a Part Property to use as the Item Property. Click **OK**. This option is available when creating an Item Flow in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].
  - (Optional) **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**. Select the check box to synchronize the Item Flow for Associations, Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages with appropriate Item Flow ends. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Edit Item Flow Realization between Activities**step, review the activities.
  - If the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**option is selected and multiple Activity Pairs are detected, remove the unnecessary pairs via the **Remove From Table**button. This step is available only in the Between Part Types creation mode. However, this step is not available if you are creating the Item Flow between Part Properties with no types set. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click**Review or update affected diagrams** to see the list of diagrams where the selected Item Flow can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified flow to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Item Flow to be displayed. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] **Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. By default, the option is set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click **Finish**. An Item Flow with the selected Conveyed Item (and Item Property if it was defined) is created and is set for the Connector and the corresponding relationships if the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**was selected.

If you want to reverse the Item Flow direction and update it in all related diagrams, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space=''].

##### Specifying the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

You can also specify a Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane if you have already created an Item Flow.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item onto the Item Flow on the diagram pane. The Create/Edit Item Flow dialog opens. If the Item Flow already has Conveyed Item(s), the Conveyed Items field is appended with the new item.
3. Follow the steps of the dialog. See the procedure steps 2-4.
4. Click Finish when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='set_as_conveyed_item.png']

##### Showing/hiding the Conveyed Item

You can choose to show or hide the Conveyed Item on the element realizing the Item Flow (Association, Connector, Activity Edge, Message).

To show/hide the Conveyed Item

1. On the diagram pane, select the element realizing an Item Flow.
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''] :
  1. If the Conveyed Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Show Realized Item Flows . Click it to show the Conveyed Item.
  2. If the Conveyed Item is displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Hide_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Hide Realized Item Flows . Click it to hide the Conveyed Item. If the element realizes only one Item Flow , the Conveyed Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Item Flows, the Conveyed Items menu opens. In it, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the **Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the **Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Realizing Item Flows

You can realize hidden Item Flows in two ways:

- Through the Item Flow Manager.
- From the selected relationship's Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To realize Item Flows through the Item Flow Manager dialog

1. Select an Association, Connector,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Item Flow Manager dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_realize.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Set the Realize column option to true for those Item Flows you want to realize.
  - Click the Realize All button, if you want to realize all Item Flows.
4. Click Close when you are finished.

To realize Item Flows from the Smart Manipulator toolbar

1. Select Association, Connector, Association,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. From the Smart Manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='realize_all.png'] Realize All Item Flows.

##### Removing not realized Item Flows

If you remove the realizing elements from the model, the no longer used Item Flows are left as a residue. However, you can specify the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows** project option to remove ItemFlows automatically after the last element that realizes them is deleted.

To specify the Remove Not Realized Item Flows project option

1. In the Main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General tab, select SysML .
3. Set the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows**option to *true*. The default value is *false*.

To find all not realized Item Flows, you can run the 'SysML ValSuite - Ports and Flows'validation suite. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Not Realized Item Flow' space='VR']** validation rule will check for any residual Item Flows. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

##### Navigating to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

To navigate to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

- Right-click the Item Flow element and in its shortcut menu, select Go To , then Usage in Diagrams , then Realizing Elements , then select the realizing element type. In its submenu, select a diagram in the displayed list.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating Item Flows</h3><p>The following procedure showcases how to create Item Flows whether you are working with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Item Flow management" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Direct or the Between Part Types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> creation mode.</p><p>To create an Item Flow </p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select a Connector or an Association (in Direct mode) or an Association, Connector, Message, Control Flow, or Object Flow (in Between Part Types mode) on the diagram pane, and on its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_item_flow.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Drag the classifier you want to use as the Conveyed Item onto the intended Connector.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Create/Edit Item Flow</strong> dialog, <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create new or Select Existing Item Flow</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> step, </span>specify the following options:<br /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Item Flow</strong>. From the drop-down menu, select an existing Item Flow or create a new one by selecting &lt;NEW&gt;.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d4579d6-5317-4dca-ac1f-fb1eb222c093"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select an existing Item Flow, you don't need to specify the rest of the options in this dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Direction</strong>. From the drop-down menu, select the direction of the new Item Flow.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6abc8f1-f405-42ca-8754-c4746a9c9522"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you are creating the Item Flow on the directed relationship, the <strong>Direction</strong> is set automatically and is not available for editing.</li><li><span>If you are creating the Item Flow between an Activity Parameter Node and an Action, the </span><strong><span class="">Direction </span></strong><span class="">field displays suggested sender/receiver elements based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Conveyed Items</strong>. Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> From the <strong>Select Conveyed Classifier</strong> dialog, select a classifier to be used as the Conveyed Classifier. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8d7cfa-0932-47a3-a500-31dbf799ac72"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have invoked the <strong>Create/Edit Item Flow</strong> dialog by dragging a classifier onto the Connector and do not want to change it, you may skip this option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>(Optional) <strong>Item Property</strong>. Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a178fdf-5a9e-4820-bb5e-b7c81a6c6aa8">From the </ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a178fdf-5a9e-4820-bb5e-b7c81a6c6aa8">Select Item Property</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a178fdf-5a9e-4820-bb5e-b7c81a6c6aa8"> dialog, select a Part Property to use as the Item Property. Click </ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a178fdf-5a9e-4820-bb5e-b7c81a6c6aa8">OK</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a178fdf-5a9e-4820-bb5e-b7c81a6c6aa8">. <br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a111cbf6-6dc7-48c1-bb72-ef57b97d92ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is available when creating an Item Flow in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>(Optional) <strong>Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships</strong>. Select the check box to synchronize the Item Flow for Associations, Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages with appropriate Item Flow ends. <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_Item_Flows_1.png" /></ac:image></span></p></li></ul></li><li><p>In the <strong>Edit Item Flow Realization between Activities </strong>step, review the activities. </p><ul><li><p>If the <strong>Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships </strong>option is selected and multiple Activity Pairs are detected, remove the unnecessary pairs via the <strong>Remove From Table </strong>button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bff46ae-3ff2-4292-b9f3-06fe6c70472c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is available only in the Between Part Types creation mode. However, this step is not available if you are creating the Item Flow between Part Properties with no types set.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_Item_Flows_2.png" /></ac:image></span></p></li></ul></li><li><p><span>Click </span><strong><span>Review or update affected diagrams</span></strong> to see the list of diagrams where the selected Item Flow can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified flow to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Item Flow to be displayed. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17ea95c0-fc33-43df-b62b-9809f99acd14"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong>Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</strong> controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. By default, the option is set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Preview Affected Diagrams dialog" /></ac:link>.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_Item_Flows_3.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong>. An Item Flow with the selected Conveyed Item (and Item Property if it was defined) is created and is set for the Connector and the corresponding relationships if the <strong>Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships </strong>was selected.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d13fa5f5-7dfc-4e08-b899-febd3d68717b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to reverse the Item Flow direction and update it in all related diagrams, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Specifying the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</span></h3><p>You can also specify a Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane if you have already created an Item Flow.</p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item onto the Item Flow on the diagram pane. The <strong>Create/Edit Item Flow</strong> dialog opens. If the Item Flow already has Conveyed Item(s), the Conveyed Items field is appended with the new item.</li><li>Follow the steps of the dialog. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Item Flows" /> procedure steps 2-4.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish </strong>when you are done. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_as_conveyed_item.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Showing/hiding the Conveyed Item</h3><p>You can choose to show or hide the Conveyed Item on the element realizing the Item Flow (Association, Connector, Activity Edge, Message).</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12c70495-6cf3-4014-8e0f-351280f67916">To show/hide the Conveyed Item</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="67802118-2580-4304-8bc8-4afe81397337">select the element realizing an Item Flow.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ol><li>If the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1378b15-1423-474f-a109-43b24ff8f93b">Conveyed Item is not displayed</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the tooltip for the button is<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_Realized_Item_Flow.png" /></ac:image><strong>Show Realized Item Flows</strong>. Click it to show the Conveyed Item.</li><li>If the Conveyed Item<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9afaee50-8621-4ed1-a546-1885f349de7c"> is displayed</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the tooltip for the button is <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hide_Realized_Item_Flow.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Hide Realized Item Flows</strong>. Click it to hide the Conveyed Item.<br />If the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="76221ad9-f793-49a0-8cfb-c527c75f8dc9">element realizes only one Item Flow</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the Conveyed Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.</li></ol></li><li>If the element realizes multiple Item Flows, the Conveyed Items menu opens. In it, do the following:<ol><li>Select the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be displayed.</li><li><p>Clear the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be hidden.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efdf0cb5-5869-4fbe-9fa1-abaa8b869b62"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To select all check boxes, click the <strong>Select All </strong>button. To clear all check boxes, click the <strong>Clear All </strong>button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p>When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls the arrow visibility.</p><p><br /></p><p><span>To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the modeling tool main menu, choose <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagrams </strong>property group, find the <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing </strong>option and set it to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Realizing Item realize Item Flows through the Item Flow Manager dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an Association, <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">Connector,<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Control Flow</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, Object Flow, </span>or Message in the diagram pane.</li><li>On the smart manipulator, following:<ul><li>Set the <strong>Realize</strong> column option to true for those Item Flows you want to realize.</li><li>Click the <strong>Realize All</strong> button, if you want to realize all Flows</span></h3><p>If you remove the realizing elements from the model, the no longer used Item Flows are left as a residue. However, you can specify the <strong>Remove Not Realized Item Flows</strong> project option to remove Item<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> Flows automatically after the last element that realizes them is deleted.</span></p><p>To specify the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Remove Not Realized Item Flows project option</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Main toolbar, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General </strong>tab, select <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>Set the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Remove Not Realized Item Flows </strong>option to <em>true</em>. The default value is <em>false</em>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To find all not realized Item Flows, you can run the 'SysML ValSuite - Ports and Flows' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validation suite. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563469 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 04972: Creating L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

- page_id: `249563469`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563469/Creating+L1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:56.213+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-high-level-operational-concept-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.

To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">An L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the L1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram.<br /></span></p><p>To create an L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 04973: Creating L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `249563467`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563467/Creating+L1+Node+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:56.156+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-l1-node-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L1 Node Types diagram

1. Create Business Architectures or Nodes.
2. Connect Nodes using Node Associations.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L1 Node Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Business Architectures or Nodes.</li><li>Connect Nodes using Node Associations.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563478 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04974: Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram

- page_id: `249563478`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563478/Creating+L2+Logical+Scenario+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:18.137+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Scenario
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an L2 Logical Scenario diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the L2 package, and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**L2 Logical Scenario**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **L2 Logical Scenario,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the L2 package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='L2_drag_operational_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an L2 Logical Scenario diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>In the Containment Tree, select the L2 package, and d</span>o one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> L2 Logical Scenario</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="187" ac:width="307"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>L2 Logical Scenario, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span>. An example is described using the Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="199" ac:width="477"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_operational_performer.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the L2 package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="255" ac:width="300"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L2_new_operational_performer_containment.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d13e10d-1e94-4838-bbd0-5cc2c6d0a234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the <strong>Operational Association</strong> also.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="887" ac:width="727"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.compartments.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.attributes.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd4c464-ed89-4754-b3b4-2f0c0451e567"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="773" ac:width="747"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related_nested.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563498 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04975: Creating L2i diagram

- page_id: `249563498`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563498/Creating+L2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:56.715+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L2 Logical Internal Scenario
- labels: ['create-l2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L2i diagram

1. Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.
2. Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L2i diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74e40a9f-bcea-41a4-8f08-0d730ceaa168">Model internal structures of created context elements using Node Roles typed by Nodes.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Associate Node Roles using Operational Connectors.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563506 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04976: Creating L3 Node Interactions table

- page_id: `249563506`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563506/Creating+L3+Node+Interactions+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:12.702+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L3 Node Interactions
- labels: ['create-l3-node-interactions-table', 'add-operational-exchange-in-l3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.

newTo add new Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent , Receiving Operational Agent , and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an L1 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new" /> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing" /> Operational Exchange to fill in a L3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the L3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, Receiving Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link">L1</span> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563512 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04977: Creating L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `249563512`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563512/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:57.203+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram' space='']Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram should be created first.

To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9ff2594-a9e8-4506-b6f9-b31d12bef112"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Logical Activities diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Logical Activities Taxonomy</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563514 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04978: Creating L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249563514`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563514/Creating+L4+Logical+Activities+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:57.301+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-l4-logical-activities-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or reuse Nodes from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space=''] view (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or reuse Nodes from <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">view </span>(recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Nodes and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563747 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04979: Creating L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `249563747`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563747/Creating+L4-P4+Systems+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:22.924+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'build-l4-p4-systems-function-to-operational-activity-traceability-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) addresses the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.

To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

1. Click the intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (L4-P4) <span>addresses the direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) linkage between Functions described in P4 and Operational Activities specified in L4.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a L4-P4 Systems Function to Operational Activity Traceability matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563529 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04980: Creating L7 diagram

- page_id: `249563529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563529/Creating+L7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:57.832+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L7 Logical Data Model
- labels: ['create-l7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an L7 diagram

1. Create Information Elements or Entity Items.
2. Add Properties.
3. Create Association.

Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an L7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Information Elements or Entity Items.</li><li>Add Properties.</li><li>Create Association.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563534 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04981: Creating L8 table

- page_id: `249563534`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563534/Creating+L8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:57.943+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: ['create-l8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991427608

1991427628

1991427618

To create an L8 table, do either

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained Operational Element (Node, Operational Activity, Entity Item, Information Element, and Operational Exchange). Edit the Rule Specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

1991427607

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Parametric' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967370 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04982: Creating layout template

- page_id: `243967370`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967370/Creating+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:29.683+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you create a Composite Structure Diagram and finish perfecting its layout and looks, you can reuse the same layout in other diagrams of your model by creating layout templates. Create layout templates directly from Composite Structure Diagrams as described below.

To create a layout template

1. Create a Composite Structure Diagram with Part Properties and their Ports or open an existing one.
2. Right click the Part Property the the layout of which you want to reuse and select **Display** > **Layout Template** > **Create Template**.

A layout template is created as the Class or Composite Structure Diagram depending on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout template creation mode' space=''] (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a layout template from the SysML Internal Block Diagram (Composite Structure Diagram in a UML project).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">When you create a Composite Structure Diagram and finish perfecting its layout and looks, you can reuse the same layout in other diagrams of your model by creating layout templates. Create layout templates directly from Composite Structure Diagrams as described below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To create a layout template</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Create a Composite Structure Diagram with Part Properties and their Ports or open an existing one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Right click the Part Property the the layout of which you want to reuse and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Template</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">A layout template is created as the Class or Composite Structure Diagram depending on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Layout template creation mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout template creation mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (see the figure below).</span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_layout_template.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a layout template from the SysML Internal Block Diagram (Composite Structure Diagram in a UML project).</h6>
````

## PAGE 04983: Creating Legends

- page_id: `243967163`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967163/Creating+Legends
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:16:56.338+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1294102865

1294102867

1294102864

**On this page**

**4**

1294102866

You can create a Legend right in a diagram or table where you want to use it. After creating a Legend, you also have to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']. If you want Legend Items to be created and specified for you, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space=''].

##### Creating Legends in a symbol diagram

When creating a Legend in a symbol diagram, you can use either the diagram palette, or the diagram tool bar, as described below.

To create a Legend in a symbol diagram

1. Open the diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Under the Common group of the diagram palette, click Legend , and click an empty space on the diagram pane.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the d iagram toolbar and select Create Legend .

After creating a Legend, use the smart manipulator on the Legend shape to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Legend shape with specified Legend Items in a Requirement Diagram.

##### Creating Legends in a table

When working with a table, you can create a Legend by using the table toolbar, as described below.

To create a Legend in a table

1. Open the table in which you want to create a Legend.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the table toolbar and select Create Legend .

In a table, a Legend is displayed as a bar right above the table. After creating a Legend in a table, the Specification window of the newly created Legend opens allowing you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Legend in a table with specified Legend Items.

1267814660

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the *Legends* sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='legends.mdzip'].*
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1 directory.

36764562

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967213 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 04984: Creating Legends automatically

- page_id: `243967213`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967213/Creating+Legends+automatically
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:16:34.408+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1287060642

1287060644

143669993

4

1287060643

You can create Legends automatically by extracting them from model elements. Additionally, you can automatically add new Legend items directly from the smart manipulator of the Legend already displayed on your diagram.

##### Extracting Legends

You can create a Legend with the selected Legend items automatically. Legends can be extracted either from or the .

Extracting from tagsTo extract Legends automatically from the applied stereotype tags typed by Boolean or Enumeration

1. Do one of the following:
  - in an open diagram, right-click the element shape and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the Model Browser, right-click the actual element and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the open table, right-click the row representing the actual element and select [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends From Row Type .
2. In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legend representing the stereotype tag to generate a Legend from. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

Extracting from element symbols

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends .
3. Extract Legends dialog In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legends you want to create automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_legends_from_elements.png']
4. Click Extract .

##### Extracting Legend items

You can add the selected Legend items automatically from the smart manipulator of the Legend shape displayed on the diagram pane.

To extract the selected Legend items automatically

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend shape, click Auto-create Legend items .
3. In the open **Auto-create Legend Items** dialog, select the specific Legend item(s) to extract. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click Extract .

The selected Legend items are automatically displayed in the Legend shape, and diagram symbols are adorned accordingly.

When extracting Legends or Legend items, you can specify the following options:

- Select Create Adornments to automatically set a Fill Color for element shapes and Pen Color for paths.
- Select Connectors by Ports to automatically create Legends for Connectors depending on the selected Ports Legend(s) ( Stereotypes of Port Types , Stereotypes of Ports , and/or Port Types ).

##### Legend extraction mechanism

Legends listed in the **** dialog are generated automatically according to the types of elements displayed as symbols on the diagram pane.

| Legend | Legend items |
| --- | --- |
| Element Types | element types (metaclasses and stereotypes) displayed on a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Port Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Part Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram |
| Stereotypes of Ports | stereotypes of ports displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Parts | stereotypes of parts displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Connectors | stereotypes of connectors displayed in a diagram. |
| Port Types | classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Part Types | classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Connector | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed connectors in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Activity Edge | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed activity edges in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Association | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed associations in a diagram. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by enumeration | enumeration literals owned by the applied stereotype tag type. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by boolean | true and false values. |

##### **Example**

Let's say that you want to extract the Legend identifying different **Risk** property values of Requirements in your model. To do this, complete the following steps:

1. Open the Requirement Diagram in which you want to create the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values.
2. Right-click the shape of any Requirement and select Tools > Extract Legends OR in the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Extract Legends .
3. In the open dialog, select the Risk Legend, as shown in the figure below.
4. Click OK .

The *Risk* Legend is automatically displayed and applied to the Requirements Diagram. The name of the element tag is used as the Legend name, and every possible **Risk** property value creates an individual Legend Item.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The process of creating the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values in a Requirement Diagram.

579958103

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the *Legends* sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='legends.mdzip'].*
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1 directory.

1287060641
