  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="755c22cc-aaad-4852-880a-b57c2d316784"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1287060642</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="823ecfc1-02f2-4a3a-bb09-6d1f77ec80c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1287060644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b57299b-9606-4ce9-a30e-9c005c424cd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">143669993</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db69c66f-b072-40b0-a432-34b9b2da00da"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ee0ae48-c6a8-4ac4-aff7-3352c04534f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1287060643</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b46abe73-045f-4d12-86e6-14131bd35739">You can create Legen</ac:inline-comment-marker>ds automatically by extracting them from model elements. Additionally, you can automatically add new Legend items directly from the smart manipulator of the Legend already displayed on your diagram. </p><h3>Extracting Legends</h3><p>You can create a Legend with the selected Legend items automatically. Legends can be extracted either from <ac:link ac:anchor="Extracting from tags"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype tags]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or the <ac:link ac:anchor="Extracting from element symbols"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elements displayed on the diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4ce9a42-dd6c-4543-80d3-860b9c5ab51d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Extracting from tags</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To extract Legends automatically from the applied stereotype tags typed by Boolean or Enumeration</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">in an open diagram, right-click the element shape and select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">Tools</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">Extract Legends</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"> in the shortcut menu.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">in the Model Browser, right-click the actual element and select </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">Tools</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">Extract Legends</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c"> in the shortcut menu.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">in the open table, right-click the row representing the actual element and select </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png" /></ac:image><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">Extract Legends From Row Type</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li><li>In the open <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog, select the Legend representing the stereotype tag to generate a Legend from. </span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_legends_from_tags.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></p><div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6e859cb-26bb-4567-85ef-6f9e352559bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Extracting from element symbols</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To extract Legends automatically from the elements displayed on a diagram</div><div><hr /></div><div><ol><li>Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png" /></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18272490-f32d-4d72-977e-a3bfd43ad3c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Extract Legends dialog</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the open </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog, select the Legends you want to create <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de714e0b-2969-4ef9-894b-1d81554f8f85">automatically</ac:inline-comment-marker>. </span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_legends_from_elements.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Extract</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Extracting Legend items</h3><p>You can add the selected Legend items automatically from the smart manipulator of the Legend shape displayed on the diagram pane. </p><p><br /></p><p>To extract the selected Legend items automatically</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.</li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend shape, click <strong>Auto-create Legend items</strong>. </li><li><p>In the open <strong>Auto-create Legend Items</strong> dialog, select the specific Legend item(s) to extract.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="auto_create_legend_items.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<strong> Extract</strong>.</li></ol><p>The selected Legend items are automatically displayed in the Legend shape, and diagram symbols are adorned accordingly.</p><p><br /></p><p>When extracting Legends or Legend items, you can specify the following options:</p><ul><li>Select <strong>Create Adornments</strong> to automatically set a Fill Color for element shapes and Pen Color for paths.</li><li>Select <strong>Connectors by Ports</strong> to automatically create Legends for Connectors depending on the selected Ports Legend(s) (<strong>Stereotypes of Port Types</strong>, <strong>Stereotypes of Ports</strong>, and/or<strong> Port Types</strong>).</li></ul></div><h3>Legend extraction mechanism</h3><p>Legends listed in the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Extract Legends dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Legends]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog are generated automatically according to the types of elements displayed as symbols on the diagram pane.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 70.6528%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 29.9922%;" /><col style="width: 70.0078%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Legend</th><th>Legend items</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Element Types</strong></td><td>element types (metaclasses and stereotypes) displayed on a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Stereotypes of Port Types</strong></td><td>stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Stereotypes of Part Types</strong></td><td>stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Ports</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of ports displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Parts</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of parts displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Connectors</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of connectors displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Port Types</strong></td><td colspan="1">classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Part Types</strong></td><td colspan="1">classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Connector</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed connectors in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Activity Edge</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed activity edges in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Association</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed associations in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Name of applied stereotype tag typed by enumeration</span></strong></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="da7d788e-009f-4d3d-a253-97511512b831"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53fd64ee-e50c-474b-ba90-9b0c1405b58a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">enumeration literals owned by the applied stereotype tag type. </span></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Name of applied stereotype tag typed by boolean</span></strong></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="14a2f36e-aed0-4789-abaa-6625ed8abf3e">true and false values</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>Example</strong></h3><p>Let's say that you want to extract the Legend identifying different <strong>Risk</strong> property values of Requirements in your model. To do this, complete the following steps:</p><ol><li>Open the Requirement Diagram in which you want to create the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values.</li><li>Right-click the shape of any Requirement and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Extract Legends </strong>OR in<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> the diagram toolbar, click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong>Extract Legends</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></li><li>In the open dialog, select the <strong>Risk</strong> Legend, as shown in the figure below.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The <em>Risk</em> Legend is automatically displayed and applied to the Requirements Diagram. The name of the element tag is used as the Legend name, and every possible <strong>Risk</strong> property value creates an individual Legend Item.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_legends_automatically.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The process of creating the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values in a Requirement Diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9ab7ecd-4572-4c4e-bdff-c89a00845e76"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">579958103</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <em>Legends</em> sample model that comes with SysML v1 plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Open the model from the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1 </em>directory.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1df00c6c-c046-4cf5-aed4-3c41e1ff9ea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1287060641</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link><br /><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and managing Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967285 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04985: Creating line jump

- page_id: `243967285`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967285/Creating+line+jump
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:27.314+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['line-jump', 'none-line-jump', 'horizontal-line-jump', 'vertical-line-jump', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.

To add line jumps

1. Right-click the diagram pane and select Diagram Properties .
2. In the Diagram Properties dialog, select the Add Line Jumps To property value from the drop-down list:
  - None - specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value). [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_none.gif']
  - Horizontal Line - specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_horizontal.gif']
  - Vertical Line**** - specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_vertical.gif']

The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.</p><p>To add line jumps</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane and select <strong>Diagram Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Add Line Jumps To</strong> property value from the drop-down list:<br /><ul><li><strong>None </strong>- specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value).<br /><ac:image ac:title="No line jumps" ac:alt="No line jumps"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_none.gif" /></ac:image></li><li><strong>Horizontal Line </strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Horizontal line jump" ac:alt="Horizontal line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_horizontal.gif" /></ac:image></li><li><strong>Vertical Line<strong> </strong></strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Vertical line jump" ac:alt="Vertical line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_vertical.gif" /></ac:image></li></ul><br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8304fe09-b127-4681-a347-cbead92601b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563544 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04986: Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart

- page_id: `249563544`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563544/Creating+Lr+Lines+of+Development.+Lr+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:14.540+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-lines-of-development', 'create-lr-gantt-chart', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create Lr Gantt chart, the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**opens.

To create an Lr Gantt chart from the wizard

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Follow the steps of the `****` [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the******[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development** check box.

To create an Lr diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Lr Package and select Create Diagram > Lr Lines of Development .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><code><strong> </strong></code></span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eba5ac24-51bf-4a20-88c8-afd68a3afc79"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the<strong> </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></strong></span> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Lr Lines of Development</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create an Lr diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Lr Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Lr Lines of Development</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank Lr Lines of Development chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5da39ff-0302-4822-b740-1df1ccaacb49"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563551 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04987: Creating Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

- page_id: `249563551`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563551/Creating+Lr+Programme+Portfolio+Relationships+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:58.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-portfolio-relationships', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='']before creating the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.

To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole) . Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) or Actual Posts ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='']) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.

You can:

- Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.
- Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix' space=''] to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3513b26-7930-47fd-873b-7bb6a849f085"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2 diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating <span>the Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">(part)</span> to another<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> (whole)</span>. Alternatively you can specify the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> whole and part </span>properties. <span> </span></li><li><p>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or Actual Posts (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[D2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them by using the Responsible For.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640a34e1-3325-41e0-8bbc-4d501ce74f88"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b87a6e49-5998-4084-9df0-12dd640e4b24">Nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lr Responsibility Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organization resources.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563546 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04988: Creating Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249563546`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563546/Creating+Lr+Programme+to+Capability+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:58.228+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-programme-to-capability-mapping', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Lr Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Lr Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563549 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04989: Creating Lr Responsibility Matrix

- page_id: `249563549`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563549/Creating+Lr+Responsibility+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:58.311+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development
- labels: ['create-lr-responsibility-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='D2 Deployed Resources' space='']D2 diagrambefore creating the Lr Responsibility Matrix.

The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.

The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To create the Lr Responsibilitymatrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b20a7230-c44c-4ae1-8fd6-2079374a1070"><ac:rich-text-body><p>It is recommended to create the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="D2 Deployed Resources" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">D2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>before creating the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility Matrix</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The Lr Responsibility Matrix is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in of this matrix represent Actual Organizational Resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To create the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Lr Responsibility </span>matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970842 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04990: Creating merged column horizontally

- page_id: `243970842`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970842/Creating+merged+column+horizontally
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:00.160+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Tableprop tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050300468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050300470

2050300467

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050300469

There are two methods that you can use to set column to merged column.

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(int number)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($Package.size())Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified string of number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns(“3”)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See **.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The number of cells will be increased by the value in mergeColumns.

For example:

**Template code #1:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |  |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Template code #2:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970847 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04991: Creating merged row vertically

- page_id: `243970847`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970847/Creating+merged+row+vertically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:00.257+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Tableprop tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050308208

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050308216

2050308206

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050308215

There are two methods that you can use to merge rows.

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(Int number)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified number.

For example:

**Template code**:

| #forrow($c in$sorter.sort($Class))$c.name$tableprop.mergeRows($c.ownedElement.size())#set($count=0)#foreach($attr in $c.ownedElement)#set($count = $count + 1) | $attr.name#if($count < $c.ownedElement.size()) |
| --- | --- |
| #end#if(false) | #end#end#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified String of number. 
 For example:

**Template code**:

| #set($a = $array.createArray())#set($void = $a.add("A"))#set($void = $a.add("B"))#set($void = $a.add("C")) |  |
| --- | --- |
| #forrow($c in $a)Name$tableprop.mergeRows("3") | $c#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

mergeRows merges only existing cells in the same column and does not increase the number of rows or cells.

For example:

**Template code:**

| #forrow($package in $Package)$tableprop.mergeRows("5")Package | $package.name#endrow |
| --- | --- |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbf39ba9-b922-440f-bee0-6aa8f6ecb6db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308208</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff575217-07b0-46d1-b0f8-40d8d7febab0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="558da54e-d0cb-4023-a93d-989d21da6e3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308206</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e3e2992-fd17-4931-bab7-a8445a7c060c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are two methods that you can use to merge rows.</p><h4><br /> <strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(Int number)</strong></h4><p>When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified number.</p><p>For example:</p><p><strong>Template code</strong>:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>#forrow($c in</p><p>$sorter.sort($Class))$c.name$tableprop.mergeRows($c.ownedElement.size())#set($count=0)</p><p>#foreach($attr in $c.ownedElement)#set($count = $count + 1)</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><br /></p><p>$attr.name#if($count &lt; $c.ownedElement.size())</p></div></td></tr><tr><td>#end#if(false)</td><td>#end#end#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="For Row Code As Merged Row.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(String stringNumber)</strong></h4><p>When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified String of number.<br /> For example:</p><p><strong>Template code</strong>:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td colspan="2"><p>#set($a = $array.createArray())</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;A&quot;))</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;B&quot;))</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;C&quot;))</p></td></tr><tr><td>#forrow($c in $a)<strong>Name</strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(&quot;3&quot;)</td><td>$c#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="For Row Code As Merged Row 2.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c444ea47-0ae5-4fa4-abf5-284ee624bf8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>mergeRows merges only existing cells in the same column and does not increase the number of rows or cells.</p><p>For example:</p><p><strong>Template code:</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p style="text-align: center;">#forrow($package in $Package)</p><p style="text-align: center;">$tableprop.mergeRows(&quot;5&quot;)Package</p></td><td>$package.name#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p>In case there are three packages, the output will be:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Template Code 1 (Merge Row - 3 Packages).png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563044 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04992: Creating MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563044`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563044/Creating+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:29.969+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires OV-2 to be completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Exchange flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Skills (DoDAF) or Competencies (MODAF) provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the Resource Internal Exchange Specification diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>You can proceed to create SV-4 view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563053 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04993: Creating MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563053`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563053/Creating+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:30.332+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a SV-1 before creating SV-2.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in SV-1.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the Systems Internal Communications Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563128 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04994: Creating NCV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563128`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563128/Creating+NCV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:17.534+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-1 Capability Vision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.

To create a NCV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in NAF architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li> Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the NCV-1, the NCV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563133 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04995: Creating NCV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563133`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563133/Creating+NCV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:17.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.

To create a NCV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property *environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-2 should be modeled just before the NCV-1 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property <em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563138 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04996: Creating NCV-3 diagram

- page_id: `249563138`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563138/Creating+NCV-3+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:17.806+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create NCV-3 diagram, **NCV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a NCV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . NCV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click **Finish** when you are done. If you do not wish to use the NCV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NCV-3”.Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the**Environment Options** dialog go to **UPDM** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a NCV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. In the open window, find and select NCV-3 Capability Phasing diagram.
3. In the NCV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create NCV-3 diagram, <strong>NCV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open window, find and select <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> diagram.</li><li>In the <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>NCV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cd4fb9a-6f91-484d-b3f4-aaeb57ff9f45"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the NCV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NCV-3”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UPDM</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-3 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>In the open window, find and select <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> diagram.</li><li>In the <strong>NCV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c0fb0c0-8239-4124-87b4-555bc057a73a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NCV-3 diagram is created.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563153 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04997: Creating NCV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249563153`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563153/Creating+NCV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:18.311+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NCV-4 is created just after the NCV-2 is completed.

To create a NCV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from NCV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NCV-4 is created just after the NCV-2 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NCV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from NCV-2 (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563162 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 04998: Creating NCV-5 table

- page_id: `249563162`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563162/Creating+NCV-5+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:03.996+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

924292202

924292214

924292200

**On this page**

924292204

##### Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table

The context of the NCV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 table are Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the NCV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the NCV-5 table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 table

To create an empty NCV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NATO Capability Viewpoint package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a NCV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

924353687

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='']

The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="daf04d76-506e-472b-8e0c-f8b209b4d3b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">924353687</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966471 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 04999: Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list

- page_id: `243966471`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966471/Creating+nested+parts+by+pasting+items+from+the+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:03.089+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1877349504

1877349516

1877349506

When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as nested parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and past it in the Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.

You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.

To create parts by pasting items from the list

1. Copy the list.
2. Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.
3. In the open menu, select Elements .
4. Select the Part.
5. In the question dialog click Yes if you need to create nested parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The nested parts are created according to the copied list

1877349493

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a9e04f7-2585-4840-aa4e-3a94608960ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349504</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20da9c8f-6e43-4b67-b7af-828690d96f5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349516</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e80bdf7c-1976-4096-ab28-fc1675757da2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as nested parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and past it in the Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.</p><p>You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create parts by pasting items from the list</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy the list.</li><li>Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.</li><li>In the open menu, select <strong>Elements</strong>.</li><li>Select the Part.</li><li>In the question dialog click <strong>Yes</strong> if you need to create nested parts.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_parts.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">The nested parts are created according to the copied list</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62806c11-b9ed-4f62-9aa4-2a079b61829b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969586 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05000: Creating new Constraint element

- page_id: `243969586`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969586/Creating+new+Constraint+element
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:31:42.781+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Examples of Constraint types

INLINE

Before creating a new Constraint element, you must decide the Constraint type to create. The Constraint type defines a target for which the validation rule will be evaluated.

There are three types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MED'] that can be evaluated:

- Classifier level Constraints . Constraints placed on the classes, datatypes and other [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space=''] of the model are evaluated on all the instances of these classifiers (i.e. those [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] that have the particular classifier set as their type). Constraints defined on some particular classifiers are evaluated on the instances of these particular classifiers when validating. Inheritance is taken into account; instances of the subclasses of the class are also validated.
- **Constraints on Metaclasses**. When a Constraint is placed on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MED'] (one of the classes in the UML Standard Profile > UML2 Metamodel), this Constraint is evaluated on all the model elements of that kind (e.g. if the Constraint is placed on the Actor metaclass, then this Constraint applies to all actor elements in the model). The following is an example of a rule (specified in OCL2.0) mandating that all actor names in the model must be capitalized. These Constraints are useful for specifying generic rules that must apply on all the model elements of a particular kind. text
- Constraints on Stereotypes . When a Constraint is placed on some [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MED'] , that Constraint applies to all the model elements that have these Stereotypes applied to them. These Constraints are useful when creating domain specific profiles. When adapting UML to some specific modeling domain, a Profile is usually created with extensions for that domain - Stereotypes, tags etc. The Constraints on these Stereotypes allow enforcing the rules of that domain.

#### NOTE: Recommendation

Recommendation

It is strongly recommend not to mix the Constraints from different metalevels into one validation suite. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>

#### TIP: Examples of Constraint types

Examples of Constraint types

The examples of each Constraint type can be found in the **model validation** sample model. You can find this sample at *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\model validation.mdzip*.

You can create a new Constraint for :

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space=''] , which is constrained – classes, datatypes, etc. (for classifier level Constraints). >]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] (for meta-classifier level Constraints). >]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MED'] located in a read-only profile. >]]>

classifierTo create Constraint for a classifier or Stereotype

1. Open the Specification window of a classifier or Stereotype. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. On the left side of the window, select the Constraints property group.
3. On the right side of the window, click the Create button and select Constraint .
4. Type its name. The new Constraint is created in the model.

metaclassTo create Constraint for a metaclass

1. Select a package where you want to store the rule.
2. Right-click this package, select Create Element > Constraint .
3. Type its name. 
The new Constraint is created in the model. If you create a new Constraint for a metaclass, you must specify the **Constrained Element** property in the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Constrained Element property' space='']>]]>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968412 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05001: Creating new elements

- page_id: `243968412`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968412/Creating+new+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:58.373+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402360606

402360608

402360607

You can create a new element directly in the element selection dialog. To create a new element, turn on the element creation mode. The Tree view is opened automatically, when the element creation mode is turned on.

To turn on the element creation mode in the element selection dialog, do one of the following

- Click the Creation Mode button.
- In the Tree tab, right-click the empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select Creation Mode .

To create a new element in the element selection dialog

1. In the Tree tab, turn the element creation mode on.
2. In the model tree, select the owner for a new element and do one of the following:
  - Click the Create button.
  - Right-click the selected element or empty space near the model tree and from the shortcut menu, select Create .
3. In the list of available elements, select the element you want to create.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of a new element opens. Specify desired element properties and click Close .

402360605

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='']

Specify desired element properties and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28683375-49fd-4ebe-9df1-752a0d44d1fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402360605</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bd718971-deef-46d9-b3e7-e55347160787"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969045 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05002: Creating new operations

- page_id: `243969045`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969045/Creating+new+operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:16.845+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325544223

1325544226

1325544225

To create a new operation

1. Click Create operation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_operation.png']
2. Click the appropriate icon on the right side of the dialog to select a new operation type (see descriptions in the following table).

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Operations group |  |
|  | Click to create a new . This icon is available only when specifying members of a union or exclude operation, input collection for a filter operation, scope and type parameters for the find operation. |
|  | Click to create a new . |
|  | Click to create a new . |
| Filter | Click to create a new filter operation and then specify both the input collection that you need to filter and the filter criteria. |
| Type Test | Click to specify a new type test operation as a condition for selected filter operation. In other cases, the icon is not available.The operation tests, whether the type of the element matches the given type or stereotype. If the types matches, it returns true, and if they not – false. You can also use this operation to check, if the element is an instance of the given classifier.For this operation, you must specify the following parameters:Element – a model element, which type you need to test.Type – a type, stereotype, or classifier for testing the element.Select the Include subtypes check box to include the inherited types, stereotypes, or classifiers of the selected Type parameter value in the test.Select the Include Custom Types check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types. |
|  | Click to create a new Property Test operation. This operation tests model elements to find out if they contain the specified property values. If the property values of a model element match with the ones you specified, the operation returns true, otherwise it returns false.To specify a Property Test operation, select the desired properties and specify their values in the Property Test specification area on the right side of the dialog.Additionally expand the operation node in the operation list on the left side of the dialog and, if needed, change the AND Operator property value. If AND Operator is set to true (by default), the operation returns true only for the elements that contain all of the specified property values. If AND Operator is set to false, the operation returns true for the elements that contain at least one of the specified property values. |
|  | Click to specify a new Nested Operation. Nested Operation is an operation, the body of which is a structured expression. It is inline equivalent to defining an executable operation in the model as an Opaque Behavior with the StructuredExpression language and then using it. Nested Operation can be used as an argument to higher order operation calls, such as Filter. In simple operation calls it is not available. |
| Union | Click to create a new union operation and then specify as many members for it as you need.You can select the Unique check box to have only one identical element count as a result.The option is especially important to return non-unique numeric values to execute mathematical operations (such as Sum) correctly. |
| Exclude | Click to create a new exclude operation and then specify both From and Excluded members for it. |
|  | Click to add a new . You can select either one of the built-in operations, which are stored in standard/system profiles of your project, or a custom operation. |
|  | Click to create a , which language can be one of the following:BeanShellGroovyJythonJavaScriptOCL 2.0It can also be a reference to a java class (Binary). |
| Values group |  |
|  | Click to select an element from the model. |
|  | Click to create a String value. |
|  | Click to create a Boolean value. |
|  | Click to create a Integer value. |
|  | Click to create a Null value. |
|  | Click to create a collection of values. |
| Other group |  |
|  | Click to add an operation executing a specified expression. Execute operation takes the supplied expression fragment and grafts it into the current expression tree for executing. Expression fragment is an XML string that can be dynamically fetched from any source, such as another tag. The unique value of this operation is that the specified expression can be calculated dynamically instead of being fixed. As a result, the calculation to be executed can depend on the . |
|  | Click to add an expression retrieving the contextual variable specified by name. Most common case is accessing the THIS variable, which stores the current . This is usually the starting point – argument for other operations – such as Simple Navigation or Metachain Navigation. |

1325551639
