This icon is available only when specifying members of a union or exclude operation, input collection for a filter operation, scope and type parameters for the find operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metachain_nav.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td style="text-align: left;">Click to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Metachain Navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[metachain navigation operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_find.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;">Click to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Find operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[find operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_filter.png" /></ac:image><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f56b0389-6eb0-404e-a7f4-812250c7a196"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Filter</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><p>Click to create a new filter operation and then specify both the input collection that you need to filter and the filter criteria.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_typetest.png" /></ac:image><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="780c39a5-1728-431d-a6b7-f188d1887a4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Type Test</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to specify a new type test operation as a condition for selected filter operation. In other cases, the icon is not available.</p><p>The operation tests, whether the type of the element matches the given type or stereotype. If the types matches, it returns <em>true</em>, and if they not – <em>false</em>. You can also use this operation to check, if the element is an instance of the given classifier.</p><p>For this operation, you must specify the following parameters:</p><ul><li>Element – a model element, which type you need to test.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type – a type, stereotype, or classifier for testing the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_type_test_updated.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc9d9f09-8357-4b51-841c-2ecc8feb4795"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Select the <strong>Include subtypes</strong> check box to include the inherited types, stereotypes, or classifiers of the selected Type parameter value in the test.</li></ul><ul><li>Select the <strong>Include Custom Types</strong> check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameters_of_type_test_operation.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="property_test.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to create a new Property Test operation. This operation tests model elements to find out if they <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3725ec1-8c54-4955-8d7d-065951d5158a">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> the specified property values. If the property values of a model element match with the ones you specified, the operation returns <em>true</em>, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c27f30f5-96f6-401b-b131-733e30d54180">otherwise it returns </ac:inline-comment-marker><em>false.</em></p><p>To specify a Property Test operation, select the desired properties and specify their values in the Property Test specification area on the right side of the dialog.</p><p>Additionally expand the operation node in the operation list on the left side of the dialog and, if needed, change the <strong>AND Operator</strong> property value. If <strong>AND Operator</strong> is set to <em>true</em> (by default), the operation returns <em>true</em> only for the elements that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ab9f398-0c0e-4c0e-8fec-055c6a853bee">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> all of the specified property values. <span>If </span><strong>AND Operator</strong><span> is set to </span><em>false</em><span>, the operation returns <em>true</em> for the elements that <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c3d2847-391b-47ee-89c4-6492f77cd0f1">contain</ac:inline-comment-marker> at least one of the specified property values.<br /></span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="property_test_operation_properties.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_nested_operation.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><p>Click to specify a new Nested Operation. Nested Operation is an operation, the body of which is a structured expression. It is inline equivalent to defining an executable operation in the model as an Opaque Behavior with the StructuredExpression language and then using it. Nested Operation can be used as an argument to higher order operation calls, such as Filter. In simple operation calls it is not available.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_union.png" /></ac:image><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1b676af-3d6d-4b4e-a397-abc1881eeb12"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Union</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to create a new union operation and then specify as many members for it as you need.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="members_of_union.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can select the <strong>Unique</strong> check box to have only one identical element count as a result.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="2022-02-25 14_55_38-Relation Criteria.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d5690b9-e0f3-4bda-99ce-0a3349591cce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The option is especially important to return non-unique numeric values to execute mathematical operations (such as Sum) correctly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_exclude.png" /></ac:image><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbac6489-d2fa-4f63-84af-8f10cd6a3093"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Exclude</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to create a new exclude operation and then specify both <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">From</strong> and <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Excluded</strong> members for it.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="members_of_exclude.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_op_from_model.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><p>Click to add a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Calling operations from the model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation from the model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can select either one of the built-in operations, which are stored in standard/system profiles of your project, or a custom operation.</p><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_script.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td style="text-align: left;"><p>Click to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating script operations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[new script operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which language can be one of the following:</p><ul><li>BeanShell</li><li>Groovy</li><li>Jython</li><li>JavaScript</li><li>OCL 2.0</li></ul><p>It can also be a reference to a java class (Binary).</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Values</strong> group</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_element.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td>Click to select an element from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_string.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td>Click to create a String value.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_boolean.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td>Click to create a Boolean value.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_integer.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to create a Integer value.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_null.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to create a Null value.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_collection.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to create a collection of values.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Other</strong> group</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_execute.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to add an operation executing a specified expression. Execute operation takes the supplied expression fragment and grafts it into the current expression tree for executing. Expression fragment is an XML string that can be dynamically fetched from any source, such as another tag. The unique value of this operation is that the specified expression can be calculated dynamically instead of being fixed. As a result, the calculation to be executed can depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;" colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_context_value.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to add an expression retrieving the contextual variable specified by name. Most common case is accessing the THIS variable, which stores the current <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This is usually the starting point – argument for other operations – such as Simple Navigation or Metachain Navigation.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3a7b510-9cb6-43d1-b937-91ad596c819a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325551639</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Metachain Navigation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Find operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966022 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05003: Creating new projects from templates

- page_id: `243966022`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966022/Creating+new+projects+from+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:26.073+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Creating and using project templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428991632

1428991635

1428991634

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

By default, the following templates are available in the **New Project** dialog: C++, CORBA IDL, Guide to UML Diagrams Project, Java, Metamodeling, RUP, Use Case modeling, and WAE.

A new project created from the template will contain specific structure, model elements, and stereotypes that were predefined in the template. As of version 17.0.3, when creating a new project from the template, you can select to clone the custom used projects that exist in the template.

To create a new project from template

1. Do one of the following: In all cases, the New Project dialog opens.
  - From the File menu, select New Project .
  - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
  - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. Expand the Other group and select the Project from Template icon.
3. Specify the new project name in the Name box.
4. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button to select the location in your file system to store the new project.
5. Select the template from the templates tree and click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following table provides descriptions of the **New****Project** dialog with the *Project**from**Template icon* selected under the **Other** category.

| Element name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Other | Category | Select the Other category to see the list of the available templates. |
| Project from Template | Icon | Select the Project from Template icon to create a new project from a template. |
| Name | Text box | Type the name of the new project to create from a template. |
| Project location | Text box | Click the button to select a new project location. The Select Location dialog opens. |
| Create directory for project and related data | Check box | Click the check box to create a new folder for the new project. The folder name will be the same as the specified project name. |
| Clone custom used projects | Check box | Click to clone used projects from template project. In the new project from template, the copies of the used projects will be created. |
| Prefix to name used projects | Text box | Type the prefix for the name of each cloned used project. The default value is Cloned._ For example, if a used project of the template project is named Requirements, then the name of the cloned used project in the newly created project is Cloned_Requirements.The text box is available only if the Clone custom used projects check box is selected. |
| Select template | List | Select <modeling tool installation folder>\templates. |
| Template description | Text area | The predefined template description is displayed. Note that this field is not editable. |

You can also import the desired template into your project. Select **File** > **Import****From** > **Another Project** for this.

1428991631

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting projects as templates' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom project templates' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561437 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05004: Creating new Rollup Pattern Block

- page_id: `249561437`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561437/Creating+new+Rollup+Pattern+Block
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:40:11.682+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1348678326

1348678329

1348678328

You can apply the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space=''] or anewly created one by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']**Rollup Pattern** **Wizard**. If you want to apply different kinds of calculations than [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']default rollup patterns for your model, you can create a different type of rollup pattern Block. To do this, copy any default rollup pattern Block, paste it in the model, change its name, and specify all required value properties. The procedure below describes how to create a different kind of rollup pattern Block.

To define a different kind of rollup pattern (e.g.,TimeRollupPattern)

1. Find the default rollup pattern Blocks under the auxiliary resources in your project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']]]>
2. Copy any default rollup pattern Block (e.g., CostRollupPattern). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Paste it in your model (e.g. under the RollUp Pattern Package). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. The copy of the pattern Block is created *(*e.g. *Cost**RollupPattern1*). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Rename the pattern Block, value properties, part property, Constraint Block, and Parametric diagram according to your needs (e.g., Block *TimeRollupPattern*, Constraint Block *totalTime*, properties*time*).
6. Define the Type property value of a value property in the Specification window according to your needs (e.g., from *Real* to *Seconds*). The different type of rollup pattern Block *(*e.g., *Time**RollupPattern*) is created (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1348678325

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

according to your needs (e.g., from <em>Real</em> to <em>Seconds</em>).</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The different type of rollup pattern Block <em>(</em>e.g., <em>Time</em><em>RollupPattern</em>) is created (see the figure below).</span></span></li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="time_rollup_pattern.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></p><p> </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69feb8e4-4da4-4276-8788-5f05f6c47e3b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348678325</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9ae224cc-e23f-4436-a304-67d4f7f9597c"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rollup Pattern Wizard" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Webinar</strong></p><ul><li><a href="http://www.nomagic.com/mbse/events/webinars/item/webinar-7-2016-total-mass-cost-and-power-rollups.html">Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Laptop Cost Analysis</strong> that comes with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969584 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05005: Creating new validation rules

- page_id: `243969584`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969584/Creating+new+validation+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:06.786+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Managing new validation rule

927074355

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Managing new validation rule

927074358

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Managing new validation rule

927074357

#### EXCERPT: Managing new validation rule

INLINE

You can create your own validation rules in the model and use them to check your model or keep them only for documentation purpose. You can locate new validation rules anywhere in your model, but usually they are created for the classifier, stereotype, or metaclass.

To create a new validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying validationRule stereotype' space='']Apply «validationRule» stereotype.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Constrained Element property' space='']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining Constraint condition' space='']Define Constraint expression. The new validation rule is created in the model .

#### NOTE: Managing new validation rule

Managing new validation rule

You can include a new validation rule to your own created validation suite.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='']>]]>
- How to include the validation rules to validation suite Package >>

You can manage new validation rules in your model as any other elements.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']>]]>

927074354

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563220 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05006: Creating NOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563220`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563220/Creating+NOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.104+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- NOV-1 Free Form.
- NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description.

An NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the NOV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram. This approach is a bit different and may appear strange to DoDAF users, but in NAF an NOV-1 is usually created at the later stages of the architecture. For this reason we have implemented an NOV-1 Free Form diagram that is more likely what DoDAF users are used to.

To create an NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram

1. Make sure you have NSV-1 and NOV-2 diagrams completed.
2. Create Concept Roles.
3. Specify the types of the Concept Roles.
4. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:</p><ul><li>NOV-1 Free Form.</li><li>NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description.</li></ul><p>An NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the NOV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>Concept Role represents logical or physical unit of your architecture. It means that you should have those units in the architecture before creating the diagram. This approach is a bit different and may appear strange to DoDAF users, but in NAF an NOV-1 is usually created at the later stages of the architecture. For this reason we have implemented an NOV-1 Free Form diagram that is more likely what DoDAF users are used to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Make sure you have NSV-1 and NOV-2 diagrams completed.</li><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles.</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563226 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05007: Creating NOV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563226`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563226/Creating+NOV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.229+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Architectures.
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performer.
3. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Architectures.</li><li>Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performer.</li><li>Associate Operational Roles using <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563233 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05008: Creating NOV-3 table

- page_id: `249563233`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563233/Creating+NOV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.401+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-3 Operational Information Requirements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-3 table

1. Add existing Operational Exchanges.
2. Click the Add Existing button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors. In case an Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.

- You need to use an NOV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.
- You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-3 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add existing Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors. In case an Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c84066e-3797-4e3b-980e-804515e76528"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to use an NOV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.</li><li>You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563238 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05009: Creating NOV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249563238`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563238/Creating+NOV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.549+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-4 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.

You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.

You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an NOV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before NSV-1 view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</p><p>You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.</p><p>You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an NOV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</p><p>Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before NSV-1 view.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563246 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05010: Creating NOV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `249563246`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563246/Creating+NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.770+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563244 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05011: Creating NOV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `249563244`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563244/Creating+NOV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.700+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from NOV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NOV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from NOV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563253 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05012: Creating NOV-6a table

- page_id: `249563253`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563253/Creating+NOV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:21.002+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-6a Operational Rule Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an NOV-6a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Create New Operational Constraint button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information , Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Operational Constraint button and select Operational Constraints from the model.

- You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.
- You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an NOV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Operational Constraint</strong> button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Information</span>, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing Operational Constraint</strong> button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a27cb72e-1936-4e1a-bee5-a1bf21cf1587"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.</li><li>You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563266 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05013: Creating NOV-7 diagram

- page_id: `249563266`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563266/Creating+NOV-7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:21.453+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-7 Information Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-7 diagram

1. Create Information Elements. 
2. Add Properties. 
3. Draw Associations.

Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Association relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-7 diagram</p><hr /><p>1. Create Information Elements.<br />2. Add Properties.<br />3. Draw Associations.</p><p><br /></p><p>Entities can be linked with Information Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Association relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563188 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05014: Creating NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563188`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563188/Creating+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.099+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.

To create an NPV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects.
2. Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.
3. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (NOV-4) or Actual Posts (NOV-4) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.

You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read (see the figure below).

You can also use an NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects by dragging one project (part) to another (whole). Alternatively you can specify the whole and part properties.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations (NOV-4) or Actual Posts (NOV-4) responsible for them by using the Organizational Project Relationship.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e80c92-1140-43e2-96c0-3b6a2eba8cd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can nest Actual Projects, Actual Organizations, and Actual Posts to one another in the diagram to make it look more attractive and easier to read (see the figure below).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9dc6c3b9-eaab-4bdc-a188-2c1ece26cd6c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use an NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix to define the association between Actual Projects and Actual Organizational resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563192 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05015: Creating NPV-1 Gantt chart

- page_id: `249563192`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563192/Creating+NPV-1+Gantt+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:19.225+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create NPV-1 Gantt chart, NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens by default.

To create an NPV-1 Gantt chart from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.
3. In the NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship dialog, click Add . NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click Next .
5. Add the project(s). Click Next .
6. Set the chart properties.
7. Click Finish .

If you do not wish to use the NPV-1 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NPV-1”.

Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the **General** properties, set **Show NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart** to false.

To create an NPV-1 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.
3. In the NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NPV-1 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create NPV-1 Gantt chart, NPV-1 Creation Wizard opens by default.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 Gantt chart from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.</li><li>In the <strong>NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner for the diagram. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Add the project(s). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>Set the chart properties.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb39f83f-86e7-4278-b51c-cd3e9f550975"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the NPV-1 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create NPV-1”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UAF</strong> tab. In the <strong>General</strong> properties, set <strong>Show NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NPV-1 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Find and select NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship.</li><li>In the <strong>NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationship</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64756969-e38f-462b-86a8-e6e793a627a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank NPV-1 diagram is created.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563273 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05016: Creating NSOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563273`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563273/Creating+NSOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:21.599+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create NSOV-1 just before finishing an NOV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the NSV-1 or NSV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create a NSOV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The NSOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create NSOV-1 just before finishing an NOV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the NSV-1 or NSV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSOV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The NSOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563294 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05017: Creating NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition diagram

- page_id: `249563294`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563294/Creating+NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:22.080+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

To create a NSOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.

To create a Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>To create a NSOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563296 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05018: Creating NSOV-5 Service Behaviour diagram

- page_id: `249563296`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563296/Creating+NSOV-5+Service+Behaviour+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:22.147+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a Service Behavior diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NSOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Behavior diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563325 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05019: Creating NSV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563325`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563325/Creating+NSV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:23.059+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-1 System Interface Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

NSV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires NOV-2 to be completed.

To create an NSV-1 diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Interfaces.

The NSV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the **System Interface Internal Description** diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create NSV-4 view as soon as the NSV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>NSV-1 is the first Systems Viewpoint view to be developed. It requires NOV-2 to be completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an NSV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Interfaces.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The NSV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competencies provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <strong>System Interface Internal Description</strong> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>You can proceed to create NSV-4 view as soon as the NSV-1 has been completed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563303 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05020: Creating NSV-10a table

- page_id: `249563303`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563303/Creating+NSV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:22.381+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-10a Systems Rule Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a NSV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Create New Resource Constraint button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Resource Constraint button and selecting Resource Constraints .

- You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a NSV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Resource Constraint</strong> button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the<strong> Add Existing Resource Constraint</strong> button and selecting <strong>Resource Constraints</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba8cb062-0adf-4a6b-86cb-48719f06dc29"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563332 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05021: Creating NSV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563332`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563332/Creating+NSV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:23.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-2 System Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a NSV-1 before creating NSV-2.

To create a NSV-2 diagram

1. Create or reuse Resources created in NSV-1.
2. Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.
3. Create Resource Ports.
4. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

NSV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the **System Internal Communications Description** diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a NSV-1 before creating NSV-2.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Resources created in NSV-1.</li><li>Associate Resources using Resource interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>NSV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular Resources. You can model the structure of each Resource by using the <strong>System Internal Communications Description</strong> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563344 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05022: Creating NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249563344`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563344/Creating+NSV-4+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:23.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create NSV-4, make sure there are NOV-5 and NSV-1 created in the architecture.

The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.

To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from NSV-1, NSV-2.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.
5. Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from NOV-5.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create NSV-4, make sure there are NOV-5 and NSV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from NSV-1, NSV-2.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from NOV-5.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563346 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05023: Creating NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

- page_id: `249563346`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563346/Creating+NSV-4+Functionality+Description+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:23.616+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-4 System Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create NSV-4, ensure NOV-5 and NSV-1 are created in the architecture.

The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchange s on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create NSV-4, ensure NOV-5 and NSV-1 are created in the architecture.</p><p>The NSV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a NSV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchange s on every Function Edge.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563371 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05024: Creating NSV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249563371`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563371/Creating+NSV-8+diagram

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SV-8 diagram

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Find and select NSV-8 System Evolution Description.
3. In the NSV-8 Capability Configuration Management dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank NSV-8 diagram is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563384 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05025: Creating NSV-9 table

- page_id: `249563384`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563384/Creating+NSV-9+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:08.875+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-9 Technology Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658547230

1658547244

1658547233

To create a NSV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658547228

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563392 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05026: Creating NTV-1 table

- page_id: `249563392`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563392/Creating+NTV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:24.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Technical Viewpoint > NTV-1 Technical Standards Profile
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a NTV-1 table, do either

- Add a new UAF Element by clicking the Add new UAF Element button and select any UAF Element (this term means any of the UAF available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.
- Add the Existing UAF elements by clicking the Add Existing UAF Element button and select the UAF elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UAF element must conform to by clicking the … button on any cell in the Standard/Policy column.

- You can remove Rows (UAF Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a NTV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new <span>UAF </span>Element by clicking the <strong>Add new UAF Element</strong> button and select any <span>UAF </span>Element (this term means any of the <span>UAF </span>available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.</li><li>Add the Existing <span>UAF </span>elements by clicking the<strong> Add Existing UAF Element</strong> button and select the <span>UAF </span>elements.</li><li>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular <span>UAF </span>element must conform to by clicking the <strong>…</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6c1ef35-9cd3-4f6b-b63e-99fa250ea91d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can remove Rows (UAF Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563397 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05027: Creating NTV-2 table

- page_id: `249563397`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563397/Creating+NTV-2+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:10.083+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Technical Viewpoint > NTV-2 Technical Standards Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658580680

1658580694

1658580683

To create an NTV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658580678

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563471 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05028: Creating Operational Exchanges in L1 Node Types diagram

- page_id: `249563471`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563471/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L1+Node+Types+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:11.992+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L1 Node Types
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l1-node-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L1 Node Types diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Associationbetween a pair of Nodes and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Association.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

An L1 Node Types diagram also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Nodes, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Nodes.

The structure of each Node can be modeled using the Logical Scenario diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the L1 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space=''] diagram.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> by doing one of the following:<span
