<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563516 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05030: Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram

- page_id: `249563516`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563516/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L4+Logical+Activities+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:13.301+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L4 Logical Activities
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l4-logical-activities', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange**wizard . To open the wizard, do either:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow between a pair of Operational Activities and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_operational_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='operational_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L4 Logical Activities diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange </strong>wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link"> </span>. To open the wizard, do either:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow between a pair of Operational Activities and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="New Operational Exchange" ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Operational Exchange" ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, select an Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d55a547b-8d05-4c0e-97a0-bb3297855056"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563524 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05031: Creating Operational Exchanges in L6 Logical Sequence diagram

- page_id: `249563524`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563524/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+L6+Logical+Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:13.853+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L6 Logical Sequence
- labels: ['create-operational-exchanges-in-l6-logical-sequence', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the L6 Logical Sequence diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Node Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Message.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Exchange in the L6 Logical Sequence diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Roles</span> and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Operational Exchange" ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Operational Exchange" ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Message.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="391935ea-18eb-4aac-98d7-aa809f0f1598"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563228 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05032: Creating Operational Exchanges in NOV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563228`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563228/Creating+Operational+Exchanges+in+NOV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:20.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-2 Operational Node Connectivity Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the NOV-2 diagram

1. Open the Operational Exchange creation wizard. To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.
  - On the Operational Exchanges toolbar in the diagram palette, click the Operational Exchange button and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.
3. Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.

An NOV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Location requirements, and Operational Ports provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operationa Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the NOV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the NOV-5 diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Exchange in the NOV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> creation wizard. To open the wizard, do either:<ul><li>Select a <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Connector </span>and on the smart manipulator, click the <strong>New Operational Exchange</strong> button.</li><li>On the <strong>Operational Exchanges</strong> toolbar in the diagram palette, click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button and then click the Operational Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>An NOV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Location requirements, and Operational Ports provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operationa Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the NOV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the NOV-5 diagram.</p>
````

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:

  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562560 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05038: Creating OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

- page_id: `249562560`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562560/Creating+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:35.580+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic
- labels: ['create-ov-1-high-level-operational-concept-graphic-diagram', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic Diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram. Since it is a bit complex, the OV-1 Free Form diagram can be used alternatively.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562565 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05039: Creating OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562565`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562565/Creating+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:35.782+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space=''] diagram.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="844e8454-fb00-4760-b817-89cb9bc6b387"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Performers.</li><li>Connect them using the Associations relationship.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</span></p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p>Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562572 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05040: Creating OV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `249562572`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562572/Creating+OV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:36.079+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Internal Description
- labels: ['create-ov-2-operational-resource-flow-internal-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.

To create an OV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab520339-ec3a-4ea9-ac87-ad3df225ba23"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The owner of the OV-2 Internal diagram must be Operational Performer.</p><p>To create an OV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Roles.</li><li>Associate Operational Roles using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Connectors</span>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562582 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05041: Creating OV-3 role-based table

- page_id: `249562582`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562582/Creating+OV-3+role-based+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:36.400+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table

1. In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Agents</span>). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the OV-3 role-based table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the OV-3 role-based table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 role-based diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3f29e07-4f97-4eae-9177-b78a40adfed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562580 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05042: Creating OV-3 table

- page_id: `249562580`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562580/Creating+OV-3+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:48.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-operational-exchange-in-ov-3', 'create-ov-3-table', 'create-operational-exchange-in-ov-3-operational-resource-flow-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 view to create or modify an Operational Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an OV-3 table.</p><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the OV-3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Agent</span>, and Implements cells are read only.</span></li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify an Operational Exchange.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562588 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05043: Creating OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249562588`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562588/Creating+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:36.541+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['create-ov-4-organizational-relationship-chart', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.

To create an OV-4 Typical diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, andPersons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an OV-4 Actual diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship . [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4_actual.png']

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.
- the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships are modeled in OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p><p>To create an OV-4 Typical diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Organizations, </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and </span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an OV-4 Actual diagram<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Persons</span>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Relate Actual Organizational Resources with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Organization Relationship</span></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4_actual.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li><li>the Resources and in general they could be modeled just before <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562954 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05044: Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `249562954`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562954/Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:27.281+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model.This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Operational Activity Flow Model diagram, the Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the Diagram.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions with Operational Activity Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Activity Edges.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562956 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05045: Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `249562956`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562956/Creating+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:27.369+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.
2. Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.

The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.

To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.
3. Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The OV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Operational Activity Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity hierarchies.</li><li>Operational Activity Flow Model. This diagram represents Operational Activity flows.</li></ol><p>The Operational Activity Model diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create or Reuse from OV-2 (recommended) Operational Performers.</li><li>Draw Is Capable to Perform relationship between them and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562595 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05046: Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `249562595`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562595/Creating+OV-5a+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['create-ov-5a-operational-activity-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.

To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562597 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05047: Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `249562597`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562597/Creating+OV-5b+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['create-ov-5b-operational-activity-flow-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Activity Model diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions wit<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562606 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05048: Creating OV-6a table

- page_id: `249562606`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562606/Creating+OV-6a+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:48.634+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: ['add-operational-constraint-in-ov-6a', 'create-ov-6a-operational-rules-model', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns: 
- Applies To . Select the cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . In the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, Operational Exchange). 
- **Rule****Specification**. Select the cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL). 
- **Rule Kind**. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.
- Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns:<br />-  </span><strong>Applies To</strong>. Select the cell and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. In the <strong>Select Subject Of Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Operational Exchange).<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the cell</span> and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. In the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).<br />- <strong>Rule Kind</strong>. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.<br /></span></span></li><li>Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. In <strong>Select Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select Operational Constraints.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The filled row is added in table. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br /><span> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563686 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05049: Creating P1 Resource Types diagram

- page_id: `249563686`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563686/Creating+P1+Resource+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:01.815+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Resource Types diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Connect Resources using Resource Associations.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.

The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P2 Resource Internal Structure' space=''] diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P4 Resource Functions' space=''] view as soon as the P1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P1 Resource Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Connect Resources using Resource Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.</li></ol><p>The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P2 Resource Internal Structure" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45fbb702-bd2a-4e42-9ae9-b6702a7e6257"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P4 Resource Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the P1 has been completed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563695 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05050: Creating P1 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `249563695`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563695/Creating+P1+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:02.124+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'build-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.

To create a P1 Service Provision matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.</p><p>To create a P1 Service Provision matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563690 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05051: Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix

- page_id: `249563690`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563690/Creating+P1+Systems+to+Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:21.879+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'build-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)****shows how resources interact with each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)<strong> </strong><span>shows how resources interact with each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563692 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05052: Creating P1 Technology Forecast table

- page_id: `249563692`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563692/Creating+P1+Technology+Forecast+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:18.659+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-technology-forecast-table', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658799737

1658799749

1658799738

To create a P1 Technology Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658799736

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563702 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05053: Creating P2 Structure diagram

- page_id: `249563702`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563702/Creating+P2+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:22.206+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a P2 Resource Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the P2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**P2 Resource Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **P2****Resource Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Resource Artifact element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Resource Artifact in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Artifact and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Resource Artifact in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Resource Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Artifact and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2_drag_resource_artifact.png']

Resource Artifact

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Artifact (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Resource Artifact

When you have Resource Artifact created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element, which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose to use this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.display_related_nested.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:filename="resources.str.compartments.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="879f1986-4eb4-464d-879b-270ccfa1b21e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.attributes.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="722" ac:width="756"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related_nested.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563726 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05054: Creating P2i diagram

- page_id: `249563726`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563726/Creating+P2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:02.706+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Internal Structure
- labels: ['create-p2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P2i diagram

1. Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Specify the types of the Resource Roles.
3. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a P2i diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types of the Resource Roles.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Ports.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563736 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05055: Creating P3 table

- page_id: `249563736`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563736/Creating+P3+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:19.488+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P3 Resource Connectivity
- labels: ['create-p3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or Resource Exchangesto fill in a P3 table.

newTo add new Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space=''] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new" /> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing" /> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>to fill in a P3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563743 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05056: Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `249563743`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563743/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:20.112+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be created first.

To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

To display possible ResourceExchanges, do either

- Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
- In the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.

P4 Resource Functions diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71af54d1-3d83-4531-b4de-b16ed860afc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">To display possible Resource </span>Exchanges<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either<br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Select Function </span>Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the Function </span>Control Flow or Function Object Flow.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span opens.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
