<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563745 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05057: Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249563745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563745/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:03.486+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space=''] view.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space=''] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563762 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05058: Creating P7 diagram

- page_id: `249563762`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563762/Creating+P7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:03.869+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P7 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-p7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P7 diagram

1. Create Data Elements.
2. Add Properties for Data Elements.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.

Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Data Elements.</li><li>Add Properties for Data Elements.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563767 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05059: Creating P8 table

- page_id: `249563767`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563767/Creating+P8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:04.005+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P8 Resource Constraints
- labels: ['create-p8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P8 table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the **Add New** button. The new row in the table is created.Select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange ). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, </span>Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</span></li><li>Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965859 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05060: Creating path variables

- page_id: `243965859`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965859/Creating+path+variables
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-03-17T15:25:41.294+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main purpose of the path variables feature is to give additional sharing flexibility. It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.

Path Variables may be used in:

- Code engineering classpath
- Working directories
- Hyperlinks
- Modules paths
- Reports

To create a path variable

1. In the main menu, go to Options > Environment . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog opens.
2. On the left part of the dialog, select Path Variables .
3. In the Path Variables area click the Add button. The Path Variable dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_options_dialog.png']
4. In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.
5. Next to the Value box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] , navigate to the desired location, and click Open .
6. In the Path Variable dialog, click OK . Your custom path variable is created and shown in the Defined Path Variables list.
7. In the Environment Options dialog, click OK .

Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the **Use Path Variables** dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project **From a predefined location** and add a new path to the **Paths to used projects** list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the **Use Path Variables** dialog box, clear the **Show suggestion to use path variables** check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the **Use Original** button.

To use a suggested path variable

- In the **Available forms** list, select a path and click the **Use Selected** button.

If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the *documentation.server* option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The main purpose of the path variables feature is to give additional sharing flexibility. It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.</p><p>Path Variables may be used in:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2d73eda7-6012-4f9e-b082-d3c6529b6ee9"><span>Code engineering classpath</span></li><li class="p" data-uuid="e58b0242-b69d-4a00-a9c4-717f4e0078fb"><p>Working directories</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="f2523e4f-fb8e-4248-8d91-c7294ba86e72"><p>Hyperlinks</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="2a08305b-a785-4a75-8630-91b2b0284c8d"><p>Modules paths</p></li><li class="p" data-uuid="5f7de312-1c37-4c59-8cde-26863e2b9808"><p>Reports</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a path variable</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b139ee84-4e51-4309-89f6-769ad80a5658">In the main menu, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li data-uuid="53dcc997-2700-4442-a976-0f9dcfa67d4d">On the left part of the dialog, select <strong>Path Variables</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="88881a3b-a8bb-4785-a0e1-8c05cdf0d1f4">In the <strong>Path Variables</strong> area click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="dc2f666c-64f6-44de-b99e-2c46972206c9">In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.</li><li data-uuid="67613556-6b19-4c34-8888-51326a61a045">Next to the <strong>Value</strong> box, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png" /></ac:image>, navigate to the desired location, and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="ec4c169e-b1a6-4792-a9c8-f231d6d2b3ca">In the <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. Your custom path variable is created and shown in the <strong>Defined Path Variables</strong> list.</li><li data-uuid="6f5d25a7-6f0d-4ef1-ab7a-6491da7fdf96">In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><p>Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the <strong>Use Path Variables</strong> dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project <strong>From a predefined location</strong> and add a new path to the <strong>Paths to used projects</strong> list.<br /> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_path_variables_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br />If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Path Variables</strong></span> dialog box, clear the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Show suggestion to use path variables</strong></span> check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Original</strong> button.</span></p><p><span class="hcp1"> </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">To use a suggested path variable</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="41e55327-5b6e-4c64-8281-b03bca4c7535">In the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Available forms</strong></span> list, select a path and click the <span class="hcp1"><strong>Use Selected</strong> button.</span></li></ul><p><span class="hcp1"> </span></p><p>If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the <em>documentation.server</em> option.</p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561813 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05061: Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action

- page_id: `249561813`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561813/Creating+pins+automatically+for+Opaque+Action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:10.558+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space=''], you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.

To create Pins automatically using the **Parse and Create Pin**button

1. Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.
2. Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.
3. Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='parse_and_create_pins.png'] .
4. Pins are created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link>, you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create Pins automatically using the <strong>Parse and Create Pin </strong>button</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.</li><li>Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.</li><li>Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Pins are created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561248 space=MT version=8 -->
## PAGE 05062: Creating ports

- page_id: `249561248`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561248/Creating+ports
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-09T12:21:22.086+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Defining interfaces using ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create the following kinds of ports by using the SysML v1 Plugin:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] - expose features of the owning Block or its internal Part Properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'] - support their own features.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'] - since it is deprecated, you use the Proxy Port typed by the Interface Block instead of the non-atomic Flow Port.

##### Creating ports

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port, do one of the following:

- From the diagram palette, select the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button and click the element on the diagram pane.
- Select an element on the diagram pane and from the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.
- In the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , select the Ports/Interfaces property group, click the Create button, and select Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.

You can also create[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']at both ends of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connecting parts through interface' space='']>]]>

##### Converting the Part Property to Full Port

A Full Port is a part on a boundary. You can convert your Parts Properties into Full Ports by dragging them to the diagram frame.

#### INFO: Note

Note

All ports, connectors, and other information, including the layout, will remain unchanged.

To convert a Part Property to the Full Port

1. Select the Part Property shape and drag it to the diagram frame.
2. When the diagram frame is highlighted in blue, drop the shape. 
The Full Port is created on the diagram frame. NoteWhen the Full Port is created on the diagram frame, you cannot drag it back (except to Undo the conversion).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Part Property converted to the Full Port.

##### Creating a port while creating a Connector

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port automatically when drawing a Connector from a port to a Part Property

1. Select a port shape on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Select Part Property shape appears. The menu appears if a new port can be created on the Part Property or it has hidden ports.
3. From the Select Port menu, select one of the following commands:

- None - if you want to connect the Connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type, and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a Connector to a port, too, and the menu appears. As a result, the nested port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Select Port** menu opens after drawing the Connector from the *CANPort* Port to Part Property typed by *GPS Module* Block.

**Related pages**

- Displaying parts and ports
- Connecting parts through interface
- Layout templates
- Refactoring
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']

**Port features**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the **diagram aspects** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563774 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05063: Creating Pr diagram

- page_id: `249563774`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563774/Creating+Pr+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:22.351+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['create-pr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create Pr diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Pr diagram

1. Click the **Add New** button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565259 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05064: Creating predefined relation map

- page_id: `249565259`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565259/Creating+predefined+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:36.232+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis > Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['create-predefined-relation-map', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.

To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either

- Right-click the element, select Create Diagram and under the UAF Analysis Diagrams , and choose the relation map you want to create.
- Right-click the element, select Related Elements and then choose the relation map you want to create.

You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:

- In the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.</p><p>To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and under the </span> <strong>UAF Analysis Diagrams</strong>, and choose the relation map you want to create.</li><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> and then choose the relation map you want to create.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac9b2656-f75a-48e1-9ee5-3f42d303776d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:</p><ul><li>In the main toolbar, click the<strong> Create Diagram</strong> button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565134 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05065: Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams

- page_id: `249565134`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565134/Creating+Process+Flow+Diagrams+From+Compositions+or+Aggregations+Defined+in+Process+Diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:35.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1179127095

INLINE

1179127116

INLINE

1179127105

INLINE

When you have a process diagram with the defined aggregation/composition of activities/functions, you can create a process flow diagram for the selected activity/function:

- From the Smart manipulator toolbar.
- From the shortcut menu in the Containment tree.

To create a process flow diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - In a process diagram pane, click the activity for which you want to create a process flow diagram. In the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the process flow diagram button. [ATTACHMENT filename='process_to_process_flow.png']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the activity and in the shortcut menu, click Create Diagram and the choose <*view*> Process Flow .
2. The Select Behavior dialog opens. In the open dialog, select which behaviors you want to be created in the process flow diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_behavior_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .

The new process flow diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Note, that only the first level behaviors will be suggested in the **Select Behavior** dialog.

1179127084

INLINE

<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c14bef8c-7930-4a4d-a2b8-b68e9728008e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1179127095</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5afe413d-6ac3-44fb-aceb-d918b5318a05"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1179127116</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c591155-3f3c-4964-b76f-2253b3f068f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1179127105</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you have a process diagram with the defined aggregation/composition of activities/functions, you can create a process flow diagram for the selected activity/function:</p><ul><li>From the Smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>From the shortcut menu in the Containment tree.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a process flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In a process diagram pane, click the activity for which you want to create a process flow diagram. In the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the process flow diagram button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="process_to_process_flow.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the activity and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and the choose <strong>&lt;<em>view</em>&gt; Process Flow</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Select Behavior</strong> dialog opens. In the open dialog, select which behaviors you want to be created in the process flow diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_behavior_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The new process flow diagram is created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_process_flow_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f2261fc-677d-4206-93c6-2e493fe2bd94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note, that only the first level behaviors will be suggested in the <strong>Select Behavior</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="952ebca7-7e7d-4dca-9c82-50b632549460"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1179127084</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="NOV-5 Operational Activity Model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="NSV-4 System Functionality Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="NSOV-5 Service Behaviour Definition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="S4 Service Functions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P4 Resource Functions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Personnel Processes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Projects Processes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Security Processes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Services Processes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Processes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966201 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05067: Creating projects for Ecore modeling

- page_id: `243966201`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966201/Creating+projects+for+Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:55.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305881821

305881823

305881822

You can use the following ways to create a project for Ecore modeling: 
 
To create a new project from the Ecore template

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **New Project**. The **New Project** dialog opens.
2. In the **Other** domain, select **Project from Template**.
3. Type a project name.
4. Specify the project location.
5. Under **Select template**, expand **Metamodeling** and then select **Ecore Template**.
6. Click **OK** when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 
To apply the Ecore profile to a project

The Ecore profile can be applied only to existing UML or CMOF / EMOF projects.

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*install root>\profiles*. The content of the <*install root>\profiles* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore_Profile.xml* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 If your Ecore model references some standard Ecore elements (such as standard data types (for example, EShort) or standard metaclasses (for example, EStructuralFeature), you need to use the standard Ecore library in your project. 
 
To use the standard Ecore library in a project

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*installroot>\modelLibraries*. The content of the <*install root>\modelLibraries* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore.mdzip* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305881820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new projects from templates' space='']

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562536 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05068: Creating PV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562536`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562536/Creating+PV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:34.892+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-diagram', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

To create a PV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.
2. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) or Actual Posts ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] ) responsible for them using Responsible For relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>To create a PV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual  Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts </span>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Responsible For </span>relationship.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562538 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05069: Creating PV-1 matrix

- page_id: `249562538`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562538/Creating+PV-1+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.336+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-matrix', 'build-pv-1-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] before creating a PV-1.

The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, andActual Posts) for Projects.

The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .

To create a PV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Posts</span>) for Projects.</p><p>The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .</p><p>To create a PV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562543 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05070: Creating PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562543`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562543/Creating+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.415+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['create-pv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a PV-2 diagram

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines** check box.

To create a PV-2 diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body> <strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a PV-2 diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562551 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05071: Creating PV-3 matrix

- page_id: `249562551`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562551/Creating+PV-3+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:47.622+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['create-pv-3-matrix', 'build-pv-3-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a PV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping</strong> describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a PV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</span></p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b006062-f431-4f55-b590-c1c8054e6b8b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966579 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05072: Creating relation map

- page_id: `243966579`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966579/Creating+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:05.635+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['create-relation-map', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a relation map

1. Select an element, for which you need to create the relation map. This element is the context of the relation map.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the Analyze menu, click Create Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Analysis Diagrams > Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Related Elements > Create Relation Map .

The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.

- The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.
- When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.
- The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a relation map</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, for which you need to create the relation map. This element is the context of the relation map.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the main toolbar, click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> button. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analyze</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> menu, click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analysis Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related Elements</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198500c2-d1fb-45cd-bbf8-9975f8f67040"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.</li><li>When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.</li><li>The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005612 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05073: Creating Requirement Diagram for sub-requirements

- page_id: `249005612`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005612/Creating+Requirement+Diagram+for+sub-requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-17T23:11:22.128+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements of the selected requirement directly on the diagram pane.

To create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements

1. Select a Requirement shape on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click the Create diagram for sub-requirements button.

A new requirements diagram for the sub-requirements is created, having the same name as the selected requirement. The selected requirement is the owner of the sub-requirements diagram and is marked with the diagram sign.

Double-click the owner symbol to open a diagram for sub-requirements and use navigation buttons on the diagram toolbar to move back or forward through diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements of the selected requirement directly on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Requirements Diagram for sub-requirements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Requirement shape on the diagram pane.</li><li>On the smart manipulator, click the <strong>Create diagram for sub-requirements</strong> button.</li></ol><p>A new requirements diagram for the sub-requirements is created, having the same name as the selected requirement. The selected requirement is the owner of the sub-requirements diagram and is marked with the diagram sign.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7cdcdd2-5841-4506-98d0-037b50f64bd5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Double-click the owner symbol to open a diagram for sub-requirements and use navigation buttons on the diagram toolbar to move back or forward through diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005543 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05074: Creating requirements

- page_id: `249005543`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005543/Creating+requirements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:02.769+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create and represent text-based requirements and relate them to other modeling elements. The requirements can be depicted in graphical, tabular, or tree structure format. This page provides instructions for creating requirements in the model. You can create requirements in the:

- 
- .
- .

##### containmentCreating requirements in the Containment tree

If you create Requirements in the Containment tree, the element is created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']. After that you can represent them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] by dragging them or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Table' space=''] by adding an existing element.

To create requirements in the Containment tree

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , right-click the package, or another Requirement where you want to create or nest a new Requirement.
3. From the shortcut menu, click Create Element .
4. In the **Create Element** dialog, select any type of Requirement. If you have created your own custom Requirement types, they appear under the**Custom Requirements** group.
5. Type a Requirement name. The new requirement is created in the Model Browser.

##### diagramCreating requirements in the Requirement diagram

If you create Requirements directly on the diagram pane, the element is created in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']as well.

To create Requirements on the Requirement Diagram pane

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''], select a **Requirement** or other type of requirement button and click on the diagram pane. The element is created in the same package where the diagram is stored.
3. On the Requirement shape, type a name, ID, and description. The Requirement is created and added in the table.

You can find more information about how to work in diagrams on the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space=''].

To select a different owner

1. Hold **Shift**, click**Add New** button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu. 
The **Select Owner** dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.

##### tableCreating requirements in the Requirement Table

You can create new, nested requirements, or add existing ones in the Requirement Table.

###### Creating new Requirements

To create a new Requirement in a Requirement Table

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. On the Requirement table toolbar, click the Add New button or press Insert ( Ctrl+I for Mac users). The list of Requirement types opens.
3. Click a desired requirement type. A new line above the first numbered requirement appears.
4. In that empty line, type a requirement name and specify other requirement properties. To select a different owner1. Hold **Shift**, click**Add New** button, and select a Requirement type from the drop-down menu. 
The **Select Owner** dialog opens, enabling you to choose a different owner.

###### Adding existing Requirements

To add existing Requirements in a Requirement Table

1. Open an existing or create a new Requirement Table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']]]>
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Requirement table toolbar, click the Add Existing button or press Ctrl + Insert ( Ctrl+I for Mac users). In the Select Requirement dialog, select the requirements you want to represent in the table and click OK .
  - Drag the entire requirements Package from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] and drop it on the empty Requirement table to represent all its content in the table.

###### Adding nested Requirements

To add nested Requirement in the Requirement Table

1. Select Requirement you want to nest in the table.
2. Click the Add Nested button or press Alt+Insert ( Alt+I for Mac users).
3. Select the desired Requirement type from the drop-down menu. If you have created your own custom Requirement types, they appear under the Custom Requirements group in the drop-down menu.

More information about how to work in tables you can find in the following pages:

- Table toolbars
- Table Criteria area
- Basic tasks in tables

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dragging objects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relating requirements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Basic tasks in tables' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005536 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05075: Creating Requirements Project

- page_id: `249005536`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005536/Creating+Requirements+Project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:02.394+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create requirements using the the Requirements Project template. It contains predefined packages, elements, and diagrams to start creating your own requirements in the model.

The Requirements Project template is only available in MagicDraw with Cameo Requirement Modeler Plugin installed.

To create a Requirement Project

1. Open the New Project dialog. Do one of the following:
  - 
    - From the File menu, select New Project .
    - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
    - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.
2. In the New Project dialog, specify the following:
  - On the left side of the dialog, under the General-Purpose Modeling domain, select the Requirements Project .
  - On the right side of the dialog, type the file name in the Name box.
  - On the right side of the dialog, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_location_button.png'] button and select the location to store the created project. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirements_projects_templates.png']
3. Click OK . The new Requirement project is created with predefined structure as shown in the image below. [ATTACHMENT filename='requirements_project_structure.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create requirements using the the Requirements Project template. It contains predefined packages, elements, and diagrams to start creating your own requirements in the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16ac13b5-48ab-4cc4-b773-d1c8fd43d925"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Requirements Project template is only available in MagicDraw with Cameo Requirement Modeler Plugin installed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="atl-forced-newline">To create a Requirement Project</p><hr /><ol><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="0bff8846-566b-4ffd-a94c-503dea97455d">Open the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog. Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li class="atl-forced-newline">From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>New Project</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Press Ctrl + Shift + N.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="a5b11f1a-686c-4c96-890b-5fcb32876319">In the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog, specify the following:<br /><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">On the left side of the dialog, under the <strong>General-Purpose Modeling</strong> domain, select the <strong>Requirements Project</strong>.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">On the right side of the dialog, type the file name in the <strong>Name</strong> box.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">On the right side of the dialog, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_location_button.png" /></ac:image> button and select the location to store the created project.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirements_projects_templates.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="a0cd5176-29b8-49bd-be0c-12c3ca1c9afb">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The new Requirement project is created with predefined structure as shown in the image below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirements_project_structure.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563046 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05076: Creating Resource  Exchange in MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249563046`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563046/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:30.051+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - In the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>In the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563055 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05077: Creating Resource Exchange in MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563055`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563055/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:30.432+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563688 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05079: Creating Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

- page_id: `249563688`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563688/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+P1+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:18.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the <span pane.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d43f9522-3e3d-4a70-8a85-739ccc6a013c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item To create an Resource Exchangein the P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow .
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562747 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05083: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram

- page_id: `249562747`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562747/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-1+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:51.420+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-1-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

## PAGE 05084: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-10c diagram

- page_id: `249562835`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562835/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-10c+diagram

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-10c', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-10c diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**RealizedResource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Message.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-10c diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="confluence-link">Realized </span>Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span> and then click the Resource Message.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2bfb81a-7f38-42d5-8cc1-8b64b196c8a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562753 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05085: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562753`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562753/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:51.876+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Association  </span>between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> resources.</span></li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2aa563e-be67-452b-ae59-f97ff2723412"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562769 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05087: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `249562769`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562769/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:52.331+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-3-matrix', 'delete-resource-interaction-from-sv-3-matrix', 'manage-sv-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Exchange appears in the selected intersection.

To remove an existing Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the filled intersection.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Exchange you want to remove from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select <strong>Add New Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>appears in the selected intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the filled intersection.</li><li>Under the <strong>Delete</strong> section, choose the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>you want to remove from the model.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563334 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05090: Creating Resource Exchanges in NSV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249563334`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563334/Creating+Resource+Exchanges+in+NSV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:23.271+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-2 System Communications Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````
