Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> resources.</span></li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b8fe9ba-f4c7-4a86-8b5b-4dc88f3d0ab8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></span></span> <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span> to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561673 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05092: Creating reusable constraint libraries

- page_id: `249561673`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561673/Creating+reusable+constraint+libraries
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:35.508+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.

The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.</p><p>The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting packages to new projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sharing project data" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563590 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05093: Creating S1 diagram

- page_id: `249563590`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563590/Creating+S1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:59.420+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-s1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create S1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='']. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create an S1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create S1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p>To create an S1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2d16256-bd5e-4a9e-b2f3-1e818e46fc67"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563602 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05094: Creating S2 diagram

- page_id: `249563602`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563602/Creating+S2+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:48:56.678+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S2 Service Structure
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an S2 Service Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**S2 Service Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for**S2 Service Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_drag_service.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Services

When a Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element that will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_edit_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_specify_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_compartment.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_parts.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an S2 Service Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> S2 Service Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="170" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="s2_new_diagram)containment_tree.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for<strong> S2 Service Structure, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="244" ac:width="562"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_create_service.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563644 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05095: Creating S4 Service Functions diagram

- page_id: `249563644`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563644/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:00.448+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563646 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05096: Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249563646`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563646/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:00.567+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions-taxonomy', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563662 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05097: Creating S8 table

- page_id: `249563662`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563662/Creating+S8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:01.321+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['create-s8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create S8 table, do either

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).
- Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create S8 table<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either</span><span> </span></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).</li><li>Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969091 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05098: Creating script operations

- page_id: `243969091`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969091/Creating+script+operations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:54.779+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

69158788

69158801

69158791

Use the script operation, when you need to specify an inline expression in OCL 2.0, as Java binary, or in any of the modeling tool-supported script languages. The modeling tool supports any script language that has a JSR-223-compliant engine.

Adding a script operation directly into a structured expression is the same as defining an operation in the model and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calling operations from the model' space=''].

To create a script operation

1. Click Create operation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_operation_in_relation_criteria_dialog.png']
2. Click the **Script** icon on the right side of the dialog.
3. If needed, change the operation name.
4. Click the three dots button next to the **Style** option to define the style****for your script operation. The Style option is available only in the **Dependency Criteria** and**Relation Criteria**dialogs.
5. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended languages (Binary or Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, (i.e., green for fast, yellow for medium, and red for slow). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears. If the engine of the language you need is not built into the modeling tool by default, you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool' space='']to the application yourself. Make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled!
6. Type in the Body box. Use the syntax of the selected language.
7. In the **Listeners Configuration** box, type a reference to the java class, which specifies when the result of the script operation must be recalculated. The listeners configuration is not necessary, but using it can improve the performance.
8. Click the three dots button next to the **Results Filter By Type** box and select one or more element types to filter the result of the script operation. 
The script operation is created. Now you can create a new one, where the result of the previously created operation is used as an argument.

69158787

**Related pages**

- Creating new operations
  - Calling operations from the model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968730 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05099: Creating Setters and Getters

- page_id: `243968730`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968730/Creating+Setters+and+Getters
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-11T10:13:51.470+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

Setters and Getters are common operations that contain almost every [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']. With the help of our modeling tool, **set** and **get** operations for Class attributes can be generated automatically.

To create a setter or a getter

There are two ways of creating a setter or a getter:

1. From the shortcut menu of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering , and then select Create Setters/Getters .
  1. The Select Attributes/Association Ends dialog opens.
2. Add a tagged value "getter/setter for attribute=attribute_name" to the selected Class.

The names of created operations (**setters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public void set + <attribute name> (<attribute type> <attribute name>)*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **setter** will look this way:

- public void setx (int x);

The names of created operations (**getters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public <attribute type> get + <attribute name> ( )*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **getter** will look this way:

- public int getx ();

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']
- Tools

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

With the help of our modeling tool, <strong>set</strong> and <strong>get</strong> operations for Class attributes can be generated automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a setter or a getter</p><hr /><p>There are two ways of creating a setter or a getter:</p><ol><li data-uuid="d80d1382-7948-4878-96bc-50fcd0d10f71">From the shortcut menu of the selected Class, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; to the selected Class.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The names of created operations (<strong>setters</strong>) are combined according to the following format:</p><p><em>public void set + &lt;attribute name&gt; (&lt;attribute type&gt; &lt;attribute name&gt;) </em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f0e47a8-6abe-4cec-ba76-0d641e5a9784"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated <strong>setter</strong> will look this way: </span></p><ul><li data-uuid="80a6238b-8216-40ce-a804-b68fe21cdf2a"><em>public void setx (int x);</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p><p><span>The names of created operations (<strong>getters</strong>) are combined according to the following format: </span></p><p><em>public &lt;attribute type&gt; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562984 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05101: Creating SOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562984`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562984/Creating+SOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:28.161+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create a SOV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SOV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563014 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05102: Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249563014`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563014/Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:29.084+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.

To create a Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563016 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05103: Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow diagram

- page_id: `249563016`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563016/Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:29.182+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562845 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05104: Creating StdV-1 table

- page_id: `249562845`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562845/Creating+StdV-1+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:55.243+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint > StdV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['create-stdv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-1 table, do either

- Create a new element: 1) Click the Add New button. 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element. 3) In the Select Owner dialog choose the owner and click OK . 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click Close . The element appears under the System element column. 5) Select the cell under the Standard/Policy column and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . 6) In the Select Standard dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.
- Add an existing element. 
 1) Click the**Add Existing** button. 
 2) In the **Select UAF element** dialog select desired elements and click **OK**. The element appears under the **System element** column. 
 3) Select the cell under the **Standard/Policy** column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 4) In the **Select Standard** dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The element appears under the <strong>System element</strong> column.<br /> 5) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .<br /> 6) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.<span> </span></li><li><p>Add an existing element.<br /> 1) Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button.<br /> 2) In the <strong>Select UAF element</strong> dialog select desired elements and click <strong>OK</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>System element</strong> column.<br /> 3) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .<br /> 4) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.</p></li></ul><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562850 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05105: Creating StdV-2 table

- page_id: `249562850`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562850/Creating+StdV-2+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:55.451+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint > StdV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['create-stdv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658487619

1658487630

1658487620

To create an StdV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658487617

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7958c7ff-10b5-433c-b56e-8f89a161cf5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1658487630</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca0a6d94-5949-41ca-899e-8489ef7d7a11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1658487620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create an StdV-2 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Press Ctrl+N.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the <strong>&lt;view name&gt;</strong> Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>&lt;diagram name&gt;</strong>.</li><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram.<br />The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.</p><h3>Adding rows</h3><p>To add rows with new resources</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong>.</li><li>Choose a resource type from the list.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose the owner.</li><li>In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add rows with existing resources</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Element </strong>dialog, choose a needed resource.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Adding new columns</h3><p>To add new columns</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add / Remove Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Filling cells</h3><p>When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d785b94-7113-40d3-8867-688f0f3fb55b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add a Subject of Forecast</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a needed cell.</li><li>Click the <strong>...</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Subject of Forecast</strong> dialog, choose the resources you need to add.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbc128d0-595e-48fb-ba4b-833cb4c1fcc7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.</p><h3>Removing columns</h3><p>To remove columns</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Add / Remove Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_columns.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Sorting columns</h3><p>After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.</p><p><br /></p><p>To sort columns</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click <strong>Sort Columns</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d30e4040-0f0c-4e2f-af8d-e0a6316c6489"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1658487617</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resources Forecast Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561536 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05106: Creating structure models by behavior models

- page_id: `249561536`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561536/Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:06.863+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

If the **Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model** command is not available under **Tools** in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:

- The Allocation Mode is set to Usage ( **Options** >**Project** > **SysML** > **Allocation** Mode ).
- The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , right-click a Block.
  - On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88cdcbe9-295e-4d19-bddc-f507bc82474a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong> command is not available under <strong>Tools</strong> in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Allocation Mode</strong> is set to <strong>Usage </strong>(<strong><strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Project</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong> &gt; <strong>Allocation</strong></strong> <strong>Mode</strong>).</li><li><p>The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click a Block. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565356 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05107: Creating structure models by behavior models in UAF

- page_id: `249565356`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565356/Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models+in+UAF
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:17.863+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > UAF Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer.
  - On the structure diagram pane, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performershape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the structure diagram pane, right-click an <span>Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer </span>shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram pane. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562871 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05108: Creating StV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562871`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562871/Creating+StV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:24.942+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.

To create a StV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.</p><p>To create a StV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562876 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05109: Creating StV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562876`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562876/Creating+StV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:25.067+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.

To create a StV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property 
*environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.</p><p>To create a StV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property<br /><em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562886 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05110: Creating StV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249562886`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562886/Creating+StV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:25.362+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.

To create a StV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567035 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05111: Creating SubChoreography

- page_id: `249567035`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567035/Creating+SubChoreography
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T13:16:43.541+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Using Cameo Business Modeler Plugin > Using BPMN Choreography Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SubChoreography can be displayed with different symbol properties:

- An Expanded SubChoreography can contain inner shapes.
- A Collapsed SubChoreography cannot display inner shapes. A plus sign (+) marker is displayed for a collapsed subprocess.

To create SubChoreography

- On the Choreography diagram pallet, click SubChoreography and select a needed SubChoreography.

To expand a SubChoreography, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu select Suppress Content .
- Right-click a SubChoreography and select Symbol(s) Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens. Select or clear the Suppress Content and click OK .

To collapse a SubChoreography, do one of the following

- Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu clear the Suppress Content .
- Right-click a SubChoreography and select Symbol(s) Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens. Clear the Suppress Content and click OK .

**Related element**

- SubChoreography

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- Using Choreography Activity
- Creating and Using Choreography Task
- Creating Call Choreography Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
