A plus sign (+) marker is displayed for a collapsed subprocess.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create SubChoreography</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="00b35a82-e9c5-454f-b64e-6d8631c42f54">On the Choreography diagram pallet, click <strong>SubChoreography</strong> and select a needed SubChoreography.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To expand a SubChoreography, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="dfb88d6b-4c4e-4f12-98fc-4a87d56d681d">Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu select <strong>Suppress Content</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="b0f88e98-7443-4929-9262-0a262cf18a45">Right-click a SubChoreography and select <strong>Symbol(s) Properties</strong>. The <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens. Select or clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong> and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To collapse a SubChoreography, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="aefd04fb-d156-45c2-8545-b14acd0e33e7">Right-click a SubChoreography and on the shortcut menu clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="315a1b5c-25c0-40b3-b23f-10e9b33baec8">Right-click a SubChoreography and select <strong>Symbol(s) Properties</strong>. The<strong> Symbol Properties</strong> dialog opens. Clear the <strong>Suppress Content</strong> and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="1b22c6de-55b8-420f-a91f-1107319514d7"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/SubChoreography">SubChoreography</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9d08be4c-d038-41b2-a69b-07948812d3e8"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="82446b0c-d77d-470a-a311-509c259966aa"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e003abde-988b-4b8a-902a-a1d5eeb8e3b4"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Using+Choreography+Activity">Using Choreography Activity</a></li><li data-uuid="1db2a921-6159-46cd-a5d8-304921509aea"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+and+Using+Choreography+Task">Creating and Using Choreography Task</a></li><li data-uuid="ff473470-3b7e-4cfa-a889-571260f6858e"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+Call+Choreography+Activity">Creating Call Choreography Activity</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562737 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05112: Creating SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562737`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562737/Creating+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:41.051+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources.</li><li>Connect resources using Association.</li><li>Create Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>flowing via<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Associations.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services.</span> You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562745 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05113: Creating SV-1 Internal diagram

- page_id: `249562745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562745/Creating+SV-1+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:41.504+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space='']OV-2 Internal diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space=''] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> Internal diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p> You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562825 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05114: Creating SV-10a table

- page_id: `249562825`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562825/Creating+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:54.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-10a Systems Rules Model
- labels: ['add-resource-constraint-in-sv-10a', 'create-sv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the **Select Subject Of Resource Constraint** dialog choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange.
- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog choose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource Artifact,</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange</span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562751 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05115: Creating SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562751`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562751/Creating+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:41.673+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create resources or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] .
2. Connect resources using Associations.
3. Create Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports. The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating SV-2.</p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources or reuse them from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect resources using Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li><p>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d06663-14f6-438b-8ed6-a66214266957"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link></span></span> diagram.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562759 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05116: Creating SV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `249562759`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562759/Creating+SV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:41.875+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2-internal', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p>You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2 System Internal Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562767 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05117: Creating SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `249562767`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562767/Creating+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:52.194+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-3-matrix', 'build-sv-3-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) shows how systems interact to each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource Exchangesand their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource Exchangebetween resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the **Row Scope** (Resources).
2. Specify the **Column Scope** (Resources).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how systems interact to each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource <span>Exchanges </span>and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p>To create a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563068 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05118: Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249563068`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563068/Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:30.936+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.
5. Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563070 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05119: Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

- page_id: `249563070`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563070/Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:30.991+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562775 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05120: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249562775`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562775/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:42.331+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''].
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562777 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05121: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `249562777`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562777/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:42.409+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-flow-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Create Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b36e701-10bd-41e6-a4db-1527fbf1fbf7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Systems Functionality Description diagram</span> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Create <span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchanges</span> </span> on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562786 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05122: Creating SV-5a matrix

- page_id: `249562786`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562786/Creating+SV-5a+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.081+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5a-matrix', 'build-sv-5a-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5a-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space=''])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562792 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 05123: Creating SV-5b matrix

- page_id: `249562792`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562792/Creating+SV-5b+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.421+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5b-matrix', 'build-sv-5b-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5b-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) describes thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']) mapping between the Systems and Operational Activities.

The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent resourcesand the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5b matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resources Artifacts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) <span>describes the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) m</span><span>apping between the Systems and Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">resources </span>and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5b matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> (Resources Artifacts).</span></li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562799 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05124: Creating SV-6 matrix

- page_id: `249562799`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562799/Creating+SV-6+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:53.781+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-6', 'create-sv-6', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Resource Exchanges, Commands, and Controlsto fill in an SV-6 matrix.

To add new Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges type are added to the matrix.

To add an existing Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchange types are added to the matrix.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges, Commands, and Controls </span>to fill in an SV-6 matrix.</p><p> </p><p>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges type </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>+</strong> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:<br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange types </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562808 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05125: Creating SV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249562808`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562808/Creating+SV-8+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:54.125+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-8 Systems Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a821551c-c5dd-447f-a321-7fe7999c5434"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3 class="confluence-link">Create new Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Version of Configuration.</li><li>Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.</li></ol><h3>Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select <strong>Resource</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Assignment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="version_of_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562818 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05126: Creating SV-9 table

- page_id: `249562818`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562818/Creating+SV-9+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:54.309+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-sv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658334558

1658334569

1658334559

To create a SV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding new subjects of forecast

To add new subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

##### Adding existing subjects of forecast

To add existing subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual **Strategic**Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658334556

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562643 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05127: Creating SvcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `249562643`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562643/Creating+SvcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.282+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-1 Services Context Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] views.

To create a SvcV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Specification.
2. Associate Service Specification usingAssociation relationships.

The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Specifications mapping and Service Specificationsinteractions with the system resources.You can model each Service Specifications internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span> using<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Association relationships</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(51,51,51);" /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s mapping and Service Specifications </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">interactions with the system resources. </span>You can model each <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s</span> internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562716 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05128: Creating SvcV-10a table

- page_id: `249562716`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562716/Creating+SvcV-10a+table

- labels: ['create-svcv-10a', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SvcV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraints. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, DataElement, Resource Exchange).
- Click the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraint from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562649 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05129: Creating SvcV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562649`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562649/Creating+SvcV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''] views.

To create a SvcV-2 diagram

1. Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.
2. Associate Service Specifications using Associations .
3. Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''] view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.

You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram' space=''] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.</li><li>Associate Service Specifications using Associations<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.</span><span> </span></li></ol><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.</p><p>You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Service Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562655 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05131: Creating SvcV-3a matrix

- page_id: `249562655`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562655/Creating+SvcV-3a+matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

SvcV-3a diagram is non editable.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562667 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05133: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `249562667`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562667/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-description', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications.
3. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.
4. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Service Functions.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications<span class="confluence-link">.  </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service  Functions and Operational Activities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562669 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05134: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `249562669`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562669/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:39.141+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-flow-description', 'display-resource-interactions-in-svcv-4', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='']SvcV-4 Service Functionality Descriptiondiagram should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Control Flow or Service Object Flow relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram" /><ac:link-body>SvcV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Service Functionality Description</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> diagram</span> should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service <span>Control Flow or Service <span> </span>Object Flow relationships.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562697 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05135: Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `249562697`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562697/Creating+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:50.058+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-8', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category******Security, Resources,**or **Operational**, and select the desired resource. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource, type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone properties defined:

- Version Released At Milestone (defines the start of a period, Kind - InService) .
- Version Withdrawn At Milestone (defines the end of a period, Kind - OutOfService).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, specify: 
 - the**Version Released At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following: - the **Version Withdrawn At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following:
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind In Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "").
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind Out Of Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "").
2. Click **Close** > **OK**. The Actual Project Milestone is specified.

In_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind In Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the start of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: In Service.
5. Click Close > OK .

Out_Of_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind Out Of Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties:
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the end of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: Out Of Service. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click Close > OK .

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562709 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05136: Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `249562709`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562709/Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:50.402+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658435249

1658435261

1658435250

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strateic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658435247

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005049 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05137: Creating SysML v1 projects

- page_id: `249005049`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249005049/Creating+SysML+v1+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-25T11:09:00.631+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are three ways to create the SysML projects.

##### Creating the blank SysML v1 project

To create a blank SysML v1 project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**> **New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select **SysML****v1 Project**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The blank project is created and stored in your file system with a defined name.

##### Creating a SysML v1 project from a template

To create a SysML v1 project from a template

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Other**, select **Project from Template**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Select the **SysML v1** template.
5. Click **OK** when you are done. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating MagicGrid projects

To create a MagicGrid project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select either:
  1. **MagicGrid QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid approach.
  2. **MagicGrid Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid approach.
  3. **MagicGrid v2 QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid v2 approach.
  4. **MagicGrid v2 Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid v2 approach.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. When youcreatea new MagicGrid project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking **Yes**changes the modeling tool's perspective to the MagicGrid perspective. The MagicGrid project is created and stored in your file system with a defined name.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563023 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05138: Creating the SV-10a table

- page_id: `249563023`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563023/Creating+the+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:29.378+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Create New Resource Constraint button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Resource Constraint button and selecting Resource Constraints .

- You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Resource Constraint</strong> button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing Resource Constraint</strong> button and selecting<strong> Resource Constraints</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7381a5-8071-43e0-aad0-e07e2453bdfc"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 05139: Creating the SV-9 table

- page_id: `249563096`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563096/Creating+the+SV-9+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:01.510+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-9 Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658644894

1658644896

1658644895

To create a SV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968843 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05140: Creating traceability relations

- page_id: `243968843`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968843/Creating+traceability+relations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:45.963+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1963513353

1963513355

1963513354

Multiple relationships can be used for traceability representation, depending on the element type and method used.

The traceability relations can be represented by the following types of element dependencies:

- UML relationships (such as abstraction, realization, derivation)
- UML properties (such as Owned Behavior )
- UML tags (such as Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams )

The traceability relations can be single level and multilevel.

- When elements are related directly by using a custom UML relationship, property, or tag, the traceability relation is considered to be a single level relation.
- When elements are related indirectly by usually using multiple types of the above mentioned dependencies, the traceability relation is considered to be a multilevel relation.

The traceability relations can be specifying, realizing, or other. This depends on which direction a relation is analyzed and which element is considered as a basis.

- A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the realization of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be realizing traceability (for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forward traceability - realization' space='']).
- A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the specification of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be specifying traceability (for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Backward traceability – specification' space='']).
- All [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Other traceability relations' space=''].[IMAGE alt='' src='']Horizontal and vertical traceability representation through the realizing, specifying, and other traceability relations

1963513351

**Related Pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74f40fff-4d5b-44d7-9757-50cf40a550cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1963513353</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c33755c-8a3c-4d60-9714-62d759693f84"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1963513355</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff4e0358-05d0-4a0f-a450-c52238b5167f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1963513354</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>Multiple relationships can be used for traceability representation, depending on the element type and method used. </span></p><p><span>The traceability relations can be represented by the following types of element dependencies: </span></p><ul><li><span>UML relationships (such as abstraction, realization, derivation)<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /> </span></li><li><span>UML properties (such as </span> <strong>Owned Behavior</strong> <span>)</span></li><li><span>UML tags (such as </span> <strong>Alternative Flow of Events Diagrams</strong> <span>)</span></li></ul><p>The traceability relations can be single level and multilevel.</p><ul><li>When elements are related directly by using a custom UML relationship, property, or tag, the traceability relation is considered to be a single level relation.</li><li><span>When elements are related indirectly by usually using multiple types of the above mentioned dependencies, the traceability relation is considered to be a multilevel relation.</span></li></ul><p>The traceability relations can be specifying, realizing, or other. This depends on which direction a relation is analyzed and which element is considered as a basis.</p><ul><li><span>A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the realization of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be </span> <span>realizing traceability </span> <span>(for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Forward traceability - realization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[forward traceability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </span></li><li><span>A relationship between a specific element and an element that is the specification of this specific element, from the point of view of the specific element, is considered to be </span> <span>specifying traceability </span> <span>(for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Backward traceability – specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[backward traceability]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</span></li><li><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>All <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Other traceability relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[other traceability relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="horizontal_vertical_traceability.png" /></ac:image> <br /> </span></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><h6><span>Horizontal and vertical traceability representation through the realizing, specifying, and other traceability <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="870da7b2-6dd0-494a-bd9f-de34dd339884">relations</ac:inline-comment-marker> </span></h6></div></div></div></div></li></ul></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c993e2cb-c343-426d-adc9-ebc5ba970b19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1963513351</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Traceability">Traceability</a><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li class="current"> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+traceability+relations">Creating traceability relations</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Traceability+relations+representation">Traceability relations representation</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Navigating+between+different+levels+of+abstraction">Navigating between different levels of abstraction</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Analyzing+traceability+relations">Analyzing traceability relations</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Predefined+traceability+rules">Predefined traceability rules</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Custom+traceability+rules">Custom traceability rules</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
