<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563104 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05141: Creating TV-1 table

- page_id: `249563104`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563104/Creating+TV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:31.849+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint > MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a TV-1 table, do either

- Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the Add new UPDM Element button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.
- Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the Add Existing UPDM Element button and select the UPDM elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the … button on any cell in the Standard/Policy column.

- You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a TV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the <strong>Add new UPDM Element</strong> button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.</li><li>Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the <strong>Add Existing UPDM</strong> Element button and select the UPDM elements.</li><li>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the<strong> …</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac771b7b-bc23-4d1e-92ac-95be2bc7b64d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563109 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05142: Creating TV-2 table

- page_id: `249563109`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563109/Creating+TV-2+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:02.461+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint > MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658721791

1658721806

1658721795

To create a TV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658721790

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969573 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 05144: Creating validation suites

- page_id: `243969573`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969573/Creating+validation+suites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:05.326+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Additional information

INLINE

The validation suite defines the set of validation rules applied when running the validation. The purpose of the validation suites is to group validation rules (constraints) without duplicating them. When creating a new validation suite you must choose the active or inactive validation suite you want to create, depending on the applied stereotype:

- «validationSuite» is dedicated for inactive validation rules. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space='']>]]>
- «activeValidationSuite» is dedicated for active validation rules. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space='']>]]>

You can:

- 
- 
- 

#### INFO: Sharing

Sharing

You can share validation suites by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space=''] and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']

createTo create a new validation suite

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. Do the one of the following: - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_new_validation_suite.png'] and select Validation Suite or Active Validation Suite command. - Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''] and select Create Validation Suite or Create Active Validation Suite command.
3. In the Select Owner dialog, choose the owner for the new validation suite Package, and click OK .
4. In the **Rename** dialog, type the name of the new validation suite Package, and click **OK**. 
An empty validation suite Package is created and is located in your model. You can include validation rules from other validation suites. >]]> AlternativeAn alternative way is to create a new or select existing Package element and apply one of the following stereotypes:- «validationSuite» 
 - «activeValidationSuite»[How to apply a stereotype >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype)

cloneTo clone existing validation suites

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select the validation suite Package you want to modify.
3. Do the one of the following: 
 - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 - Right-click it and select **Clone Validation Suite** command.
4. In the Select Owner dialog, choose the owner for the cloned validation suite Package, and click OK .
5. In the **Rename** dialog, type the name of the cloned validation suite Package, and click **OK**. 
The clone of the existing validation suite Package is created and contains the same validation rules as the original. You can include or exclude validation rules from the cloned validation suites Package. >]]>

fillTo include/exclude the validation rules to/from a validation suite Package

1. Open the Validation Suites dialog. [How to open the **Validation Suites** dialog >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog)
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select the newly created or cloned validation suite Package. You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Suites dialog' space=''], select/clear the check boxes for the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule to include/exclude validation rules to/from the new validation suites Package. 
The validation suites Package contains only selected validation rules. Additional informationAfter you include/exclude the validation rules (constraints), the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED'] relation is created/deleted in the model. You can check that in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''], under newly created or cloned validation suites Package, **Relations** group. In the image below, the *Numbering Validation*, *Path Overlapping*, and *Pins Displaying* validation suites with all Constraints are included in the newly created *Specific constraints* validation suite via the Package Import relation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aea72ef1-04ad-48d9-95eb-beaffcce90b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The validation suite defines the set of validation rules applied when running the validation. The purpose of the validation suites is to group validation rules (constraints) without duplicating them. When creating a new validation suite you must choose the active or inactive validation suite you want to create, depending on the applied stereotype:</p><ul><li>«validationSuite» is dedicated for inactive validation rules. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined validation suites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about predefined validation suites >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>«activeValidationSuite» is dedicated for active validation rules. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Active validation suites" Suites</strong> dialog. <span class="confluence-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog">How to open the <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Suites area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the validation suite Package you want to modify.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Do the one of the following:<br /> - In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone_validation_suite.png" /></ac:image> .<br /> - Right-click it and select <strong>Clone Validation Suite</strong> command.<br /></span></li><li>In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose the owner for the cloned validation suite Package, and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Rename</strong> dialog, type the name of the cloned validation suite Package, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The clone of the existing validation suite Package is created and contains the same validation rules as the original. You can include or exclude validation rules from the cloned validation suites Package. <ac:link ac:anchor="fill"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to include/exclude validation rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5918508-3a97-41e8-8dca-1fa902945a54"><ac:parameter ac:name="">fill</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To include/exclude the validation rules to/from a validation suite Package</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog. <span class="confluence-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation+Suites+dialog#ValidationSuitesdialog-OpeningtheValidationSuitesdialog">How to open the <strong>Validation Suites</strong> dialog &gt;&gt;</a></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Suites area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the newly created or cloned validation suite Package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64a5481a-f4f8-454c-84ff-686ee2a1f205"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot include/exclude the validation rules to/from read-only validation suites.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Suites dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Rules area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select/clear the check boxes for the entire Package or an appropriate validation rule to include/exclude validation rules to/from the new validation suites Package.<br />The validation suites Package contains only selected validation rules.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bae1785-a055-48d3-9380-6ded5324efe2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After you include/exclude the validation rules (constraints), the appropriate <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> relation is created/deleted in the model. You can check that in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, under newly created or cloned validation suites Package, <strong>Relations</strong> group. In the image below, the <em>Numbering Validation</em>, <em>Path Overlapping</em>, and <em>Pins Displaying</em> validation suites with all Constraints are included in the newly created <em>Specific constraints</em> validation suite via the Package Import relation.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_import_relation_when_reusing_validation_rules.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970724 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05146: CSV Export Report Designer

- page_id: `243970724`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970724/CSV+Export+Report+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:54.369+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this section, you will learn how to export a Diagram Table in CSV format.

To export a Diagram Table in the CSV format using the Magic Report plugin

1. On the main menu, click Options>Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Report Wizard option group, click Add to add a new row in the Configure template mappings table. [ATTACHMENT filename='Environment Options dialog.png']
3. In the Template mappings dialog, add . csv as the file extension and . txt as the Template type. [ATTACHMENT filename='Template mapping dialog.png']
4. Click OK .
5. Create a blank text file and copy the sample code below in the text file. = 0 || \$value.indexOf(',') >= 0)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#else
#foreach ($c in $value.toCharArray())
#if($c.getType($c)==15)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#break
#end
#end
#end
#end
#import("table", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DiagramTableTool")
#foreach($d in $table.filterDiagramTables($Diagram))
#set($colIds = $table.getColumnIds($d))$d.getName()
#foreach($te in $table.getColumnNames($d, $colIds))#formatValue($te)$value,#end
#foreach($re in $table.getRows($d))
#foreach($id in $colIds)#formatValue($table.getStringCellValue($d, $re, $id))$value,#end
#end
$table.clearTablesCache()
#end]]>
6. Save this text file with a CSV file extension.
7. Add the CSV template to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space=''] and generate a CSV report.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In this section, you will learn how to export a Diagram Table in CSV format.</p><p>To export a Diagram Table in the CSV format using the Magic Report plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options&gt;Environment</strong>. The Environment Options dialog opens.</li><li>In the Report Wizard option group, click <strong>Add</strong> to add a new row in the Configure template mappings table.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Environment Options dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the Template mappings dialog, add .<strong>csv </strong>as the file extension and .<strong>txt </strong>as the Template type.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Template mapping dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Create a blank text file and copy the sample code below in the text file.<br /><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7c0570a-351b-45ba-b332-f2e8b9b49af3"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#macro(formatValue $s)
#set($value = $s)
#if($value.indexOf('"') >= 0 || \$value.indexOf(',') >= 0)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#else
#foreach ($c in $value.toCharArray())
#if($c.getType($c)==15)
#set($value = '"' + $s + '"')
#break
#end
#end
#end
#end
#import("table", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DiagramTableTool")
#foreach($d in $table.filterDiagramTables($Diagram))
#set($colIds = $table.getColumnIds($d))$d.getName()
#foreach($te in $table.getColumnNames($d, $colIds))#formatValue($te)$value,#end
#foreach($re in $table.getRows($d))
#foreach($id in $colIds)#formatValue($table.getStringCellValue($d, $re, $id))$value,#end
#end
$table.clearTablesCache()
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save this text file with a CSV file extension.</li><li>Add the CSV template to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard dialog" /></ac:link> and generate a CSV report.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971394 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05147: Custom tool

- page_id: `243971394`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971394/Custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:13.074+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Custom tool is a template library written in Java to encapsulate functions in context. The Custom tool, see figure below, is extended from the report API. The tool can be used to develop custom context properties that are presentation centric or that can take advantage of the existing ‘tool’ extensions or by document authors familiar with Java.

Report Wizard uses the word 'tool' meaning an object encapsulating dynamic functions on the JavaBean. A tool is a "carrier" of data between the Java and template layers. In other words, the tool simplifies template development and maintenance.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Custom tool API Structure.

#### Context Name

A Context name is a name for variables or references in the template. Report Wizard uses the Velocity Template Language (*VTL*) as a standard syntax for the template. *VTL* uses “$” as a leading character followed by an identifier for the variable.

For example:

```text

```

#### Context Object

A Context object is a Java Object representing a variable. A Context object is modeled on the JavaBean specifications defined by Sun Microsystems.

For example, if a String represents the variable $foo.name in context, the value of the String will be printed out in the output report as follows.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A tool is a &quot;carrier&quot; of data between the Java and template layers. In other words, the tool simplifies template development and maintenance.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Custom Tool API Structure." ac:alt="Custom Tool API Structure."><ri:attachment ri:filename="OverviewComponent.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Custom tool API Structure.</h6><h2>Context Name</h2><p>A Context name is a name for variables or references in the template. $foo.name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Context Object</h2><p>A Context object is a Java Object representing a variable. A Context object is modeled on the JavaBean specifications defined by Sun Microsystems.</p><p>For example, if a String represents the variable $foo.name in context, the value of the String will be printed out in the output report as follows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8a80fef-4a5c-4939-baee-47e3876f7d7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Foo Name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968944 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05148: Custom traceability rules

- page_id: `243968944`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968944/Custom+traceability+rules
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-09T15:27:58.417+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create custom traceability rules if predefined traceability rules are not sufficient to trace and analyze relationships between elements in your model. In a model, custom traceability rules are considered as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='UPDG'], because their values are produced or calculated from other properties. Usually, the values of custom traceability rules cannot be changed directly, but you can and these rules the same way as any other property.

##### Creating a custom traceability rule

Custom traceability rules can be quickly created from the Specification window of a particular element. This way you create a new Derived Property for all elements of the selected element type.

The customization of the newly created traceability rule is saved in the *Derived Properties* Package with the «derivedPropertiesSuite» stereotype. This folder is normally hidden in the containment tree of your model. You can find it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick Find dialog' space='']**Quick Find** functionality. This may be useful when you want to change traceability rule properties.

To create a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element for which you want to create a custom traceability rule to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. Click the Create button. [ATTACHMENT filename='1_create_custom_traceability_rule_1.png']
4. In the open Expression dialog, specify the name and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] of a new traceability rule.
5. Click OK and close the Specification window. If you want to create a Derived Property with a result other than an element, you need to change the Derived Property type manually. [ATTACHMENT filename='1_create_custom_traceability_rule_2.png']

To create a custom traceability rule in a table

- In the toolbar of an active table, click the Columns button, and in the menu select the New Derived Property command. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_derived_property_in_a_table.png']

##### Specifying a custom traceability rule

When you create a new custom traceability rule for an element or a Derived Property in a table, you need to define the expression in the **Expression** dialog. Keep in mind:

- The Derived Property type and multiplicity (conveyed via the Single Value check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications in the expression itself. [ATTACHMENT filename='derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png']
- The root union of a Derived Property structured expression has the Unique check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result. By default, the check box is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='unique_ordered_options.png']

#### INFO: Additional information

Additional information

To learn more about specifying expressions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] page.

##### editEditing a custom traceability rule

You can modify a custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element.

#### NOTE: Editing traceability rule values

Editing traceability rule values

You can edit the value of a custom traceability rule directly only when its operation type is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Simple Navigation' space=''] and you specified only one criterion.

To edit a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to edit to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to edit.
4. Click the Edit button. [ATTACHMENT filename='2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_1.png']
5. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .
6. Close the Specification window.

##### editEditing a custom traceability rule in a table

If a custom traceability rule is displayed in a table, you can edit it directly from that table.

To edit a custom traceability rule in a table

1. In a table, right-click the header of the desired custom traceability rule column and select Edit Derived Property . [ATTACHMENT filename='2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png']
2. In the Expression dialog, make desired changes and click OK .

##### displayDisplaying a custom traceability rule

If you want to visualize custom traceability rules, you can display them in the compartments of element shapes or in tables.

To display a custom traceability rule on an element shape

1. Do one of the following:
  - In a diagram, right-click the desired element shape and select Edit Compartments .
  - In a diagram, select the desired element shape and click the **Compartments** smart manipulator, click on the down arrow to expand the menu, and select **Edit Compartments**.
2. In the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the **Element Properties** tab.
3. In the Hidden list on the left side of the dialog, select the traceability rules you want to display.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_to_selected.png'] to add the selected traceability rules to the Selected list on the right side of the dialog. If you want to display all properties from the Hidden list, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_all.png'] .
5. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_element_shape.png']

To display a custom traceability rule in a table

1. In the toolbar of an active table, click the Show Columns button and from the menu select the Select Columns command. [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_1.png']
2. In the Select Columns dialog, set the traceability rules you want to display as true .
3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_2.png']

##### Removing a custom traceability rule

You can remove a Custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element or directly from a table if the traceability rule is displayed in it.

To remove a custom traceability rule

1. In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to remove to open its Specification window.
2. In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the Traceability group.
3. In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to remove.
4. Click the Delete button and close the Specification window. [ATTACHMENT filename='4_remove_custom_traceability_rule.png']

To remove a custom traceability rule in a table

- In a table, right-click the header of the unwanted custom traceability rule column and select Remove Derived Property . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_remove_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png']

**Related pages**

- Traceability
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can create custom traceability rules if predefined traceability rules are not sufficient to trace and analyze relationships between elements in your model. In a model, custom traceability rules are considered as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDG" ri:content-title="Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derived Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, because their values are produced or calculated from other properties. Usually, the values of custom traceability rules cannot be changed directly, but you can <ac:link ac:anchor="edit" /> and <ac:link ac:anchor="display" /> these rules the same way as any other property.</p><h3>Creating a custom traceability rule</h3><p>Custom traceability rules can be quickly created from the Specification window of a particular element. This way you create a new Derived Property for all elements of the selected element type.</p><p>The customization of the newly created traceability rule is saved in the <em>Derived Properties</em> Package with the «derivedPropertiesSuite» stereotype. This folder is normally hidden in the containment tree of your model. You can find it using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Quick Find dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Quick Find</strong> functionality</ac:link-body></ac:link>. This may be useful when you want to change traceability rule properties.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom traceability rule</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2a2a008d-7796-49af-8534-c53ff86a861a">In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element for which you want to create a custom traceability rule to open its Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="3e48b1b0-9cf5-4c3e-a2b0-2ebe4eeb8d47">In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Traceability</strong> group.</li><li data-uuid="22c899e6-9382-40db-822c-f9e0ba9e8c71">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_custom_traceability_rule_1.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="914d1530-b42d-454c-b21c-771015b16ddd">In the open <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, specify the name and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a new traceability rule.</li><li data-uuid="59533d51-d0cc-4c47-a25d-d7e89860e01f">Click <strong>OK</strong> and close the Specification window. If you want to create a Derived Property with a result other than an element, you need to change the Derived Property type manually.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_custom_traceability_rule_2.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a custom traceability rule in a table</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="5d695d76-0912-483e-9b15-479979774bfd">In the toolbar of an active table, click the <strong>Columns</strong> button, and in the menu select the <strong>New Derived Property</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_derived_property_in_a_table.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Specifying a custom traceability rule</h3><p>When you create a new custom traceability rule for an element or a Derived Property in a table, you need to define the expression in the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog. Keep in mind:</p><ul><li data-uuid="422a86b0-be57-4e47-8510-45ea87d25db1">The Derived Property <strong>type</strong> and <strong>multiplicity</strong> (conveyed via the <strong>Single Value</strong> check box) are set automatically according to the specified expression once it is created or edited. You can also change them manually, however, they will only be applied if you make no modifications in the expression itself.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="derived_property_type_and_multiplicity_img.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="ea6a401e-7bbf-425f-8682-168530b84a3b">The root union of a Derived Property structured expression has the <strong>Unique</strong> check box which you can select to have only one identical element count as the result. By default, the check box is selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="unique_ordered_options.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62275b61-1db5-4461-b210-61eb3e15d88c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about specifying expressions, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93bf5c32-88d1-4c06-8c19-7f19c09e08af"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing a custom traceability rule</h3><p>You can modify a custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b289255-dbc9-4181-bf70-f88863e32b1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing traceability rule values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can edit the value of a custom traceability rule directly only when its operation type is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simple Navigation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and you specified only one criterion.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To edit a custom traceability rule</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="41fc11d6-d79a-42bb-ad3f-29a7fc87d3c3">In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to edit to open its Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="46a7656c-58f4-4126-88d5-8f6f910fd92e">In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Traceability</strong> group.</li><li data-uuid="263732c1-306a-4758-a41a-7c02870e5041">In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to edit.</li><li data-uuid="a91cfd16-cb3f-45d9-bac7-2496cfd2e26f">Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_1.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="2aa8ba9a-e625-43f5-ba8d-7f8ff5031aec">In the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, make desired changes and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="3318a6bf-208a-4b9c-8b92-eb2e8524c233">Close the Specification window.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="606f87d0-f0a8-4715-a9b8-5a0beea6b569"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing a custom traceability rule in a table</h3><p><span>If a custom traceability rule is displayed in a table, you can edit it directly from that table.<br /><br /></span></p><p>To edit a custom traceability rule in a table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f906158d-4610-4094-abda-afaaf59d1fb7">In a table, right-click the header of the desired custom traceability rule column and select <strong>Edit Derived Property</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="2_edit_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d54eaffe-a561-4165-ab47-73610d7f0128">In the <strong>Expression</strong> dialog, make desired changes and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0874203b-e27b-4e7d-ad41-efcd9d0dde9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">display</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying a custom traceability rule</h3><p>If you want to visualize custom traceability rules, you can display them in the compartments of element shapes or in tables.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display a custom traceability rule on an element shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a60b618a-41c5-4a31-a0a8-889f6ed17e84">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In a diagram, right-click the desired element shape and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</li><li><p>In a diagram, select the desired element shape and click the <strong>Compartments</strong> smart manipulator, click on the down arrow to expand the menu, and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</p></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8f651dd7-8eb6-414f-9cdf-31679ecbc3e4"><p>In the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab.</p></li><li data-uuid="a8119271-aa0f-47d1-a535-51cdf73f560f">In the <strong>Hidden</strong> list on the left side of the dialog, select the traceability rules you want to display.</li><li data-uuid="3462262c-7cbb-4b59-abb4-b5bd8df15a2f">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_to_selected.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add the selected traceability rules to the <strong>Selected</strong> list on the right side of the dialog. If you want to display all properties from the <strong>Hidden</strong> list, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="34606403-0f98-4d4a-8107-148a180aa4a2">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_element_shape.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display a custom traceability rule in a table</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="efaac682-d172-414e-ae52-be82e80e7d48">In the toolbar of an active table, click the <strong>Show Columns</strong> button and from the menu select the <strong>Select Columns</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_1.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="aa0817f3-8cf8-4879-8999-ef19dced16ee">In the <strong>Select Columns</strong> dialog, set the traceability rules you want to display as <em>true</em>.</li><li data-uuid="75bab4f0-31e3-436c-88eb-d7de12b335f9">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_display_custom_traceability_rule_on_in_table_2.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3><br />Removing a custom traceability rule</h3><p>You can remove a Custom traceability rule from the Specification window of a particular element or directly from a table if the traceability rule is displayed in it.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a custom traceability rule</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="472b01e5-0020-4886-9f2a-915dd0ca2371">In the model browser or a diagram, double-click the element with the traceability rule you want to remove to open its Specification window.</li><li data-uuid="ee285b09-bd59-440e-a816-c67b4e347e0a">In the property group list on the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Traceability</strong> group.</li><li data-uuid="c8f538b9-fc37-472e-b513-c2d3022edd6d">In the property specification area on the right side of the window, select the traceability rule you want to remove.</li><li data-uuid="ab1b982c-3a0f-446f-8f5a-0dc6cb0c7a48">Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button and close the Specification window.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="4_remove_custom_traceability_rule.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove a custom traceability rule in a table</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="97745f73-66e5-4232-94e2-2a6a60e51b8a">In a table, right-click the header of the unwanted custom traceability rule column and select <strong>Remove Derived Property</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="4_remove_custom_traceability_rule_in_table.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="69a8ef68-5abb-473e-8746-3ce8d78f6171"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Traceability">Traceability</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+traceability+relations">Creating traceability relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Traceability+relations+representation">Traceability relations representation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Navigating+between+different+levels+of+abstraction">Navigating between different levels of abstraction</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Analyzing+traceability+relations">Analyzing traceability relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Predefined+traceability+rules">Predefined traceability rules</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965821 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 05150: Customize Perspectives dialog

- page_id: `243965821`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965821/Customize+Perspectives+dialog
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-12T13:25:02.789+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the Customize Perspectives dialog

- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**and then **Customize**. falsemcse,msa[IMAGE alt='' src=''] falsemsosa[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Clone SelectedPerspective | Copy the selected perspective to a new one. |
| Rename SelectedPerspective | The Enter Perspective Name dialog opens. Change name of the perspective and click OK. You can also rename perspectives by pressing F2. |
| Remove SelectedPerspective | Delete the selected perspective from the list. |
| Import NewPerspective | The Open dialog appears. Select *.umd extension file and click Open to import the perspective into the modeling tool environment. |
| Export SelectedPerspective | The Save dialog opens. Type a name for the created perspective and click Save to store it as *.umd extension file. |
| Full FeaturedQuick StartSoftware ArchitectSystem Analyst | List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration. |
| MagicDraw Area | List of customizable toolbars and command sections. |
| Edit | Click the Edit button to open the Customize Main Menu dialog.Change he commands mode by selecting radio buttons. |
| Description | Learn the short description about each selected area. |
| Reset to Defaults | Reset all changes back to the default configuration. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 05151: Customizing and Selecting Perspective

- page_id: `243965817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965817/Customizing+and+Selecting+Perspective
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:46.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

278424292

278424294

278424293

The Startup dialog appears when you launch a modeling tool (MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, or Cameo Enterprise Architecture) for the first time. Select your work perspective in this dialog.

Due to their growing numbers, many features may be configured for the standard or expert user. The tool better satisfies the needs of different software development process roles by using Perspective.

Use perspectives for:

- Selecting a predefined configuration and features according to your software development process role.
- Finding features faster, because there are fewer.
- Choosing a suitable experience mode with a single click.
- Customizing a set of predefined features and configurations based on user needs.

There are four perspectives:

- **Full Featured**perspective provides all features available in the modeling tool and installed plugins.
- **Quick Start**perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling with an interface that is not overcrowded, making learning quicker. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden yet easily reachable in expert mode.
- **Software Architect**perspective provides features primarily involved in designing and implementing projects. It is a set of roles consisting of Software Architect, Designer, Interface designer, and Database designer. This is the default modeling tool configuration. All functionalities are available for expert mode.
- **System Analyst**perspective provides features primarily dedicated to obtaining requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Configuration is modeling-oriented. Code engineering, transformations, and other features are hidden.

To set the perspective for the modeling tool environment

- Launch the program for the first time. The **Startup**dialog opens where you canswitch between perspectives. Select the desired perspective from the list and click **OK**.
- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**> **Perspectives**. The **Select****Perspective**dialog opens. Select the desired perspective and click the **Apply**button.
- On the **Perspectives**toolbar, select the desired perspective from the list.

278424290

**Related pages**

true
